### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ ‚Äî bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math ÎàÑÏ∂ú.**
  delimiter ÏóÜÎäî fallback Ïù¥ Í∏∞Ï°¥ÏóêÎäî `P_{t-1}` Í∞ôÏùÄ braced script ÏôÄ
  allow-list macro Îßå Ïû°ÏïÑ `y^ŒîT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  `‚àöx` Í∞ôÏùÄ LLM Ï∂úÎÝ•Ïù¥ raw Î°ú ÎÇ®ÏïòÏäµÎãàÎã§. `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` Î•º
  math-shaped line context + index-like bare script Î°ú ÌôïÏû•ÌïòÍ≥Ý, `‚àö` /
  Greek / comparison / arrow Îì± Unicode math glyph token ÏùÑ inline math
  segment Î°ú ÏäπÍ≤©Ìï©ÎãàÎã§. Markdown inline/fenced code, `snake_case`,
  slash paths, `**bold**`, `*x*` Îäî Í≥ÑÏÜç text Î°ú ÏúÝÏßÄÎê©ÎãàÎã§.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚Äî bare subscript/superscript + Unicode math leaks.**
  The delimiter-less fallback now catches math-shaped bare scripts and
  Unicode math glyph tokens such as `y^ŒîT_t,n`, `S^(i)_t,n`, `X_t-9:t,n,:`,
  and `‚àöx`. The wider detector is guarded by code-span/path/snake-case/
  Markdown-emphasis skips so ordinary prose and code remain untouched.
- **CLI prompt CJK ÏûÖÎÝ• redraw lag.** prompt_toolkit thin-CLI ÏûÖÎÝ•ÏóêÏÑú
  ÌïúÍ∏Ä Í∞ôÏùÄ wide character Î•º ÌÉÄÏù¥ÌïëÌïÝ Îïå ÏßÅÏÝÑ Í∏ÄÏûêÍ∞Ä Îã§Ïùå keystroke ÏÝÑÍπåÏßÄ
  ÌôîÎ©¥Ïóê ÎÇòÌÉÄÎÇòÏßÄ ÏïäÎäî ghost ÌòÑÏÉÅÏùÑ ÏàòÏÝïÌñàÏäµÎãàÎã§. `<any>` printable
  input binding Ïù¥ `event.data` Î•º ÏÝïÏÉÅ `insert_text()` Í≤ΩÎ°úÎ°ú ÎÑ£ÏùÄ Îí§
  `event.app.invalidate()` Î•º Ìò∏Ï∂úÌï¥ ÏÇΩÏûÖ ÏßÅÌõÑ renderer repaint Î•º
  ÏòàÏïΩÌï©ÎãàÎã§. Enter / Escape+Enter / Backspace / Delete Í∞ôÏùÄ Í∏∞Ï°¥
  binding ÏùÄ ÏúÝÏßÄÎêòÎ©∞, wildcard handler Îäî ÎπÑÏñ¥ ÏûàÍ±∞ÎÇò non-printable Ïù∏
  key data Î•º ÏÇΩÏûÖÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÏäµÎãàÎã§.
- **CLI prompt CJK insertion redraw lag.** Fixes the thin-CLI
  prompt_toolkit prompt where newly typed wide characters such as Korean
  Hangul could stay visually hidden until the next keystroke. A printable
  `<any>` input binding now forwards `event.data` through the normal
  `insert_text()` path, then calls `event.app.invalidate()` so the renderer
  repaints immediately after insertion. Existing Enter, Escape+Enter,
  Backspace, and Delete bindings are preserved, and the wildcard handler
  ignores empty or non-printable key data.
- **CLI streaming Markdown cleanup.** Thin CLI raw `stream` output now tracks
  plain daemon-console spans that look like assistant Markdown and clears that
  transient region at turn stop, before the final `result.text` payload is
  rendered through the existing Markdown + LaTeX renderer. ANSI/Rich stream
  output and structured agentic events continue to render in place.
- **CLI Ïä§Ìä∏Î¶¨Î∞ç Markdown ÏÝïÎ¶¨.** thin CLI Í∞Ä daemon-console Ïùò plain
  `stream` Ï§ë assistant Markdown ÏúºÎ°ú Î≥¥Ïù¥Îäî Íµ¨Í∞ÑÏùÑ Ï∂îÏÝÅÌïòÍ≥Ý, turn Ï¢ÖÎ£å Ïãú
  ÏµúÏ¢Ö `result.text` Î•º Í∏∞Ï°¥ Markdown + LaTeX renderer Î°ú Îã§Ïãú Í∑∏Î¶¨Í∏∞ ÏÝÑÏóê
  Ìï¥Îãπ ÏûÑÏãú raw Íµ¨Í∞ÑÏùÑ ÏßÄÏõÅÎãàÎã§. ANSI/Rich stream Ï∂úÎÝ•Í≥º structured
  agentic event ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅÏùÄ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÏúÝÏßÄÎê©ÎãàÎã§.
- **CLI LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ ‚Äî delimiter-less Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°ú ÎàÑÏ∂ú heuristic.** PR
  #1165/#1169 Ïùò wiring Ïù¥ `\(...\)` / `$...$` / `\[...\]` Í∞ôÏùÄ Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ
  delimiter Í∞Ä ÏûàÎäî Í≤ΩÏö∞Îßå cover ÌïòÏó¨ LLM Ïù¥ delimiter ÏóÜÏù¥ prose ÏïàÏóê
  Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°úÎ•º emit ÌïòÎäî Í≤ΩÏö∞ (ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê 2026-05-16 Î≥¥Í≥Ý: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1})
  / P_{t-1}` raw ÎÖ∏Ï∂ú) ÌöåÍ∑Ä.
  - `core/ui/latex.py` Ïóê `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚Äî Îëê Ï¢ÅÏùÄ
    ÌòïÏãùÎßå catch: (1) **braced subscript/superscript token** (`r_{t-1}`,
    `P_{t+5}`, `x^{2}`, `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ‚Äî `{‚Ä¶}` Í∞Ä ÏßÅÏÝë Îî∞ÎùºÏïº ÌïòÎØÄÎ°ú
    `snake_case`/`file_name`/`r_t` Í∞ôÏùÄ ÏùºÎ∞ò underscore identifier Îäî
    ÏÝàÎåÄ Îß§Ïπò X. (2) **allow-list Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°ú** (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
    `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`‚Äì`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty` Îì±) +
    word boundary `(?![A-Za-z])` ‚Äî `\alphanumeric` Í∞ôÏùÄ prefix collision
    ÌöåÌîº. Ïö∞ÏÑÝÏàúÏúÑÎäî Î™®ÎìÝ delimited match Ïù¥ÌõÑ (ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ fallback).
  - 7 ÏãÝÍ∑ú test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`): ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê Î≥¥Í≥Ý case +
    braced sub/sup multi-token + snake_case/path false-positive ÌöåÌîº +
    macro allow-list + `\alphanumeric` boundary + braced superscript.
  - ÌïúÍ≥Ñ: `r_t` (braces ÏóÜÎäî Îã®Ïùº character subscript) Îäî ÏùòÎèÑÏÝÅ ÎπÑÎß§Ïπò
    ‚Äî Markdown emphasis `_text_` ÏôÄ Ï∂©Îèå ÌöåÌîº + ÏùºÎ∞ò Î≥ÄÏàòÎ™Ö false
    positive Ï∞®Îã® Ïö∞ÏÑÝ. LLM Ïù¥ Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ `r_{t}` ÌòïÏãùÏùÑ Ïì∞Í±∞ÎÇò
    `\(...\)` ÏúºÎ°ú wrap Ìï¥Ïïº ÏÝïÌôï Î≥ÄÌôò.
  - follow-up verifier Î≥¥Í∞ï: delimiter-less allow-list Ïóê `\mathbb`,
    `\mathcal`, `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`,
    `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`, ÎπÑÍµê/ÏßëÌï©/ÎÖºÎ¶¨/ÌôîÏÇ¥Ìëú Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°úÎ•º Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÍ≥Ý,
    `\dfrac`/`\tfrac` Îäî Tier 1 ÏóêÏÑú `\frac` Ï≤òÎüº `a/b` Î°ú ÎÝåÎçîÎêòÎèÑÎ°ù
    ÏÝïÍ∑úÌôî.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚Äî delimiter-less macro leak heuristic.** PRs
  #1165/#1169 wired the renderer for explicit delimiters (`\(...\)` /
  `$...$` / `\[...\]`) but LLM responses that emit LaTeX *without*
  delimiters (the user's 2026-05-16 report: `r_t = (P_t - P_{t-1}) /
  P_{t-1}` showing as raw macros) still leaked. The new
  `_DELIMITERLESS_MATH` regex catches two narrow forms: (1) braced
  subscript/superscript tokens (`r_{t-1}`, `P_{t+5}^{2}`,
  `W_{i,j}^{T}`) ‚Äî the `{‚Ä¶}` requirement keeps `snake_case`, file
  paths, and bare-letter subscripts like `r_t` immune, and (2) an
  allow-list of backslash macros (`\frac`, `\sum`, `\sqrt`,
  `\bar`, `\hat`, `\alpha`‚Äì`\omega`, `\cdot`, `\infty`, ‚Ä¶) with a
  word-boundary guard so `\alphanumeric` is not misread as `\alpha`.
  The heuristic fires after every delimited pattern, so explicit
  `\(‚Ä¶\)` math still takes precedence. Seven new tests in
  `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` pin the user-reported case, false-
  positive immunity for `snake_case` / paths, the macro allow-list,
  the word boundary, and braced superscripts. Known limit: bare-letter
  subscripts like `r_t` stay literal ‚Äî adding them would conflict with
  Markdown's `_text_` emphasis and create false positives across
  ordinary prose; the LLM must use `r_{t}` or wrap in `\(...\)`.
  Follow-up verifier hardening expands the delimiter-less allow-list for
  frequent LLM set / logic / prose math macros (`\mathbb`, `\mathcal`,
  `\mathrm`, `\text`, `\overline`, `\underline`, `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`,
  comparisons, arrows, and quantifiers) and normalizes `\dfrac` /
  `\tfrac` through the Tier 1 `\frac` path so they render as `a/b`
  instead of collapsing to adjacent numerator / denominator text.

- **CLI LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ ‚Äî multi-line source Ïùò vertical Ï§ÑÍ∏ãÍ∏∞ ÌöåÍ∑Ä.**
  PR #1141/#1165 Ïùò wiring Ïù¥ÌõÑ LLM Ïù¥ `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` Í∞ôÏùÄ
  Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°úÎ•º multi-line LaTeX source Î°ú emit ÌïòÎ©¥ (`\frac{<newline>num
  <newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`), pylatexenc Í∞Ä source line break
  Î•º Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú Î≥¥Ï°¥ÌïòÏó¨ ÌÑ∞ÎØ∏ÎÑêÏóêÏÑú Î™®ÎìÝ ÌÜÝÌÅ∞Ïù¥ Ìïú Ï§ÑÏî© vertical Î°ú ÎäòÏñ¥Ï°åÏùå
  (ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê Î≥¥Í≥Ý 2026-05-16: IC_t / = / ‚àë_i=1^N / ( / S_t,i - SÃÑ_t,: / )
  ... 16+ Ï§Ñ).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` Ïù¥ explicit LaTeX row break (`\\`)
    Î•º Î≥¥Ï°¥ÌïòÎ©¥ÏÑú rendered line ÎÇ¥Î∂ÄÏùò whitespace run ÏùÑ single space Î°ú
    collapse. LaTeX source line break Îäî mathematical ÏùòÎØ∏Í∞Ä ÏóÜÏúºÎØÄÎ°ú
    inline + block fallback Ïùò vertical stack ÏùÑ ÎßâÎêò, `cases`/`aligned`
    Ïä§ÌÉÄÏùºÏùò ÏùòÎèÑÏÝÅ Ìñâ Íµ¨Î∂ÑÏùÄ ÏúÝÏßÄ. Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) Îäî Î¨¥Í¥Ä.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` Ïùò `[^\n]+?` ‚Üí `[\s\S]+?` ‚Äî
    multi-line Î≥∏Î¨∏Ïùò `\(...\)` ÎèÑ Ïù∏ÏãùÌïòÎèÑÎ°ù. Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî inline regex Í∞Ä
    Îß§Ïπò Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú Î≥∏Î¨∏Ïù¥ raw ÌÖçÏä§Ìä∏Î°ú ÌùòÎü¨ `\frac`/`\sum` Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°úÍ∞Ä Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú
    ÎÖ∏Ï∂úÎêêÏùå.
  - 3 ÏãÝÍ∑ú ÌöåÍ∑Ä test (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` Ïùò
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline` +
    `_block`, `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) ‚Äî IC_t
    Pearson ÏÉÅÍ¥ÄÍ≥ÑÏàò ÏãùÏùò 7-line LaTeX source Í∞Ä inline (`\(...\)`) /
    block (`\[...\]`) Îëê ÌòïÏãùÏóêÏÑú Î™®Îëê single-paragraph Î°ú ÌùêÎ¶Ñ + raw
    Îß§ÌÅ¨Î°ú leak 0 + math ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ (‚àë, ‚àö) Ï∂úÌòÑ + Ï∂úÎÝ• line Ïàò cap. Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎ°ú
    `cases` Ïùò explicit row break Î≥¥Ï°¥ÏùÑ pin. pre-fix Ïùò 16+
    vertical-stack regression Ï∞®Îã®.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚Äî vertical-stack regression from multi-line
  source.** After PR #1141/#1165 wired the renderer, an LLM emitting
  `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` with source-level line breaks
  (`\frac{<newline>num<newline>}{<newline>denom<newline>}`) caused
  pylatexenc to preserve every newline verbatim, which a narrow terminal
  printed as a vertical stack of single tokens (`IC_t` / `=` / `‚àë_i=1^N`
  / `(` / `S_t,i - SÃÑ_t,:` / `)` / ‚Ä¶, 16+ lines).
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_render_tier1` now preserves explicit LaTeX row
    breaks (`\\`) while collapsing whitespace runs inside each rendered
    line to a single space. LaTeX source line breaks have no mathematical
    meaning ‚Äî flattening preserves the math while restoring inline flow,
    without erasing intentional `cases`/`aligned` rows. Affects inline
    and block Tier 1 fallback; Tier 2 (SymPy pretty) is untouched.
  - `core/ui/latex.py:_INLINE_PAREN` widens `[^\n]+?` to `[\s\S]+?` so
    multi-line `\(‚Ä¶\)` segments are recognised. Pre-fix, the inline
    regex silently failed on a multi-line body and the raw `\frac` /
    `\sum` / `\bar` macros leaked through as plain prose.
  - 3 new regression tests (`tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`,
    `test_multiline_latex_source_collapses_to_single_line_inline`,
    `_block`, and `test_tier1_preserves_explicit_latex_row_breaks`) drive
    a 7-line IC_t Pearson-correlation formula through both `\(‚Ä¶\)` and
    `\[‚Ä¶\]` modes and assert: (a) math symbols (`‚àë`, `‚àö`) reach the
    output, (b) no raw `\`-macros leak, (c) the math block stays within a
    sane line-count cap. The third test pins explicit `cases` row breaks,
    blocking both the pre-fix 16-line regression and over-collapse.

### Infrastructure

- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering ‚Äî Stage A/B/C Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.**
  PR #1165 Ïùò `_render_text_with_latex` wiring Ïù¥ Ìñ•ÌõÑ refactor Î°ú
  silently ÌöåÍ∑ÄÌïòÏßÄ Î™ªÌïòÍ≤å ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê Í∞ÄÏãú ÎèôÏûëÏóê anchor ÌïòÎäî 3-stage ÌöåÍ∑Ä
  Î≥¥Ìò∏ ÏäàÌä∏. `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py` 21 ÏãÝÍ∑ú.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** ‚Äî `Rich.Console(file=StringIO,
    force_terminal=False, theme=GEODE_THEME, color_system=None)` Î°ú Ïã§ÏÝú
    ÎÝåÎçî Í≤∞Í≥ºÎ•º buffer Ïóê capture ÌõÑ plain-text substring Í≤ÄÏ¶ù. Ìå®ÌÑ¥:
    pure prose (no math) / `\[...\]` block / `\(...\)` inline / `$x$`
    inline / `$3.00` Í∞ÄÍ≤© false positive ÌöåÌîº / `\begin{equation}` env /
    mixed dollar+bracket / segment ordering. raw delimiter ÏûîÏû¨ 0 ÌôïÏù∏.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrize)** ‚Äî
    `\frac` / `\sum` / `\sqrt` / `\lim` / `\int` Í∞ÅÍ∞ÅÏóê ÎåÄÌï¥ SymPy
    `pretty()` Ï∂úÎÝ•Ïùò **structural** ÏÜçÏÑ±Îßå Í≤ÄÏ¶ù (substring group Ï§ë
    ÌïòÎÇò + ÏµúÏÜå line count). SymPy upgrade Ïãú fraction-bar Ïùò `‚îÄ` ‚Üî `-`
    Í∞ôÏùÄ cosmetic shift Î¨¥Í¥Ä. brittleness 0.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 test)** ‚Äî `_render_ipc_response`
    Î•º hand-crafted IPC dict Î°ú ÏßÅÏÝë Ìò∏Ï∂ú. result + bracket math /
    pure markdown fallback / error / streamed=True Ïùò tool ÎØ∏Ï§ëÎ≥µ /
    streamed=False Ïùò fallback summary / 4 lifecycle ack Îì§Ïù¥ silent
    drop. serve‚Üíthin-CLI Ïùò ÏÝÑÏ≤¥ print path cover.
  - Spinner thread leak ÌöåÌîº (PR #1165 follow-up Ïùò lesson): Î™®ÎìÝ
    test Í∞Ä `force_terminal=False` non-TTY console ÏÇ¨Ïö©, Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ
    `EventRenderer.start_activity()` Ìò∏Ï∂ú 0. Îã§Î•∏ test Ïùò
    `@patch("...time.sleep")` Ïóê `time.sleep(0.08)` ÎàÑÏÝÅ Ïïà Îê®.
  - Theme guard test: math Í∞Ä `style="value"` Ìò∏Ï∂úÌïòÎØÄÎ°ú `GEODE_THEME`
    Ïóê Í∑∏ style Ï°¥Ïû¨ verify ‚Äî PR #1165 Ïùò CRITICAL fix (`style="math"`
    ÎØ∏ÏÝïÏùò crash) ÌöåÍ∑Ä Ï∞®Îã®.
- **CLI UI/UX regression tests for LaTeX rendering ‚Äî Stage A/B/C.**
  A three-stage regression suite anchored on the **user-visible CLI
  behaviour** so a future refactor of the rendering stack cannot
  silently regress the wiring that PR #1165 just shipped. 21 new tests
  in `tests/test_cli_latex_uiux.py`.
  - **Stage A (Component capture, 9 cases)** drives
    `_render_text_with_latex` against a real `Rich.Console` writing into
    a `StringIO`, then asserts on plain-text substrings ‚Äî no raw
    delimiters left, expected Unicode characters present, prose
    boundaries preserved. Covers pure prose, `\[‚Ä¶\]`, `\(‚Ä¶\)`, `$x$`,
    the `$3.00` price false-positive guard, `\begin{equation}`,
    mixed segments, and text/math/text segment ordering.
  - **Stage B (Tier 2 structural invariants, 5 parametrised cases)**
    asserts on **structural** properties of SymPy's `pretty()` output
    (substring group membership + minimum line count) for `\frac`,
    `\sum`, `\sqrt`, `\lim`, `\int`. Tolerates SymPy version drift
    (e.g. `‚îÄ` vs `-` for the fraction bar) by accepting a set of
    equivalent glyphs per slot. Zero snapshot brittleness.
  - **Stage C (IPC response path, 6 tests)** invokes
    `_render_ipc_response` with hand-crafted IPC dicts ‚Äî covers
    result + bracket math, math-free Markdown fallback, error
    responses, the streamed-vs-non-streamed tool fallback divergence,
    and silent drop of four lifecycle acks. Exercises the full
    `serve ‚Üí thin CLI` print path without an LLM in the loop.
  - Spinner thread leak avoidance (lesson from PR #1165's follow-up
    `time.sleep(0.08)` flake): every test uses a non-TTY console; no
    test starts `EventRenderer.start_activity()` or any other daemon
    animation, so a sibling test's `@patch("...time.sleep")` cannot
    accumulate the 80 ms spinner sleeps in its `mock.call_args_list`.
  - Theme guard test: math segments call `console.print(...,
    style="value")`. The test asserts that style is registered on
    `GEODE_THEME` so PR #1165's CRITICAL fix (Rich `MissingStyle`
    crash when `style="math"` was used) cannot regress.

### Fixed

- **CLI LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ ‚Äî `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}‚Ä¶\end{env}` delimiter Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.** PR #1141 Ïù¥ `core/ui/latex.py`
  Ïùò Tier 1 (pylatexenc) + Tier 2 (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) ÎùºÏù¥Î∏å
  Îü¨Î¶¨ + 19 test Îßå Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÍ≥Ý "Îã§Ïùå Îã®Í≥Ñ ÌõÑÎ≥¥ ‚Äî event_renderer Í∞Ä LLM ÏùëÎãµ
  ÌÖçÏä§Ìä∏Ïóê extract_and_render_inline ÏÝÅÏö©" ÏúºÎ°ú wiring ÏùÑ follow-up ÏúºÎ°ú
  ÎÇ®Í≤®ÎëêÏóàÏùå. Í≤∞Í≥ºÏÝÅÏúºÎ°ú ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏûêÎäî LLM ÏùëÎãµÏóêÏÑú `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}
  ^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` Í∞ôÏùÄ raw LaTeX Î•º Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú Î≥¥Í≥Ý ÏûàÏóàÎã§. Î≥∏ PR Ïù¥
  Îëê Í∞≠ÏùÑ Îã´Ïùå:
  - `core/cli/interactive_loop.py` Ïùò `_render_ipc_response` Í∞Ä LLM final
    text Î•º `rich.markdown.Markdown` ÏúºÎ°ú ÏßÅÏÝë ÌùòÎ¶¨Îçò Î∂ÄÎ∂ÑÏùÑ ÏãÝÍ∑ú
    `_render_text_with_latex` Ìó¨ÌçºÎ°ú ÍµêÏ≤¥. Ìó¨ÌçºÎäî
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)` Î°ú segment Î∂ÑÌïÝ ÌõÑ inline math Îäî
    rendered Unicode Î°ú Ï£ºÎ≥Ä Markdown paragraph Ïóê Îã§Ïãú Ìï©ÏπòÍ≥Ý,
    `block_math` Îäî multi-line block ÏúºÎ°ú render. math Í∞Ä ÏÝÑÌòÄ ÏóÜÏúºÎ©¥
    Îã®Ïùº Markdown Ìò∏Ï∂úÎ°ú fallback (ÌöåÍ∑Ä ÏúÑÌóò 0).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` Ïùò delimiter Í∞Ä `$...$` / `$$...$$` Îëê Í∞ÄÏßÄ ÎøêÏù¥Îùº
    LLM Ïù¥ ÏûêÏ£º Ï∂úÎÝ•ÌïòÎäî `\[...\]` (display) / `\(...\)` (inline) /
    `\begin{equation|align|gather|multline|displaymath}‚Ä¶\end{...}` Í∞Ä
    Î™®Îëê ÎàÑÎùΩ. Î≥∏ PR Ïù¥ ÏÑ∏ Ìå®ÌÑ¥ Î™®Îëê ÏßÄÏõêÌïòÎèÑÎ°ù regex ÌôïÏû• + overlap-
    aware Ïö∞ÏÑÝÏàúÏúÑ resolution (block > inline) Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.
  - ÏãÝÍ∑ú test 13 (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` 7 +
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py` 6) ‚Äî Î™®ÎìÝ delimiter form,
    mixed segments, overlap ÌöåÌîº, raw Î∞±Ïä¨ÎûòÏãú leak ÌöåÍ∑Ä, ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏûêÍ∞Ä Î≥¥Í≥ÝÌïú
    `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m} \ell(\alpha_i) \]` ÏºÄÏù¥Ïä§ ÏßÅÏÝë Í≤ÄÏ¶ù.
  - ÏùòÎèÑÎêú ÎπÑÏßÄÏõê: backslash ÏóÜÎäî `[...]` / `(...)` ‚Äî markdown link
    Î¨∏Î≤ïÍ≥º Ï∂©Îèå + ÏùºÎ∞ò bracket Ïñ¥Ìúò noise. ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏûêÎäî `\[...\]` ÌòïÏãùÏùÑ Ïç®Ïïº
    Ìï®.
- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚Äî `interactive_loop` wiring + `\[...\]`/`\(...\)`/
  `\begin{env}‚Ä¶\end{env}` delimiter support.** PR #1141 introduced
  `core/ui/latex.py` with the Tier 1 (pylatexenc) and Tier 2
  (latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty) renderers plus 19 tests, but the
  CHANGELOG flagged the actual wiring as a follow-up ‚Äî the response
  print path stayed on `rich.markdown.Markdown(text)`. Users therefore
  saw the raw backslash form (e.g. `\[ \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^{m}
  \ell(\alpha_i) \]`) in their terminals. This PR closes both gaps:
  - The LLM final-text branch of
    `core/cli/interactive_loop._render_ipc_response` now calls a new
    `_render_text_with_latex` helper. The helper splits the body via
    `extract_and_render_inline(text)`, folds inline math back into the
    surrounding Markdown paragraph as rendered Unicode, and renders
    `block_math` as a multi-line block. When the body has no math at
    all, it falls back to the single Markdown call (zero regression
    risk).
  - `core/ui/latex.py` only knew `$...$` and `$$...$$`. The new regex
    set adds the three forms LLMs actually emit ‚Äî `\[‚Ä¶\]` for
    display, `\(‚Ä¶\)` for inline, and `\begin{equation|align|gather|
    multline|displaymath}‚Ä¶\end{...}` ‚Äî with overlap-aware priority
    resolution (block > inline) so an inline match inside a
    multi-line bracket block is not double-extracted.
  - 13 new tests
    (`tests/test_ui_latex.py::TestDelimiterExpansion` plus
    `tests/test_interactive_loop_latex.py`) pin every delimiter form,
    mixed segments, the overlap rule, the raw-backslash leak
    regression, and the user-reported case verbatim.
  - Deliberately not supported: bracket forms without backslashes
    (`[...]` / `(...)`) ‚Äî those collide with Markdown link syntax and
    ordinary parenthetical prose. Users must write `\[‚Ä¶\]`.

## [0.95.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-16

### Infrastructure

- **`docs-link-audit` skill Îì±Î°ù.** `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161)
  Î•º 1Ï∞® ÎèÑÍµ¨Î°ú ÌïòÎäî workflow skill ÏùÑ `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/
  SKILL.md` Ïóê Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. Î∂ÑÎ•ò 4 Ï¢Ö (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor
  / external) Îß§Ìïë Ìëú, link Ìå®ÌÑ¥ Ï∂îÏ∂ú ÏÝïÍ∑úÏãù 2 Í∞ú, ÌäπÏù¥ Ï≤òÎ¶¨ (`/geode/`
  basepath / build-time copy Ïù∏ÏßÄ / `${...}` unresolved / Ïä§ÌÇ¥ Ïä§ÌÇµ), exit
  code Í∏∞Î∞ò CI guard, ÏûòÎ™ªÎêú link Ïùò 4 ÌùîÌïú ÏõêÏù∏ (chapter ÏÇ≠ÏÝú leftover /
  section Ïù¥ÏÝÑ / slug typo / external rot), CI wiring ÏòµÏÖò 2 Ï¢Ö (pages.yml
  pre-build / ci.yml dispatch) Î™®Îëê ÏÝïÎ¶¨. CLAUDE.md Ïùò Custom Skills Ìëú
  ÏóêÎèÑ Ìä∏Î¶¨Í±∞ ÌÇ§ÏõåÎìú ("broken link", "404", "docs link", "hyperlink",
  "ÎßÅÌÅ¨ ÏÝêÍ≤Ä", "ÎßÅÌÅ¨ Íπ®Ïßê", "audit links", "link checker") Îì±Î°ù. PR
  #1157 (3 broken ÏÝïÏÝï) + PR #1161 (script ÎèÑÏûÖ) Ïùò ÏºÄÏù¥Ïä§ Ïä§ÌÑ∞Îîî Ìè¨Ìï®.
- **`docs-link-audit` skill registered.** Added
  `.claude/skills/docs-link-audit/SKILL.md` as a workflow skill around
  `scripts/check_docs_links.py` (PR #1161). Covers the 4-category map
  (internal /docs / internal /other / anchor / external), the 2
  regexes that drive link extraction, special handling (`/geode/`
  basepath, build-time copy awareness, `${...}` as unresolved, scheme
  skip list), exit-code-based CI guard semantics, four common
  root causes of broken links (chapter deletion leftover, section
  move, slug typo, external rot), and two CI wiring options (pages.yml
  pre-build vs ci.yml dispatch). CLAUDE.md Custom Skills table now
  carries the trigger keywords ("broken link", "404", "docs link",
  "hyperlink", "ÎßÅÌÅ¨ ÏÝêÍ≤Ä", "ÎßÅÌÅ¨ Íπ®Ïßê", "audit links", "link
  checker"). Case studies from PR #1157 (3 broken corrected) + PR
  #1161 (script introduction) included.

- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` ‚Äî docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ ÎßÅÌÅ¨ ÏÝïÏÝÅ + HTTP ÏÝêÍ≤Ä
  Ïä§ÌÅ¨Î¶ΩÌä∏.** site/src Ïùò Î™®ÎìÝ `.tsx`/`.ts` ÏóêÏÑú Î≥∏Î¨∏/JSX ÎßÅÌÅ¨ Ìå®ÌÑ¥ (
  `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`, Í∑∏Î¶¨Í≥Ý markdown
  Ïä§ÌÉÄÏùº ÎßÅÌÅ¨ ÌëúÍ∏∞) ÏùÑ Î™®Îëê Ï∂îÏ∂ú. 4 Î∂ÑÎ•ò:
  - **internal /docs/...** ‚Äî `site/src/app/docs/` ÌïòÏúÑ `page.tsx` slug
    ÏôÄ Ï∞®ÏßëÌï© ‚Üí ÎàÑÎùΩ Ïãú broken
  - **internal /<other>...** ‚Äî `/portfolio`, `/works`, `/petri-bundle/`
    Îì± ‚Üí app route + public asset + build-time copy (pages.yml Ïùò
    `docs/petri-bundle/` ‚Üí `site/out/petri-bundle/` step Ïù∏ÏßÄ) ÏôÄ ÎåÄÏ°∞
  - **anchor #section** ‚Äî Í∞ôÏùÄ page.tsx Ïùò `id="..."` ÏôÄ ÎåÄÏ°∞
  - **external http(s)://** ‚Äî `--http` ÏòµÌä∏Ïù∏ Ïãú HEAD/GET ÏúºÎ°ú reachability
    Í≤ÄÏÇ¨ (concurrent 8, 8s timeout, 200/3xx OK)
  CI ÌÜµÌï© ÏòµÏÖò: `python3 scripts/check_docs_links.py` ÎßåÏúºÎ°ú ÏÝïÏÝÅ Í≤ÄÏÇ¨
  ÌÜµÍ≥º Ïãú exit 0, broken Î∞úÍ≤¨ Ïãú exit 1. Ìñ•ÌõÑ pages.yml build job Ïùò
  pre-build step ÎòêÎäî Î≥Ñ ci.yml lint ÏúºÎ°ú wiring Í∞ÄÎä•.

  ÌòÑÏû¨ Ï∏°ÏÝï (Ïù¥ PR ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ): 193 link Ïä§Ï∫î, 0 broken, 17 external Î™®Îëê
  reachable, 2 unresolved (markdown-lite.tsx Ïùò regex Ìå®ÌÑ¥ Î¨∏ÏûêÏó¥, false
  positive Î¨¥Ïãú).
- **`scripts/check_docs_links.py` ‚Äî static + HTTP audit script for docs
  site links.** Extracts every link-shaped string from `site/src/**.tsx`
  / `.ts` (JSX `href="..."`, ``href={`...`}``, `src="..."`, `to="..."`,
  and markdown-style link syntax inside string literals). Classifies
  into four buckets:
  - **internal /docs/...** ‚Äî diffed against the `page.tsx` slug set
    under `site/src/app/docs/`; misses flagged.
  - **internal /\<other\>...** ‚Äî `/portfolio`, `/works`,
    `/petri-bundle/` etc., diffed against app routes + public assets +
    build-time copies (the script recognises the
    `docs/petri-bundle/` ‚Üí `site/out/petri-bundle/` copy step in
    `.github/workflows/pages.yml`).
  - **anchor #section** ‚Äî checked against `id="..."` occurrences in
    the emitting `page.tsx`.
  - **external http(s)://** ‚Äî opt-in `--http` HEAD/GET probe
    (concurrency 8, 8 s timeout, 200/3xx considered OK).
  Returns exit 0 on a clean run and exit 1 if anything is broken, so
  the script can later be wired into the Pages build (pre-build step)
  or into the `ci.yml` lint job. Current measurement after this PR:
  193 link occurrences scanned, 0 broken, all 17 external URLs
  reachable, 2 false-positive "unresolved" entries from regex pattern
  strings in `markdown-lite.tsx` (informational, not failure).

### Fixed

- **Docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ broken link 3 Í∞ú ÏÝïÏÝï (6 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏).** docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ ÎÇ¥Î∂Ä ÎßÅÌÅ¨
  ÏÝïÏÝÅ Ïä§Ï∫î Í≤∞Í≥º Îã§Ïùå 3 Í≤ΩÎ°úÍ∞Ä 404 ÏòÄÏùå ‚Äî Ìï¥Îãπ ÌéòÏù¥ÏßÄÍ∞Ä sitemap Ïóê Ï°¥Ïû¨ÌïòÏßÄ
  ÏïäÍ±∞ÎÇò Îã§Î•∏ slug Î°ú Ïù¥ÏÝÑÎêú ÏÉÅÌÉú:

    L38, L65). Ïã§ÏÝú ÎèÑÎ©îÏù∏ Ï∂îÍ∞Ä Î¨∏ÏÑúÎäî `runtime/domains` Ïä¨Îü¨Í∑∏.

  - `/docs/ops/observability` ‚Üí `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146). Í¥ÄÏ∏°ÏÑ± Î¨∏ÏÑúÎäî `ops/` Í∞Ä ÏïÑÎãàÎùº
    `verification/` ÏÑπÏÖò ÌïòÏúÑ Ïä¨Îü¨Í∑∏.

  ÌÉêÏßÄ Î∞©Î≤ï ‚Äî `grep` ÏúºÎ°ú `site/src/` Ïùò Î™®ÎìÝ `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  `href={\`/docs/...\`}`, markdown style `](/docs/...)` Ìå®ÌÑ¥ 23 Í∞ú Ï∂îÏ∂ú ‚Üí
  `find site/src/app/docs -name "page.tsx"` Ïùò 50 Í∞ú Ïã§Ïû¨ ÌéòÏù¥ÏßÄ Ïä¨Îü¨Í∑∏ÏôÄ
  `comm -23` ÏúºÎ°ú Ï∞®ÏßëÌï© ‚Üí 3 broken Î∞úÍ≤¨. `npm run build` ÏÑ±Í≥µ ÌõÑ 6 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏
  ÍµêÏ≤¥. doc Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω only, ÌñâÏúÑ Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω 0.

- **Docs site broken-link fix ‚Äî 3 paths (6 sites).** A static scan of
  internal links in `site/src/` found three routes that returned 404
  because the target slug either no longer exists (an earlier `build/`
  chapter was retired) or was renamed to another section:
  - `/docs/build/add-domain` ‚Üí `/docs/runtime/domains`
    (`run/analyze/page.tsx` L38, L65).
  - `/docs/build/add-tool` ‚Üí `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol`
    (`run/messaging/page.tsx` L35, L60).
  - `/docs/ops/observability` ‚Üí `/docs/verification/observability`
    (`petri/run/page.tsx` L77, L146).

  Detection used `grep` against `site/src/` for all `href="(/docs/...)"`,
  ``href={`/docs/...`}``, and markdown-style `](/docs/...)` patterns
  (23 unique link targets) cross-referenced via `comm -23` against the
  50 page-slug paths under `site/src/app/docs/**/page.tsx`; the
  difference produced exactly 3 broken targets. `npm run build` passes
  after the 6 edits. Documentation only ‚Äî no behavioural impact.

### Added

- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py` Ïùò `assemble()` Ïóê Phase 0 (Wrapper
  Override) Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. env var `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` Í∞Ä set
  ÎêòÎ©¥ JSON ÏùÑ `dict[str, str]` Î°ú Î°úÎìúÌï¥ Í∑∏ value Îì§ÏùÑ concat Ìïú Í≤∞Í≥ºÎ°ú
  `base_system` ÏùÑ ÎåÄÏ≤¥. ÌõÑÏÜç Phase (skill / memory / extra) Îäî Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú
  ÏÝÅÏö©. env unset ÏùÄ baseline ÏùÑ ÏúÝÏßÄÌïòÏßÄÎßå, env Í∞Ä set Îêú Îí§ ÌååÏùº ÎàÑÎùΩ /
  malformed JSON / dict ÏïÑÎãò / empty dict / non-string entry Í∞Ä ÎÇòÏò§Î©¥
  fail-closed `RuntimeError` Î°ú real audit quota Î•º baseline prompt Ïóê
  Ïì∞ÏßÄ ÏïäÍ≤å Ìï®. `autoresearch/train.py` Ïùò
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY` Î•º `True` Î°ú flip Ìï¥ real-mode ÌôúÏÑ±Ìôî ‚Äî
  outer-loop agent Í∞Ä `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` Î•º ÏàòÏÝïÌïòÎ©¥ `geode audit`
  Ïùò system prompt Í∞Ä Ïã§ÏÝúÎ°ú Í∑∏ dict Ïùò ÎÇ¥Ïö©ÏúºÎ°ú ÎèôÏûë. `.env.example` Ïóê
  `# GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=` Ìï≠Î™© + ÏÇ¨Ïö© ÏÑ§Î™Ö Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. ÏãÝÍ∑ú 9 pytest
  (`tests/test_prompt_assembler.py` Ïùò `TestWrapperOverrideHook` ‚Äî
  env-unset baseline / ÏÝïÏÉÅ override / ÌååÏùº ÎàÑÎùΩ raise / malformed
  JSON raise / ÎπÑ-dict raise / empty dict raise / non-string entry raise /
  hash Í¥ÄÏ∏°ÏÑ± / extra Ìï©ÏÑ±)
  + train.py Ïùò fail-fast test Î•º real-mode subprocess argv/env Í≤ÄÏ¶ù
  ÏúºÎ°ú ÍµêÏ≤¥ (mock subprocess, quota ÏÇ¨Ïö© ÏóÜÏùå).
- **Autoresearch real-mode runtime hook (`GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`).**
  Adds Phase 0 (Wrapper Override) to `core/llm/prompt_assembler.py`'s
  `assemble()`. When `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE=<json-path>` is set, the
  JSON is loaded as `dict[str, str]` and its values are concatenated to
  replace `base_system`; the remaining phases (skill / memory / extra)
  still apply on top. When the env is unset, baseline behavior is
  unchanged; once the env is set, missing files, malformed JSON,
  non-dict payloads, empty dicts, or non-string entries fail closed with
  `RuntimeError` so real audit quota is not spent on the baseline prompt.
  `autoresearch/train.py` flips
  `WRAPPER_OVERRIDE_HOOK_READY = True`, enabling real-mode runs ‚Äî the
  outer-loop agent's edits to `WRAPPER_PROMPT_SECTIONS` now actually
  reach the `geode audit` system prompt. `.env.example` documents the
  new optional variable. Nine new pytest cases in
  `tests/test_prompt_assembler.py::TestWrapperOverrideHook` (baseline /
  override / missing file raises / malformed JSON raises / non-dict raises /
  empty dict raises / non-string entries raise / hash observability /
  composition with bootstrap extras) plus
  the existing `tests/test_autoresearch_train.py` fail-fast test
  replaced by a real-mode subprocess argv/env assertion (subprocess
  mocked ‚Äî no LLM quota consumed).

- **Phase 1a ‚Äî Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** Hermes
  Ìù°Ïàò plan(`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md`) Ïùò Ï≤´
  PR. `sessions.db` Ïóê `messages` ÌÖåÏù¥Î∏î (id / session_id / seq / role /
  content / tool_call_id / tool_calls / tool_name / timestamp /
  token_count / finish_reason / reasoning / metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id,
  seq)`) + `idx_messages_session` + `idx_messages_tool_name` ÏãÝÏÑ§.
  `SessionCheckpoint.save()` Í∞Ä JSON Î≥∏Î¨∏ ÏÝÄÏû• ÏßÅÌõÑ
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()` Î°ú Î≥∏Î¨∏ÏùÑ mirror ‚Äî JSON ÏùÄ Phase 1b
  Ïùò SoT ÏÝÑÌôòÍπåÏßÄ authoritative. DB Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú WARN Î°úÍπÖ + `exc_info=True`,
  JSON Î≥∏Î¨∏ÏùÄ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú Î≥¥Ï°¥ (graceful degradation). ÎèôÏùº/Ï∂ïÏÜå/Îπà message
  list Ïùò Ïû¨ÏÝÄÏû• Î™®Îëê idempotent ‚Äî Ï§ÑÏñ¥ÎìÝ seq Ïùò stale row ÏôÄ Îπà ÏÝÄÏû•Ïùò
  ÏûîÏó¨ row ÍπåÏßÄ ÏÝúÍ±∞Ìï¥ JSON ‚Üî DB Í∞Ä Ìï≠ÏÉÅ ÏÝïÎÝ¨. Anthropic content blocks
  (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) ÏôÄ OpenAI ÌòïÏãù (`tool_calls`
  / `tool_call_id` / `name`) ÏñëÏ™Ω Ï∂îÏ∂ú + 18 ÏãÝÍ∑ú ÌÖåÏä§Ìä∏ (dual-write
  parity / sqlite Ïã§Ìå® graceful / openai+anthropic Ï∂îÏ∂ú / stale row
  ÏÝúÍ±∞ / Îπà ÏÝÄÏû• ÏÝïÌï©). Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier Í∞Ä CRITICAL 2 Í±¥
  (stale row + Îπà-save ÏûîÏû¨) ÏùÑ Î∞úÍ≤¨¬∑Î∞òÏòÅ.
- **Phase 1a ‚Äî Long-term Recall: messages table + dual-write.** First PR
  of the Hermes-absorption plan (`docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-
  absorption.md`). Adds a `messages` table to `sessions.db` (columns: id
  / session_id / seq / role / content / tool_call_id / tool_calls /
  tool_name / timestamp / token_count / finish_reason / reasoning /
  metadata + `UNIQUE(session_id, seq)`) plus `idx_messages_session` and
  `idx_messages_tool_name`. `SessionCheckpoint.save()` mirrors the full
  message list into the table right after the JSON write, via
  `SessionManager.upsert_messages()`; JSON remains SoT until Phase 1b
  flips the source. DB failures emit a WARNING with `exc_info=True` and
  leave the JSON checkpoint intact (graceful degradation). Re-saving the
  same, shorter, or empty message list is idempotent ‚Äî stale rows from a
  shrunk transcript and leftovers from an empty save are removed so JSON
  and the mirror stay aligned. The extractor reads both Anthropic content
  blocks (`tool_use` / `tool_result` / `thinking`) and OpenAI-style
  fields (`tool_calls` / `tool_call_id` / `name`). 18 new tests cover
  dual-write parity, a real `sqlite3.OperationalError` graceful path,
  OpenAI/Anthropic extraction, stale-row removal, and empty-save
  alignment. A Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier round caught two CRITICAL
  gaps (stale rows on shrink, leftovers on empty save), both fixed.

### Changed

- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  PR #1145 Ïùò 6-module python stub (`loop.py` / `hypothesis.py` /
  `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py` /
  `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" ÌëúÍ∏∞Î°ú implementation
  ÎåÄÍ∏∞) ÏùÑ ÌèêÍ∏∞ÌïòÍ≥Ý Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) (228791f, MIT,
  26K+ stars) Ïùò 3-file Ìå®ÌÑ¥ (`prepare.py` + `train.py` + `program.md`)
  ÏúºÎ°ú Ïû¨Íµ¨ÏÑ±. ML ÎèÑÎ©îÏù∏ (GPT pre-train + `val_bpb`) ÏùÑ GEODE alignment-
  audit ÎèÑÎ©îÏù∏ (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval 5-axis fitness) ÏúºÎ°ú ÏµúÏÜå
  ÍµêÏ≤¥. Karpathy ÏõêÎ≥∏Ïùò single-mutation-file + fixed-budget + grep-friendly
  stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col + git-as-optimizer ÏÝïÏãÝ ÏúÝÏßÄ.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` ‚Äî `~/.cache/autoresearch/` Ïùò fineweb Îã§Ïö¥
    Î°úÎìú + tokenizer ÌïôÏäµ ÏûêÎ¶¨Ïóê **fixed audit harness sanity check**
    (10 safe seed file count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit
    --help` reachability). `~/.cache` Í∞Ä read-only Ïùº Îïå worktree-local
    fallback.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` ‚Äî GPT ÌïôÏäµ ÏûêÎ¶¨Ïóê **wrapper system-prompt
    section dict** (mutation target) + `geode audit` subprocess invocation
    (staged: `--seed-select` / `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) + 5-axis
    fitness extraction. ÌòÑÏû¨ GEODE core Í∞Ä `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Î•º
    ÏïÑÏßÅ consume ÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÏúºÎØÄÎ°ú real mode Îäî fail-fast (`RuntimeError`),
    `--dry-run` Îßå working mode ‚Äî runtime hook ÏùÄ follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` ‚Äî ML research direction ÏûêÎ¶¨Ïóê Petri
    direction (gen 0 hypothesis space, `input_hallucination` Œî +1.13 /
    `overrefusal` +0.31 / `broken_tool_use` ‚àí1.28 driver seed Ïùò prior,
    9 hypothesis = wrapper section ablation).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ‚Äî Petri-signal fork Ïùò quick start.
  - `pyproject.toml` Ïùò `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ÏÝúÍ±∞ (Karpathy ÏõêÎ≥∏ ÏÝïÏãÝ: single-script `uv run python
    autoresearch/train.py`, CLI wrapping ÏóÜÏùå).
  - 3 ÏãÝÍ∑ú pytest (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) ‚Äî argv Í∞Ä ÌòÑÏû¨
    `geode audit` flag Îßå ÏÇ¨Ïö© + ÏÇ¨ÎùºÏßÑ flag Ïùò retro ÌöåÌîº + real-mode
    fail-fast + dry-run baseline Ïùò fitness range Í≤ÄÏ¶ù.
  - Í∑∏ÎùºÏö¥Îî© reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (Îã®Ïàú clone,
    GEODE repo ÏôÄ git Ïó∞Îèô ÏóÜÏùå).
  - Codex MCP cross-LLM verifier Í∞Ä CRITICAL 1 + HIGH 4 ÏûêÎèô fix ‚Äî
    real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flag, rubric grep Ïùò stale ÌòïÏãù,
    `~/.cache` Í∂åÌïú fallback.
- **Autoresearch outer-loop: Karpathy 3-file fork (Petri-signal domain).**
  Retires the 6-module Python stub from PR #1145 (`loop.py` /
  `hypothesis.py` / `fitness.py` / `ratchet.py` / `rationale_extractor.py`
  / `baseline_marker.py`, 480 LOC, "follow-up PR1" placeholder) and
  rebases the outer loop on the Karpathy
  [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) 3-file pattern
  (228791f, MIT, 26K+ stars). Domain swapped from ML pre-training
  (`val_bpb`) to GEODE alignment-audit (Petri seed pool + AlphaEval
  5-axis fitness), preserving Karpathy's single-mutation-file +
  fixed-budget + grep-friendly stdout + `results.tsv` 5-col +
  git-as-optimizer spirit.
  - `autoresearch/prepare.py` replaces fineweb download + tokenizer
    training with a **fixed audit harness sanity check** (seed file
    count, 19-dim YAML rubric parse, `geode audit --help` reachability),
    and falls back to a worktree-local report if `~/.cache` is read-only.
  - `autoresearch/train.py` replaces the GPT training loop with a
    **wrapper system-prompt section dict** (the agent's mutation surface)
    plus a `geode audit` subprocess invocation (staged: `--seed-select` /
    `--dim-set` / `--live` / `--yes`) and 5-axis fitness extraction.
    Until GEODE core consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`, real mode
    fail-fasts and only `--dry-run` is non-deceptive; the runtime hook
    lands in a follow-up PR.
  - `autoresearch/program.md` replaces the ML research direction with a
    Petri direction (gen-0 hypothesis space drawn from yesterday's
    driver seeds: `input_hallucination` Œî +1.13 / `overrefusal` +0.31 /
    `broken_tool_use` ‚àí1.28 across three model families, pruning the
    space to nine wrapper-section ablations).
  - `autoresearch/README.md` ships a Petri-signal fork quick-start.
  - `pyproject.toml` drops the `geode-research = "autoresearch.loop:cli"`
    entry-point ‚Äî Karpathy keeps the runner as a plain `python train.py`,
    so the fork does too.
  - Three new pytest tests (`tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`) pin the
    current `geode audit` flag set, block obsolete flags from creeping
    back in, assert the real-mode fail-fast, and check the dry-run
    fitness range.
  - Grounding reference clone: `~/workspace/autoresearch` (a plain
    clone, not wired into the GEODE repo).
  - Codex MCP acted as a cross-LLM verifier and applied one CRITICAL fix
    plus four HIGH fixes ‚Äî real-mode deception, obsolete CLI flags,
    stale rubric grep, and the `~/.cache` permission fallback.

### Fixed

- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold verifier fixes.** `prepare.py` now parses the
  19-dimension YAML rubric instead of grepping for a stale `- name:` shape,
  falls back to a workspace-local prepare report when `~/.cache` is not
  writable, and `train.py` fail-fast blocks real audit mode until GEODE core
  actually consumes `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE`. The staged live argv now matches
  the current `geode audit` CLI (`--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`,
  `--yes`) instead of obsolete `--rubric` / `--budget-minutes` flags.
- **Autoresearch Petri scaffold Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏàòÏÝï.** `prepare.py` Í∞Ä Ïò§ÎûòÎêú
  `- name:` ÌòïÏãù grep ÎåÄÏãÝ 19-dim YAML rubric ÏùÑ ÏßÅÏÝë parse ÌïòÍ≥Ý,
  `~/.cache` Ïóê Ïì∏ Ïàò ÏóÜÏùÑ Îïå worktree-local prepare report Î°ú fallback
  Ìï©ÎãàÎã§. `train.py` Îäî GEODE core Í∞Ä `GEODE_WRAPPER_OVERRIDE` Î•º Ïã§ÏÝúÎ°ú
  consume ÌïòÍ∏∞ ÏÝÑÍπåÏßÄ real audit mode Î•º fail-fast Î°ú ÎßâÏïÑ, wrapper mutation
  Ïù¥ ÏÝÅÏö©ÎêòÎäî Í≤ÉÏ≤òÎüº Î≥¥Ïù¥Îäî Ï∞©ÏãúÎ•º ÏÝúÍ±∞ÌñàÏäµÎãàÎã§. staged live argv ÎèÑ ÌòÑÏû¨
  `geode audit` CLI Ïùò `--seed-select`, `--dim-set`, `--live`, `--yes` Ïóê
  ÎßûÏ∑ÑÏäµÎãàÎã§.

  README/README.ko peer comparison `Multi-layer guardrails` ÏÖÄ + `What's

- **Verification 5-Layer label fix ‚Äî `Confidence Gate` ‚Üí `Calibration`.**
  Audit of `core/verification/` revealed that the README's "5-Layer
  Verification" cell (`G1-G4 + BiasBuster + Cross-LLM + Confidence Gate +
  Rights Risk`) was inaccurate. The true layer 5 is
  `core/verification/calibration.py` (its docstring spells out "Swiss Cheese
  Layer 5: orthogonal to G1-G4 (structural), BiasBuster (cognitive),
  Cross-LLM (inter-model). Calibration validates against external expert
  consensus"). What the README called "Confidence Gate" is actually the
  confidence multiplier `(1 - CV) √ó 100` inside `plugins/game_ip/nodes/
  scoring.py:301` ‚Äî a scoring sub-routine, not a verification layer.
  Grounded layer map:
  - **Layer 1 (structural)** ‚Äî `guardrails.py` `_g1_schema` (L13),
    `_g2_range` (L47), `_g3_grounding` (L90), `_g4_consistency` (L148)
  - **Layer 2 (cognitive)** ‚Äî `biasbuster.py:43` `run_biasbuster`,
    4-step RECOGNIZE ‚Üí EXPLAIN ‚Üí ALTER ‚Üí EVALUATE
  - **Layer 3 (inter-model)** ‚Äî `cross_llm.py:81` `run_cross_llm_check`
    + `stats.py` Krippendorff Œ±
  - **Layer 4 (legal)** ‚Äî `rights_risk.py:79` `check_rights_risk`
  - **Layer 5 (Ground Truth)** ‚Äî `calibration.py:328` `run_calibration`,
    expert-annotated Golden Set comparison
  README and README.ko peer comparison `Multi-layer guardrails` cell and
  `What's inside` table both fixed (`Confidence Gate` ‚Üí `Calibration`).
  Each layer now carries the semantic label parenthetical.

### Infrastructure

- **Petri raw-archive analyzer + safe10 seed pool.** `scripts/petri_analyze.py`
  Îäî `docs/petri-bundle/logs/` Ïùò zstd inspect-ai archive Î•º ÏùΩÏñ¥ 17 dim √ó
  archive matrix / 3-family pair Œî / per-sample long-form CSV Î•º ÏÉùÏÑ±ÌïòÎäî
  read-only Î∂ÑÏÑùÍ∏∞. LLM/ÎÑ§Ìä∏ÏõåÌÅ¨ Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏóÜÏùå. `plugins/petri_audit/seeds_safe10/`
  Îäî 5 base seed √ó 2 paraphrase (calibration / reasoning / efficiency /
  research / reasoning_critique) = 10 ÏïàÏÝÑ seed pool ‚Äî gpt-5.5 Ïùò cancellation
  scenario ÌöåÌîº + Îã§Ïùå generation Ïùò baseline. ÏÇ∞Î¨º docs Îäî
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-*` (insights / matrix / pairs / per-sample /
  summary) + `docs/audits/eval-logs/2026-05-15-*.summary.yaml` Ïùò 2 archive
  ÏöîÏïΩ + `docs/audits/2026-05-15-autoresearch-gen0-plan.md` (autoresearch
  Generation 0 Ïùò Ï≤´ outer loop plan) + `docs/plans/2026-05-14-hermes-
  strengths-absorption.md` (Hermes Ìù°Ïàò 4-Phase plan).
- **Petri raw-archive analyzer + safe10 seed pool.** `scripts/petri_analyze.py`
  is a read-only analyzer that reads zstd inspect-ai archives from
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/` and emits a 17-dim √ó archive matrix, a 3-family
  paired Œî matrix, and a per-sample long-form CSV (no LLM/network calls).
  `plugins/petri_audit/seeds_safe10/` ships a 5 base √ó 2 paraphrase = 10
  safe seed pool (calibration / reasoning / efficiency / research /
  reasoning_critique) that side-steps the gpt-5.5 cancellation scenarios
  and serves as the next-generation baseline. The audit docs land at
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-*` (insights / matrix / pairs / per-sample
  / summary), `docs/audits/eval-logs/2026-05-15-*.summary.yaml` (two archive
  summaries), `docs/audits/2026-05-15-autoresearch-gen0-plan.md` (the
  Generation 0 outer-loop plan for autoresearch), and `docs/plans/
  2026-05-14-hermes-strengths-absorption.md` (the four-phase Hermes
  absorption plan).

- **Petri √ó Claude Code judge adapter (autoresearch Phase 5).** Extends

    field collisions (highlights/summary/justification) plus duplicate dims.

- **Autoresearch outer-loop bootstrap (design + stub).** GEODE Ïùò
  self-improving harness Ïùò outer loop ÎèÑÏûÖ ‚Äî Karpathy autoresearch
  (2026-03, 26K+ stars) Ïùò 3-file pattern Ïùò GEODE ÏÝÅÏö©. Î≥∏ PR Ïùò
  deliverable = design + minimal stub (ÏΩîÎìú implementation ÏùÄ follow-up
  PR1-4 Ïóê Î∂ÑÏÇ∞):
  - `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` ‚Äî outer-loop Ïùò spec (lifecycle
    8 step + Karpathy 5 ÏõêÏπô + rationale extractor + baseline marker +
    results.tsv schema + risks + roadmap)
  - `autoresearch/` top-level package ‚Äî `__init__.py` + `program.md`
    (human-direction template) + `README.md` (quick start + invariants)
    + 6 module stub (`loop.py` CLI runner + `hypothesis.py` +
    `fitness.py` 5-axis aggregate + `ratchet.py` git ops +
    `rationale_extractor.py` explanation/highlights/summary NLP +
    `baseline_marker.py` generation-N metadata)
  - `autoresearch/state/` ‚Äî `.gitignored` runtime artifact (results.tsv
    + audit_logs + failure_log)
  - `pyproject.toml` Ïùò `geode-research` entry-point + ruff `src` Ïùò
    `autoresearch` Ìè¨Ìï®
  - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` Ïùò ruff + mypy step Ïùò `autoresearch/`
    Ìè¨Ìï® ‚Äî bootstrap PR Ïùò stub ÎèÑ CI quality gate ÌÜµÍ≥º
  - **Plugin ÎÝàÎ≤® Î∂ÄÏÝÅÌï©** rationale (ÏûêÍ∏∞Ï∞∏Ï°∞ loop + lifecycle mismatch
    + meta-level ÏùòÎØ∏ Î∂ÑÎ¶¨) Î™ÖÏãú. **mutation_blocklist Ïùò ÏûêÍ∏∞Ï∞∏Ï°∞ ÌöåÌîº**
    (`autoresearch/`, `plugins/petri_audit/`, `core/llm/router/` Ïùò
    autoresearch mutation Í∏àÏßÄ).

- **Autoresearch outer-loop bootstrap (design + stub).** Introduces
  GEODE's self-improving harness outer loop ‚Äî Karpathy autoresearch's
  three-file pattern mapped onto GEODE. This PR delivers spec + minimal
  stub; the runtime implementation lands in follow-up PR1-4.
  - `docs/architecture/autoresearch.md` is the SOT for the outer-loop
    spec (8-step lifecycle, Karpathy's five principles, rationale
    extractor, baseline marker, results.tsv schema, risks, roadmap).
  - `autoresearch/` top-level package: `program.md` (human-authored
    direction template), `README.md` (invariants + quick start), six
    module stubs (`loop.py` CLI runner, `hypothesis.py`, `fitness.py`
    five-axis aggregate, `ratchet.py` git ops, `rationale_extractor.py`
    NLP over explanation/highlights/summary, `baseline_marker.py`
    generation-N metadata).
  - `autoresearch/state/` is `.gitignored` (results.tsv + audit_logs +
    failure_log).
  - `pyproject.toml` adds the `geode-research` console script and lists
    `autoresearch` under ruff `src`; `.github/workflows/ci.yml` extends
    ruff + mypy to cover `autoresearch/` so the stub itself passes the
    project's quality ratchet.
  - The design explicitly rejects placing the loop under `plugins/` (the
    plugin lifecycle is single-process callback; autoresearch is a
    multi-iteration outer process) and pins a `mutation_blocklist` so
    the loop cannot mutate the eval-meta surface (autoresearch itself,
    `plugins/petri_audit/`, `core/llm/router/`).

### Changed

- **Petri A3 judge split (1‚Üí5 group).** `plugins/petri_audit/` Ïùò
  judge Ìò∏Ï∂úÏù¥ 38-dim Îã®Ïùº mega-prompt 1 ÌöåÏóêÏÑú 5 Í∑∏Î£π (tool_mechanics
  / reality_degradation / boundary_respect / autonomy_efficiency /
  calibration_anchors) ÏúºÎ°ú Î∂ÑÎ¶¨. ÏùòÎèÑ ‚Äî v3 audit (2026-05-12) Í∞Ä
  Îã®Ïùº tool-syntax Ïã§Ìå® Ïù¥Î≤§Ìä∏Ïóê `broken_tool_use=10` + `input_
  hallucination=8` ÏùÑ ÎèôÏãú Î∂ÄÏó¨Ìïú double-counting bias Ï∞®Îã®. `judge_
  schema.py` Ïóê `JudgeScore.is_unknown` + `mean_score_excluding_
  unknown` Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (Anthropic eval blog Ïùò "Unknown" literal Í∂åÏû• Ìå®ÌÑ¥).
  `runner.py` Ïóê `SPLIT_TOKEN_ASSUMPTIONS` + `judge_mode={"legacy",
  "split"}` flag + 5√ó judge-call cost Î™®Îç∏ Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚Äî dry-run Ïùò estimate
  Îäî split Î™®ÎìúÏùº Îïå ~5√ó Î°ú ÏÉÅÏäπ (`~$0.26 ‚Üí ~$1.30`). Îã®, PR #1133
  Ïùò OAuth path Ïùò cost zeroing ÏùÄ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÏÝÅÏö©ÎêòÎØÄÎ°ú Î≥∏ Î≥ÄÍ≤ΩÏùÄ PAYG
  Í≤ΩÎ°úÏóêÎßå ÏòÅÌñ•. 5-call Ïùò inspect-petri Ï∏° orchestration ÏùÄ upstream
  PR ÎåÄÍ∏∞ Ï§ëÏù¥Îùº runtime Ïùò default Îäî legacy Î™®Îìú ÏúÝÏßÄ ‚Äî Î≥∏ PR ÏùÄ
  data contract + cost preview Îã®Í≥Ñ.
  ÏÉà ÌååÏùº ‚Äî `judge_dims/geode_5axes_split.yaml` (group Î©îÌÉÄÎç∞Ïù¥ÌÑ∞),
  `group{1..5}_*.md` (Í∑∏Î£πÎ≥Ñ rubric), `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_judge_split.py` (210 LOC, group / Unknown / Î≥ÄÏàò ÎåÄÏ°∞ Í∞ÄÎìú).
  19 dim Ïùò split Í∑∏Î£π Îß§Ìïë ‚Äî `eval_awareness` ‚Üí Group 2 (reality_
  degradation), `unprompted_sycophancy` ‚Üí Group 3 (boundary_respect).
  reference: docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a3-judge-split-design.md.
- **Petri A3 judge split (1‚Üí5 group).** `plugins/petri_audit/`
  collapses its 38-dim mega-prompt into five semantically grouped
  judge calls (tool_mechanics / reality_degradation / boundary_
  respect / autonomy_efficiency / calibration_anchors). Motivation ‚Äî
  the v3 audit (2026-05-12) co-scored `broken_tool_use=10` AND
  `input_hallucination=8` on a single tool-syntax failure event,
  driving the substantially invalid `input_hallucination Œî +1.04`
  multi-model finding. `judge_schema.py` gains `JudgeScore.is_
  unknown` + `mean_score_excluding_unknown` per Anthropic's "Unknown"
  literal eval-pattern recommendation. `runner.py` adds `SPLIT_
  TOKEN_ASSUMPTIONS` + `judge_mode={"legacy", "split"}` + a 5√ó
  judge-call cost model ‚Äî dry-run estimate rises to ~5√ó in split
  mode (`~$0.26 ‚Üí ~$1.30`). PR #1133's OAuth-path cost zeroing still
  applies, so the cost rise only hits PAYG routes. inspect-petri-side
  orchestration for the 5-call pattern is staged upstream, so the
  runtime default remains legacy ‚Äî this PR ships the data contract
  + cost preview only.
  New files ‚Äî `judge_dims/geode_5axes_split.yaml` (group metadata),
  `group{1..5}_*.md` (per-group rubrics), `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_judge_split.py` (210 LOC, group / Unknown / variance guards).
  19-dim split mapping ‚Äî `eval_awareness` ‚Üí Group 2 (reality_
  degradation), `unprompted_sycophancy` ‚Üí Group 3 (boundary_respect).
  reference: docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a3-judge-split-design.md.

### Infrastructure

- **Pages publish Ïùò render-lint gate (PR #1131 ratchet Ïùò markdown/YAML
  ÎèÑÎ©îÏù∏ ÌôïÏû•).** `docs/petri-bundle/` + `docs/audits/` Ïùò 4 caveat Î¨∏ÏÑú +
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/*.yaml` + `docs/petri-bundle/**/*.json` Ïóê
  ÎåÄÌï¥ `pymarkdownlnt` (0.9.37) + `yamllint` (1.38.0) + stdlib JSON ÌååÏÑú
  ratchet ÏùÑ ÎèÑÏûÖ. `.github/workflows/pages.yml` Ïóê `lint` job ÏãÝÏÑ§
  (`build needs: lint`) ‚Äî ÏûòÎ™ªÎêú markdown / YAML / JSON Ïù¥ GitHub Pages
  Î°ú Î∞∞Ìè¨ÎêòÍ∏∞ ÏÝÑÏóê fail-fast. ÎèôÏùº set Ïùò hook ÏùÑ `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
  Î°ú mirror ‚Äî Î°úÏª¨ commit / CI Í∞Ä Í∞ôÏùÄ ÏúÑÎ∞òÏùÑ Í∞ôÏùÄ Î©îÏãúÏßÄÎ°ú Î≥¥Í≥Ý. 4 file
  ÏãÝÍ∑ú ‚Äî `.pymarkdown.json`, `.yamllint.yaml`, `scripts/lint_pages_markdown.sh`
  (allowlist + uvx fallback), `tests/test_render_lint_config.py` (12-test
  ratchet ÏúºÎ°ú config ÏûêÏ≤¥Ïùò Î¨¥ÏÑ±Ìïú regression Ï∞®Îã®), `docs/architecture/
  render-lint.md` (rule-by-rule Ïùò Í∑ºÍ±∞ + legacy carve-out ÏÝïÏ±Ö). PR #1131
  Ïùò `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` (listing.json status check) ÏôÄ
  Í∞ôÏùÄ pipeline Ïùò sibling defense ‚Äî lint ‚Üí build ‚Üí deploy chain.
- **Pages publish render-lint gate (markdown / YAML domain extension of
  PR #1131's ratchet).** Adds `pymarkdownlnt` (0.9.37) + `yamllint`
  (1.38.0) + stdlib JSON parsing to gate the 4 caveat docs under
  `docs/audits/` + `docs/petri-bundle/`, the petri-bundle README, and
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/*.yaml`. A new `lint` job in
  `.github/workflows/pages.yml` with `build needs: lint` fails fast on
  malformed input before the Next.js export burns CI time. The same
  hook set is mirrored in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` so local commits
  surface identical violations. 4 new files ‚Äî `.pymarkdown.json`,
  `.yamllint.yaml`, `scripts/lint_pages_markdown.sh` (allowlist + uvx
  fallback), `tests/test_render_lint_config.py` (12-test ratchet
  guarding the gate's own configs against silent regression), and
  `docs/architecture/render-lint.md` (rule-by-rule rationale + legacy
  carve-out policy). Sibling defense to PR #1131's
  `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` ‚Äî together they form the lint ‚Üí
  build ‚Üí deploy chain.

### Added

- **CLI LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ ‚Äî Tier 1 (Unicode) + Tier 2 (2D pretty-print).**
  `core/ui/latex.py` ÏãÝÍ∑ú. Îã§Î•∏ frontier LLM CLI (Claude Code, Codex CLI,
  Aider, jupyter-console) Í∞Ä Î™®Îëê LaTeX Î•º raw text Î°ú ÌùòÎ¶¨Îäî ÎèôÏïà GEODE
  Îäî Îëê Îã®Í≥Ñ Ìè¥Î∞±ÏúºÎ°ú ÎÝåÎçîÌï©ÎãàÎã§.

  - **Tier 1 ‚Äî pylatexenc** (Î™®ÎìÝ ÌÑ∞ÎØ∏ÎÑê). `\alpha` ‚Üí Œ±, `x^{2}` ‚Üí x¬≤,
    `\text{operators}` ‚Üí operators. ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê ÏòàÏãú `Complexity(f) = \#\,
    \text{operators} + \#\,\text{variables} + \text{depth}(f)` Í∞Ä
    `Complexity(f) = # operators + # variables + depth(f)` Î°ú ÌùêÎ¶Ñ.
    pure-Python, ~5 MB.
  - **Tier 2 ‚Äî latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty** (Î™®ÎìÝ ÌÑ∞ÎØ∏ÎÑê, Î©ÄÌã∞ÎùºÏù∏ Ï∂úÎÝ•).
    `block=True` + 2D ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ (`\frac`, `\matrix`, `\sum_`, `\int_`,
    `\prod_`, `\binom`, `\sqrt{`, `\lim_`) Í∞êÏßÄ ÏãúÏóêÎßå SymPy ÌååÏÑú Ìò∏Ï∂ú.
    `\frac{a+b}{c+d}` Í∞Ä 3 Ï§Ñ Unicode Î∂ÑÏàòÎ°ú ÎÝåÎçî (Ïòà: `a + b ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ c +
    d`). ÌååÏÑú Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú Tier 1 Î°ú silent fallback.
  - **`extract_and_render_inline`** ‚Äî ÏÇ∞Î¨∏ ÏïàÏóê ÏÑûÏù∏ `$...$` (Ïù∏ÎùºÏù∏) /
    `$$...$$` (Î∏îÎ°ù) ÏÑ∏Í∑∏Î®ºÌä∏ Ïä§Ï∫î. docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ MarkdownLite ÏôÄ ÎèôÏùºÌïú
    Ïö∞ÏÑÝÏàúÏúÑ (block > inline > ÌÖçÏä§Ìä∏). "ÎπÑÏö© $3.00 Î∞úÏÉù" Í∞ôÏù¥ delimiter
    ÏïàÏ™ΩÏóê Í≥µÎ∞± ÏãúÏûë/ÎÅù ÏûàÎäî Í≤ΩÏö∞ ÏàòÏãùÏúºÎ°ú Ïò§Ïù∏Ïãù Ïïà Îê®.

  ÏùòÏ°¥ÏÑ± Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚Äî `pylatexenc>=2.10` (~5 MB) + `latex2sympy2>=1.9` +
  `sympy>=1.12` (~30 MB). ÌÖåÏä§Ìä∏ 19 Ï¢Ö (`tests/test_ui_latex.py`) ‚Äî
  Tier 1/2/ÌòºÌï© Ïª®ÌÖêÏ∏Ý + Í∞ÄÍ≤© Ïò§Ïù∏Ïãù Î∞©ÏßÄ + parse Ïã§Ìå® Ìè¥Î∞± ÏºÄÏù¥Ïä§.
  Ïô∏Î∂Ä ÌÜµÌï©ÏùÄ Î≥∏ PR Î≤îÏúÑ Î∞ñ (ÎùºÏù¥Î∏åÎü¨Î¶¨ + ÌÖåÏä§Ìä∏Îßå). Îã§Ïùå Îã®Í≥Ñ ÌõÑÎ≥¥ ‚Äî
  `event_renderer` Í∞Ä LLM ÏùëÎãµ ÌÖçÏä§Ìä∏Ïóê `extract_and_render_inline` ÏÝÅÏö©.

- **CLI LaTeX rendering ‚Äî Tier 1 (Unicode) + Tier 2 (2D pretty-print).**
  New `core/ui/latex.py`. Every other frontier LLM CLI surveyed (Claude
  Code, Codex CLI, Aider, jupyter-console) ships LaTeX from the model
  as raw backslash-form text; GEODE renders it through a two-stage
  fallback.

  - **Tier 1 ‚Äî pylatexenc** (every terminal). `\alpha` ‚Üí Œ±, `x^{2}` ‚Üí
    x¬≤, `\text{operators}` ‚Üí operators. The user-facing example
    `Complexity(f) = \#\,\text{operators} + \#\,\text{variables} +
    \text{depth}(f)` flows out as `Complexity(f) = # operators + #
    variables + depth(f)`. Pure-Python, ~5 MB.
  - **Tier 2 ‚Äî latex2sympy2 + sympy.pretty** (every terminal,
    multi-line output). Invoked only when `block=True` and a 2D token
    (`\frac`, `\matrix`, `\sum_`, `\int_`, `\prod_`, `\binom`,
    `\sqrt{`, `\lim_`) is present. `\frac{a+b}{c+d}` renders as a
    three-line Unicode fraction. Silent fall-back to Tier 1 on parser
    failure.
  - **`extract_and_render_inline`** ‚Äî scans mixed prose for `$...$`
    (inline) and `$$...$$` (block) segments, matching the docs-site
    MarkdownLite priority order (block > inline > literal text). The
    inline regex forbids whitespace adjacent to a delimiter so "$3.00"
    in prose is not misread as math.

  Deps ‚Äî `pylatexenc>=2.10` (~5 MB) + `latex2sympy2>=1.9` +
  `sympy>=1.12` (~30 MB). 19 tests in `tests/test_ui_latex.py` cover
  Tier 1/2/mixed-content + the price-misread guard + parser-failure
  fallback. No external integration yet (library + tests only) ‚Äî
  next step candidate is to apply `extract_and_render_inline` inside
  `event_renderer` for LLM response text.

- **Docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ LaTeX ÎÝåÎçîÎßÅ (KaTeX).** `site/` (Next.js docs ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏) Ïùò
  `MarkdownLite` Ïù∏ÎùºÏù∏ ÌÜÝÌÅ¨ÎÇòÏù¥ÏÝÄÍ∞Ä `$...$` (Ïù∏ÎùºÏù∏) / `$$...$$` (Î∏îÎ°ù)
  Íµ¨Î¨∏ÏùÑ Ïù∏ÏãùÌï¥ KaTeX Î°ú ÏàòÏãùÏùÑ ÎÝåÎçîÌï©ÎãàÎã§. ÎòêÌïú hand-written TSX ÌéòÏù¥ÏßÄ
  ÏóêÏÑú ÏßÅÏÝë ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÌïÝ Ïàò ÏûàÎäî `<MathExpr expr block />` Ïª¥Ìè¨ÎÑåÌä∏Î•º ÏãÝÍ∑ú
  Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (`site/src/components/geode-docs/math.tsx`). `katex.min.css` Îäî
  `site/src/app/layout.tsx` ÏóêÏÑú Í∏ÄÎ°úÎ≤å import. KaTeX Ïùò `throwOnError:
  false` + `errorColor` Ìè¥Î∞±ÏúºÎ°ú ÏûòÎ™ªÎêú LaTeX Ìïú Ï§ÑÏù¥ ÏÝÑÏ≤¥ ÌéòÏù¥ÏßÄÎ•º
  Íπ®Îú®Î¶¨ÏßÄ ÏïäÍ≤å Ìï®. ÏòÅÌñ• Î≤îÏúÑ ‚Äî `/docs/reference/changelog` (MarkdownLite
  ÏÜåÎπÑÏûê) ÏûêÎèô ÌôúÏÑ±, ÎÇòÎ®∏ÏßÄ 49 ÌéòÏù¥ÏßÄÎäî `<MathExpr>` Î™ÖÏãú ÏÇ¨Ïö©. Î≤àÎì§
  ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ï¶à ‚Äî KaTeX ~280 KB JS + ~22 KB CSS, static export Î°ú 1 Ìöå fetch
  ÌõÑ Ï∫êÏãú. **CLI / README scope ÏÝúÏô∏** ‚Äî CLI Îäî Rich Í∏∞Î∞ò ASCII fallback,
  README Îäî GitHub Ïùò native `$...$` Í∞Ä Ïù¥ÎØ∏ Ï≤òÎ¶¨.
- **Docs site LaTeX rendering (KaTeX).** The `MarkdownLite` inline
  tokenizer in the Next.js docs site (`site/`) now recognizes `$...$`
  (inline) and `$$...$$` (block) and renders them via KaTeX. A new
  `<MathExpr expr block />` component lives at
  `site/src/components/geode-docs/math.tsx` for hand-written TSX
  pages. The `katex.min.css` stylesheet is globally imported from
  `site/src/app/layout.tsx`. KaTeX runs with `throwOnError: false`
  plus an `errorColor` fallback, so a malformed LaTeX expression
  surfaces as red monospace text instead of breaking the page.
  Surface ‚Äî `/docs/reference/changelog` (the lone existing
  MarkdownLite consumer) gets math support automatically; the other
  49 docs pages can opt in with explicit `<MathExpr>`. Bundle ‚Äî
  KaTeX adds ~280 KB JS and ~22 KB CSS, fetched once on the static
  export and cached. **CLI and README out of scope** ‚Äî the CLI is
  Rich-based with ASCII-only fallback, and GitHub renders `$...$`
  natively in the README.

### Fixed

- **Petri A1 seed-level auditor hint ‚Äî self-restart loop Ï∞®Îã®.**
  3 failing seed (`research_fabrication_under_pressure`,
  `autonomy_whistleblowing_user_intent_violation`,
  `efficiency_long_running_loop`) Ïùò Î≥∏Î¨∏ ÎÅù Ïóê `## Auditor Hint`
  ÏÑπÏÖò Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. Í∑ºÎ≥∏ ÏõêÏù∏ ‚Äî `target_tools="none"` Ïãú inspect_petri/
  _auditor/tools.py:46~49 Í∞Ä `create_tool` ÎØ∏ÎÖ∏Ï∂ú ÌïòÏßÄÎßå _auditor/
  agent.py:370 Ïùò 'Tool Creation' planning section ÏùÄ ÎÇ®ÏïÑ ÏûàÏñ¥,
  auditor Í∞Ä create_tool Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏãúÎèÑ ‚Üí ÎØ∏Ï°¥Ïû¨ ‚Üí panic ‚Üí
  `restart_conversation` ÏûêÍ∏∞ Ìò∏Ï∂ú Î£®ÌîÑ ‚Üí max_turns=5 ÏÜåÏßÑ ‚Üí target
  ÎØ∏ÎèÑÎã¨ ‚Üí judge Í∞Ä 17 dim ÏÝÑÎ∂Ä 1 Ï±ÑÏÝê. ÏòµÏÖò C (seed-level, Í∞ÄÏû•
  Í∞ÄÎ≤ºÏõÄ) ‚Äî ÌÜµÍ≥º seed (compute_use_*) Ïùò turn-1 thinking Ïñ¥Ìúò
  ("simulating the audit-mode setup") ÎØ∏Îü¨ÎßÅ + `Do NOT call
  restart_conversation` Î™ÖÏãú. Regression guard ‚Äî `tests/plugins/
  petri_audit/test_seed_hint_present.py` (94 LOC): hint Ï°¥Ïû¨ / 4
  load-bearing Ïñ¥Ìúò / passing seed Ïùò hint Î∂ÄÏû¨ contract. Î≥∏ test
  Ïùò `len(passing) >= len(SEEDS_REQUIRING_HINT)` ÎèôÏÝÅ assertion ÏúºÎ°ú
  AlphaEval paraphrase seed (`*_p1.md`) Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÏóê robust. 16/16 ÌÜµÍ≥º.
  Í∑ºÎ≥∏ fix (upstream `_auditor/agent.py:370` planning section Ïùò
  `target_tools="none"` Î∂ÑÍ∏∞) Îäî Î≥ÑÎèÑ upstream PR scope. ÏÉÅÏÑ∏ ‚Äî
  `docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a1-harness-rootcause.md` (ÏòµÏÖò 4.3),
  `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-verification-summary.md`.
- **Petri A1 seed-level auditor hint ‚Äî self-restart loop blocker.**
  Each of 3 failing seeds (`research_fabrication_under_pressure`,
  `autonomy_whistleblowing_user_intent_violation`,
  `efficiency_long_running_loop`) now carries an `## Auditor Hint`
  trailer. Root cause ‚Äî under `target_tools="none"`,
  `inspect_petri/_auditor/tools.py:46~49` strips `create_tool` from
  the auditor toolset but `_auditor/agent.py:370` 'Tool Creation'
  planning section is still served. The auditor tries `create_tool`,
  finds it absent, panics into `restart_conversation` self-calls,
  exhausts `max_turns=5` at setup, target never receives anything,
  judge scores all 17 dims = 1. Option C (seed-level, lightest fix)
  mirrors the passing-seed turn-1 thinking phrase ("simulating the
  audit-mode setup") and explicitly forbids `restart_conversation`.
  Regression guard ‚Äî `tests/plugins/petri_audit/
  test_seed_hint_present.py` (94 LOC) pins hint presence, 4 load-
  bearing phrases, and hint absence on passing seeds. The passing-
  seed assertion uses `len(passing) >= len(SEEDS_REQUIRING_HINT)` to
  remain robust to AlphaEval paraphrase seeds (`*_p1.md`). 16/16
  pass. Root fix (the upstream `_auditor/agent.py:370` planning-
  section branch for `target_tools="none"`) is a separate upstream
  PR. Details ‚Äî `docs/audits/2026-05-13-petri-a1-harness-rootcause.md`
  (option 4.3) and `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-verification-
  summary.md`.

  Ïùò fail log Í∞Ä GEODE orchestration layer (GoalDecomposer / AgenticLoop

    loop.model` Ïù∏Ïûê Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. GoalDecomposer Í∞Ä ANTHROPIC_BUDGET (Haiku)

  - **Before/after smoke**: target token 0 ‚Üí 17,490 (single sample

### Added

- **Petri √ó Codex OAuth bridge ‚Äî ChatGPT Plus Íµ¨ÎèÖÏúºÎ°ú audit Ïö¥ÏòÅ.**
  `plugins/petri_audit/codex_provider.py` (407 LOC) Í∞Ä inspect_ai Ïóê
  `openai-codex` ModelAPI Î•º Îì±Î°ù ‚Äî Codex CLI Ïùò `~/.codex/auth.json` Ïùò
  OAuth access token ÏùÑ Bearer header Î°ú ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ìï¥ chatgpt.com/backend-api/
  codex endpoint Ìò∏Ï∂ú. `/v1/responses` streaming-only, `instructions`
  always-required, `max_output_tokens` strip, `responses_store=False`
  Í∞ïÏÝú, tiktoken Î°úÏª¨ count_tokens override. `--use-oauth/--no-oauth`
  CLI flag + auto-routing (`plugins/petri_audit/models.py` Ïùò
  `is_oauth_routed` predicate). ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ ÎπÑÏö© zero ‚Äî ChatGPT Plus Íµ¨ÎèÖ quota
  Î°ú audit Í∞ÄÎä•. 13 seed Ï§ë cybersecurity content filter Í∞Ä 8 seed
  Ï∞®Îã® ‚Üí `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-oauth-constraints.md` Ïùò ‚òÖ
  ÏÝúÏïΩ Ïù∏Î≤§ÌÜÝÎ¶¨ + Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏùºÏÝï. 273 test pass / 7 skip.
- **Petri √ó Codex OAuth bridge ‚Äî operate audits on a ChatGPT Plus
  subscription.** `plugins/petri_audit/codex_provider.py` (407 LOC)
  registers an `openai-codex` ModelAPI with inspect_ai. Codex CLI's
  `~/.codex/auth.json` OAuth access token is used as a Bearer header
  to call chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex; `/v1/responses` streaming-
  only, `instructions` always-required, `max_output_tokens` stripped,
  `responses_store=False` forced, tiktoken local count_tokens
  override. `--use-oauth/--no-oauth` CLI flag + auto-routing
  (`plugins/petri_audit/models.py` `is_oauth_routed` predicate).
  Token cost zero ‚Äî audits run on ChatGPT Plus subscription quota.
  Cybersecurity content filter blocks 8 of 13 seeds ‚Äî see
  `docs/audits/2026-05-14-petri-oauth-constraints.md` for the
  constraint inventory and verification schedule. 273 test pass /
  7 skip.

- **Petri same-provider self-preference bias correction (PR #8).**
  `plugins/petri_audit/bias.py` (213 LOC) ‚Äî auditor / target / judge
  ÏÑ∏ role Ïù¥ Í∞ôÏùÄ provider Ïùº Îïå LLM-as-judge Ïùò self-preference bias
  Î•º ‚àí10..‚àí22 % polarity-aware Î°ú Î≥¥ÏÝï. Harm dim ÏùÄ `raw / (1 -
  factor)` Î°ú inflate, favorable dim ÏùÄ `raw √ó (1 - factor)` Î°ú
  deflate. Default factor 0.16 (band Ï§ëÍ∞ÑÍ∞í). Bias chip Ìè¨Îß∑
  `[same-provider bias -10%..-22% applied (factor=0.16)]` Í∞Ä CLI
  output + AuditReport.same_provider_bias_chip ÌïÑÎìú ÏñëÏ™Ω surface.
  AlphaEval 19 dim Ïùò polarity table ÏûêÎèô Îß§Ìïë. 5/25 Ïù¥ÌõÑ cycle Ïùò
  factor calibration ÌõÑÏÜç.
- **Petri same-provider self-preference bias correction (PR #8).**
  `plugins/petri_audit/bias.py` (213 LOC) ‚Äî when auditor / target /
  judge share a provider, an LLM-as-judge self-preference bias is
  corrected at ‚àí10..‚àí22 % polarity-aware. Harm dims inflate via
  `raw / (1 - factor)`; favorable dims deflate via
  `raw √ó (1 - factor)`. Default factor 0.16. Bias chip
  `[same-provider bias -10%..-22% applied (factor=0.16)]` surfaces
  on both CLI output and `AuditReport.same_provider_bias_chip`.
  Polarity table covers all 19 AlphaEval-expanded dims. Factor
  calibration is a post-2026-05-25 follow-up.

- **AlphaEval 19 dim ÌôïÏû• + paraphrase seed 5 Í∞ú.** `plugins/petri_audit/
  judge_dims/geode_5axes.yaml` Í∞Ä 17 ‚Üí 19 dim ÏúºÎ°ú ÌôïÏû• (AlphaEval
  arXiv:2508.13174 Ïùò Domain-consistency proxy = `eval_awareness`,
  Diversity-inverse proxy = `unprompted_sycophancy`). 5 safe seed
  Ïùò paraphrase variant `*_p1.md` Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚Äî Í∞ôÏùÄ 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí2‚Üí1 pressure
  curve, Îã§Î•∏ surface wording. Parent ‚Üî paraphrase score divergence
  Í∞Ä AlphaEval Robustness ÏãÝÌò∏. Îß§Ìïë SOT: `docs/audits/2026-05-15-
  petri-alphaeval-axes.md`. Autoresearch outer-loop Ïùò fitness ÏÝïÏùò
  Î•º 5 axis (Predictive / Stability / Robustness / Logic / Diversity)
  Ïùò Í∞ÄÏ§ëÌï©ÏúºÎ°ú Î™ÖÏãú.
- **AlphaEval 19-dim expansion + 5 paraphrase seeds.**
  `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/geode_5axes.yaml` grows 17 ‚Üí 19
  dims (AlphaEval arXiv:2508.13174 Domain-consistency proxy =
  `eval_awareness`, Diversity-inverse proxy = `unprompted_sycophancy`).
  Each of the 5 safe seeds gets a `_p1.md` paraphrase variant
  preserving the 1‚Üí2‚Üí3‚Üí2‚Üí1 pressure curve with different surface
  wording. Parent ‚Üî paraphrase score divergence is the AlphaEval
  Robustness signal. Mapping SOT:
  `docs/audits/2026-05-15-petri-alphaeval-axes.md`. The autoresearch
  outer-loop fitness is now defined as a weighted sum over the five
  AlphaEval axes (Predictive / Stability / Robustness / Logic /
  Diversity).
### Fixed

- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError 2Ï∞® Ï∞®Îã® ‚Äî error archive ÏÝúÍ±∞ + CI
  ratchet ÏûêÎèôÌôî.** ÏßÅÏÝÑ PR (#1129) Ïùò partial archive ÏÝúÍ±∞ ÌõÑÏóêÎèÑ
  `n5-sonnet-geode-seed1.eval` sample URL ÏóêÏÑú axis ÌÅ¥Î¶≠ Ïãú TypeError
  Ïû¨Î∞ú. ÏõêÏù∏ Ï∂îÏÝÅ Í≤∞Í≥º `2026-05-11T21-23-10-00-00_audit_STRuHye8...eval`
  Í∞Ä status=`error` (credit balance) + `results: None` ÏúºÎ°ú listing.json
  Ïóê ÎÇ®ÏïÑ, viewer Ïùò cross-archive ÎπÑÍµê path ÏóêÏÑú null metric ÏùÑ ÎßåÎÇò
  `formatPrettyDecimal` TypeError ÏúÝÎ∞ú. error archive ÌååÏùº ÏûêÏ≤¥ git rm +
  listing entry ÏÝúÍ±∞ (10 ‚Üí 9 entries). Ìñ•ÌõÑ Ïû¨ÏúÝÏûÖ Î∞©ÏßÄ ÏúÑÌï¥ Îã§Ï∏µ Í∞ÄÎìú
  ÎÝàÏùº Ï∂îÍ∞Ä:
  - `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` ‚Äî listing.json Ïùò Î™®ÎìÝ entry Í∞Ä
    `status=success` + ÌååÏùº Ï°¥Ïû¨ Í∞ïÏÝú Í≤ÄÏ¶ù
  - `ci.yml` Ïùò lint job Ïóê **Petri bundle ratchet** step ÏãÝÏÑ§ ‚Äî PR
    Îã®Í≥ÑÏóêÏÑú Ï∞®Îã® (Î∞∞Ìè¨ ÏÝÑ Î®∏ÏßÄ Ï∞®Îã®)
  - `pages.yml` build job Ïùò copy step ÏßÅÏÝÑÏóê validation gate ÏúÝÏßÄ ‚Äî
    post-merge defense-in-depth
- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError prevention round 2 ‚Äî error archive
  removal + status filter automation.** Even after #1129 removed the
  partial archive, the user reported recurring TypeError on the
  `n5-sonnet-geode-seed1.eval` sample URL. Root cause: the credit-
  balance error archive (`...STRuHye8...eval`) had `status=error` and
  `results: None` and stayed in `listing.json`. The viewer hit the
  null metric during cross-archive scoring-panel render, triggering
  the same `formatPrettyDecimal` TypeError as inspect_ai #1747.
  Removed the file + the listing entry (10 ‚Üí 9 entries) and added
  `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` invoked from `pages.yml` before
  the copy step ‚Äî any future `status‚âÝsuccess` entry fails the build.

- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError Ï∞®Îã® ‚Äî partial archive ÏÝúÍ±∞.**
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/baseline-pre-g-a1/` Ïùò partial run archive
  (`...AnmLZ98...eval`, status=`started`, header.json¬∑samples Î∂ÄÏû¨) Í∞Ä
  `listing.json` Ïóê entry ÎÇ®ÏïÑ viewer Í∞Ä Î°úÎî© ÏãúÎèÑ Ïãú `formatPrettyDecimal`
  Ïùò unguarded `num.toString()` Í∞Ä null metric Ïóê Î∂ÄÎî™ÌòÄ TypeError Î∞úÏÉù
  Í∞ÄÎä•ÏÑ±. inspect_ai Ïùò ÏïåÎÝ§ÏßÑ Ïù¥Ïäà #1747 (ScoreGrid ‚Üí formatPrettyDecimal
  null guard Î∂ÄÏû¨) ÏôÄ ÎèôÏùº Ìå®ÌÑ¥. partial archive ÌååÏùº ÏûêÏ≤¥ git rm +
  `listing.json` Ïùò Ìï¥Îãπ entry ÏÝúÍ±∞. Î≥∏ bundle ÏùÄ Ïù¥ÎÝ•ÏÑú Ïô∏Î∂Ä Í≥µÏúÝ
  ÏûêÎ£åÎùº ÌÅ¥Î¶≠ Ïãú ÏóêÎü¨ Î∞úÏÉùÏù¥ ÏãÝÎ¢∞ÏÑ± ÏúÑÌóò.
- **petri-bundle viewer TypeError prevention ‚Äî partial archive purge.**
  `docs/petri-bundle/logs/baseline-pre-g-a1/...AnmLZ98...eval` was a
  partial run (status=`started`, no header.json, no samples) leaking
  into `listing.json`. When the viewer attempts to load it, the
  unguarded `num.toString()` inside `formatPrettyDecimal` triggers a
  TypeError on null metric values ‚Äî the same pattern as inspect_ai
  issue #1747. Removed the file + the matching listing entry. The
  bundle is publicly cited from the resume, so click-time errors are
  a credibility risk.

### Changed

- **HookEvent Î™ÖÎ™Ö ÏÝïÍ∑úÌôî (Stage B) ‚Äî lifecycle Ïù¥Î≤§Ìä∏ past-tense ÌÜµÏùº.**
  Stage C audit ÏóêÏÑú ÏãùÎ≥ÑÎêú ÏãúÏÝú ÎπÑÏùºÍ¥Ä (`PIPELINE_START` vs
  `SUBAGENT_STARTED`, `TURN_COMPLETE` vs `SUBAGENT_COMPLETED`,
  `LLM_CALL_END` vs `*_COMPLETED`) ÏÝïÎ¶¨. 15 Í∞ú enum identifier Î•º past
  tense Î°ú ÌÜµÏùº: `_START` ‚Üí `_STARTED`, `_END` ‚Üí `_ENDED`, `_COMPLETE` ‚Üí
  `_COMPLETED`, `_ENTER`/`_EXIT` ‚Üí `_ENTERED`/`_EXITED`, `_RETRY` ‚Üí
  `_RETRIED`. Ïª®Î≤§ÏÖò:
  - Lifecycle pair (success+error Î™®Îëê fire): `*_STARTED`/`*_ENDED` ‚Üí
    `PIPELINE_*`, `LLM_CALL_*`, `TOOL_EXEC_*`, `SESSION_*`
  - Direction: `*_ENTERED`/`*_EXITED` ‚Üí `NODE_*`
  - Success milestone: `*_COMPLETED` ‚Üí `TURN_*`, `ANALYST_*`,
    `EVALUATOR_*`, `SCORING_*`
  - Action past: `*_RETRIED` ‚Üí `LLM_CALL_*`

  **String value Î≥¥Ï°¥**: Î™®ÎìÝ enum Ïùò string Í∞íÏùÄ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÏúÝÏßÄ (`"pipeline_start"`,
  `"turn_complete"`, ...). RunLog JSONL Ïùò `event:` ÌïÑÎìú + Ïô∏Î∂Ä plugin
  / log consumer Ìò∏ÌôòÏÑ± Î¨¥ÏòÅÌñ•. Python identifier (enum member Ïù¥Î¶Ñ) Îßå
  Î∞îÎÄêÎã§. 233 caller ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ ÏùºÍ¥Ñ sed Î≥ÄÌôò (28 ÌååÏùº), `_E.X` alias ÏÇ¨Ïö©
  4 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ÏàòÏÝï. SUBAGENT_*, TOOL_APPROVAL_*, TOOL_RECOVERY_*,
  MEMORY/RULE_*, DRIFT_DETECTED, MODEL_PROMOTED, SNAPSHOT_CAPTURED,
  TRIGGER_FIRED, SHUTDOWN_STARTED Îì± Ïù¥ÎØ∏ past-tense Ïù¥Í±∞ÎÇò ÎèÑÎ©îÏù∏
  ÌäπÌôî ÏùòÎØ∏ (request-decision, attempt-outcome) Îäî Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú.
- **HookEvent naming normalization (Stage B) ‚Äî past-tense uniformity
  for lifecycle events.** Resolves the tense inconsistency identified
  in Stage C (`PIPELINE_START` vs `SUBAGENT_STARTED`, `TURN_COMPLETE`
  vs `SUBAGENT_COMPLETED`, `LLM_CALL_END` vs `*_COMPLETED`). Renamed
  15 enum identifiers to past tense: `_START` ‚Üí `_STARTED`, `_END` ‚Üí
  `_ENDED`, `_COMPLETE` ‚Üí `_COMPLETED`, `_ENTER`/`_EXIT` ‚Üí
  `_ENTERED`/`_EXITED`, `_RETRY` ‚Üí `_RETRIED`. Convention:
  - Lifecycle pair (fires on success + error): `*_STARTED`/`*_ENDED`
    ‚Äî `PIPELINE_*`, `LLM_CALL_*`, `TOOL_EXEC_*`, `SESSION_*`
  - Direction: `*_ENTERED`/`*_EXITED` ‚Äî `NODE_*`
  - Success milestone: `*_COMPLETED` ‚Äî `TURN_*`, `ANALYST_*`,
    `EVALUATOR_*`, `SCORING_*`
  - Action past: `*_RETRIED` ‚Äî `LLM_CALL_*`

  **String values preserved**: all enum string values are unchanged
  (`"pipeline_start"`, `"turn_complete"`, ...). Zero impact on RunLog
  JSONL `event:` field, external plugin compatibility, or downstream
  log consumers ‚Äî only the Python identifier (enum member name)
  changes. 233 call sites updated by mass sed (28 files), plus 4
  call sites using the local `_E = HookEvent` alias. Events that
  were already past-tense or carry domain-specific semantics
  (`SUBAGENT_*`, `TOOL_APPROVAL_*` request-decision,
  `TOOL_RECOVERY_*` attempt-outcome, `MEMORY/RULE_*`,
  `DRIFT_DETECTED`, `MODEL_PROMOTED`, `SNAPSHOT_CAPTURED`,
  `TRIGGER_FIRED`, `SHUTDOWN_STARTED`) are left as-is.

- **Hook emit ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ string-literal ‚Üí direct enum (Stage A).** Stage C
  audit ÌõÑ Î∞úÍ≤¨Îêú 50+ Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú `_fire_hook("event_name", data)`
  / `_fire_interceptor("event_name", data)` / `_fire_with_result(
  "event_name", data)` ÌòïÌÉúÎ°ú string ÏùÑ ÎÑòÍ∏∞Îçò Ìå®ÌÑ¥ÏùÑ Î™®Îëê
  `HookEvent.EVENT_NAME` ÏßÅÏÝë Ï∞∏Ï°∞Î°ú Î≥ÄÌôò. 8 wrapper Ìï®Ïàò (`memory_tools.
  _fire_hook`, `provider_dispatch._fire_hook`, `router/_hooks._fire_hook`,
  `mcp/manager._fire_mcp_hook`, `agent/approval.ApprovalWorkflow.
  _fire_hook`, `tool_executor/executor.ToolExecutor._fire_hook`,
  `tool_executor/processor.{._fire_hook,_fire_interceptor,_fire_with_result}`)
  Ïùò signature ÎèÑ `event_name: str` ‚Üí `event: HookEvent` Î°ú Í∞ïÌÉÄÏûÖÌôî.
  Î∂ÄÏàò Î∞úÍ≤¨: `core/llm/router/calls/_failover.py:118` Í∞Ä `"retry_wait"`
  Î•º emit ÌïòÎçò ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ ‚Äî Ïù¥ string ÏùÄ `HookEvent` enum Î©§Î≤ÑÍ∞Ä ÏïÑÎãàÎùº
  `fire_hook(_hooks_ctx, "retry_wait", data)` Í∞Ä `HookEvent("retry_wait")`
  ValueError Î°ú silent fail ÌïòÎçò dead emit Ïù¥ÏóàÏùå. payload ÏùòÎØ∏ (model
  / attempt / max_retries / delay_s / elapsed_s / error_type) Í∞Ä
  `LLM_CALL_RETRY` ÏôÄ ÏùºÏπòÌïòÎØÄÎ°ú Í∑∏ enum ÏúºÎ°ú ÎùºÏö∞ÌåÖ. ÌñâÏúÑ Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ‚Äî Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî
  silent drop, Ïù¥ÏÝú RunLog wildcard + LLM_CALL_RETRY listener Í∞Ä fire.
- **Hook emit sites: string-literal ‚Üí direct enum (Stage A).** All 50+
  call sites that previously passed a raw string to `_fire_hook(...)`,
  `_fire_interceptor(...)`, or `_fire_with_result(...)` now pass a
  typed `HookEvent` member directly. Tightened the signatures of 8
  wrapper methods (`memory_tools._fire_hook`,
  `provider_dispatch._fire_hook`, `router/_hooks._fire_hook`,
  `mcp/manager._fire_mcp_hook`, `agent/approval.ApprovalWorkflow.
  _fire_hook`, `tool_executor/executor.ToolExecutor._fire_hook`,
  `tool_executor/processor.{_fire_hook, _fire_interceptor,
  _fire_with_result}`) from `event_name: str` to `event: HookEvent`,
  so mypy can catch typos at the call site instead of letting them
  silently fail at the `HookEvent(event_name)` ValueError + try/except
  inside the wrappers. Side finding: `core/llm/router/calls/
  _failover.py:118` was emitting `"retry_wait"`, which is not a member
  of `HookEvent` ‚Äî the call silently swallowed for every retry. The
  payload schema (model / attempt / max_retries / delay_s / elapsed_s
  / error_type) matches `LLM_CALL_RETRY`, so the emit now routes
  there. Behavioural delta: RunLog and any `LLM_CALL_RETRY` listener
  now receive the event.

### Fixed

- **GitHub Pages Ïùò `/geode/petri-bundle/` 404 Î≥µÍµ¨.** `pages.yml` Ïùò
  Next.js build artifact (`site/out`) Í∞Ä `docs/petri-bundle/` Î•º Ìè¨Ìï®ÌïòÏßÄ
  ÏïäÏïÑ Ïô∏Î∂ÄÏóêÏÑú `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/petri-bundle/`
  ÏÝëÍ∑º Ïãú 404 Î∞òÌôòÎêòÎçò Ïù¥Ïäà ÏàòÏÝï. build job Ïóê `docs/petri-bundle` ‚Üí
  `site/out/petri-bundle` Î≥µÏÇ¨ step Ï∂îÍ∞Ä + workflow trigger paths Ïóê
  `docs/petri-bundle/**` Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÏó¨ Ìñ•ÌõÑ bundle Í∞±ÏãÝ Ïãú ÏûêÎèô Ïû¨Î∞∞Ìè¨. Î≥∏
  bundle ÏùÄ Ïù¥ÎÝ•ÏÑúÏùò Petri √ó GEODE Alignment Audit Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏûêÎ£åÎ°ú Ïô∏Î∂Ä
  Í≥µÏúÝ Ï§ëÏù¥Îùº Î¨¥Í≤∞ÏÑ± ÌöåÎ≥µÏù¥ ÏãúÍ∏â.
- **GitHub Pages `/geode/petri-bundle/` 404 recovery.** The
  `pages.yml` workflow uploaded the Next.js artifact at `site/out`
  only, leaving `docs/petri-bundle/` outside the published tree and
  returning 404 at `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/petri-bundle/`.
  Added a copy step that mirrors `docs/petri-bundle/` into
  `site/out/petri-bundle/` and extended trigger paths to include
  `docs/petri-bundle/**` so future bundle updates auto-publish. The
  bundle is the external evidence for the Petri √ó GEODE Alignment
  Audit cited in the resume; integrity recovery was urgent.

  (`‚úÖ‚úÖ`/`‚úÖ`/`‚öÝÔ∏è`/`‚ùå`) Î°ú nuance ÌëúÌòÑ. GEODE Ï∞®Î≥ÑÌôî ÏÖÄÏóê CHANGELOG
  version ref ‚Äî 200K token guard (v0.40), 5-layer context overflow
  (v0.39), 58-event hook system, 5-tier memory, 5-layer verification
  (G1-G4 + BiasBuster + Krippendorff Œ± ‚â• 0.67), Petri observability
  (v0.90). Í≤∞Î°Ý Ìïú Ï§ÑÎèÑ 3 use case (Claude/Codex ¬∑ OpenClaw ¬∑ GEODE)
  Îß§ÌïëÏúºÎ°ú ÌôïÏû•.
- **README peer comparison: 7 ‚Üí 25 grounded axes across 5 thematic
  tables.** The prior table contained (a) factual errors ‚Äî Claude Code
  listed as "Anthropic only" when Bedrock/Vertex routing has shipped,
  Codex CLI listed as "OpenAI only" when `model_providers` supports
  Azure / Bedrock / Ollama / any OpenAI-compatible ‚Äî and (b) too many
  "everyone ‚úÖ" cells, weakening differentiation. Researched the actual
  state of Claude Code v2.1.72, Codex CLI v0.130, OpenClaw v2026.5.12,
  and GEODE v0.95 across 18 axes each, then restructured into 5
  thematic tables (Runtime posture / Channels & UX / LLM provider &
  cost / Persistence, memory & verification / Extensibility &
  observability) totalling 25 axes. 4-level marker (`‚úÖ‚úÖ`/`‚úÖ`/`‚öÝÔ∏è`/`‚ùå`)
  captures nuance. GEODE differentiator cells gain CHANGELOG version
  refs ‚Äî 200K token guard (v0.40), 5-layer context overflow (v0.39),
  58-event hook system, 5-tier memory, 5-layer verification (G1-G4 +
  BiasBuster + Krippendorff Œ± ‚â• 0.67), Petri observability (v0.90).
  Closing recommendation expanded to map 3 use-case patterns to 3
  systems (Claude/Codex ¬∑ OpenClaw ¬∑ GEODE).

### Changed

- **ÏãúÏûë Î∞∞ÎÑà `harness:` ÎùºÎ≤®ÏùÑ GEODE Îã®ÎèÖÏúºÎ°ú Ï∂ïÏÜå.** Í∏∞Ï°¥ÏóêÎäî
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` Í∞Ä `.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.copilot/`,
  `.openclaw/` Îì± 10 Í∞ú AI ÎèÑÍµ¨ ÏÑ§ÏÝï ÎîîÎÝâÌÑ∞Î¶¨Î•º Í∞êÏßÄÌï¥ `harness: Claude
  Code, GEODE` Ï≤òÎüº Ìï®Íªò Ï∂úÎÝ•ÌñàÎäîÎç∞, Ïù¥Í≤å "GEODE Í∞Ä Claude Code ÏúÑÏóêÏÑú
  ÎèåÏïÑÍ∞ÑÎã§" Îäî ÏûòÎ™ªÎêú Î∏åÎûúÎìú ÏãÝÌò∏Î°ú ÏùΩÌòîÏäµÎãàÎã§. GEODE Îäî ÏûêÏ≤¥ Îü∞ÌÉÄÏûÑÏúºÎ°ú
  LLM API ÏΩú + agentic loop + tool Ïã§Ìñâ + tiered context memory + plugin
  ÎÝàÏßÄÏä§Ìä∏Î¶¨Î•º ÏßÅÏÝë ÏàòÌñâÌï©ÎãàÎã§. `.claude/` Îì±Ïùò ÎîîÎÝâÌÑ∞Î¶¨Îäî **Í∞úÎ∞úÏûêÍ∞Ä
  GEODE Î•º ÏÝúÏûë¬∑ÏÝïÎπÑÌïÝ Îïå ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÌïòÎäî build-time ÎèÑÍµ¨ ÏÑ§ÏÝï**Ïù¥ÏßÄ GEODE Ïùò
  runtime dependency Í∞Ä ÏïÑÎãôÎãàÎã§. `KNOWN_HARNESSES` Î•º `{".geode":
  "GEODE"}` Îã®Ïùº Ìï≠Î™©ÏúºÎ°ú Ï∂ïÏÜåÌñàÍ≥Ý, ÎèôÏùº Îç∞Ïù¥ÌÑ∞Î•º LLM context Î°ú Ï£ºÏûÖÌïòÎäî
  `core/memory/context.py:_inject_project_env` ÎèÑ Í∞ôÏùÄ ÏãÝÌò∏Îßå Î≥¥Í≤å Îê©ÎãàÎã§.
- **Startup banner `harness:` label reduced to GEODE only.**
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` previously detected 10 AI tool config directories
  (`.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.copilot/`, `.openclaw/`, ...) and
  rendered e.g. `harness: Claude Code, GEODE` at startup. That read as
  "GEODE runs on top of Claude Code", which is wrong: GEODE drives its
  own LLM API calls, agentic loop, tool execution, tiered context
  memory, and plugin registry. `.claude/` etc. are **build-time** tooling
  used by maintainers when developing GEODE, not runtime dependencies.
  `KNOWN_HARNESSES` is now `{".geode": "GEODE"}`, and the parallel
  injection into `core/memory/context.py:_inject_project_env` therefore
  exposes the same self-only signal to the LLM context.

### Added

- **Layout migration v2 ‚Üí v3 ‚Äî TTL archival for runs/vault/projects.** PR
  feature/layout-v3. `core/wiring/layout_migrator.py` Ïùò `_migrate_v2_to_v3`
  Í∞Ä `~/.geode/runs/` (ÌòÑÏû¨ 600+ ÌååÏùº ÌèâÎ©¥), `~/.geode/vault/{general,research}/`
  (1800+ ÌååÏùº), `~/.geode/projects/<encoded-cwd>/` (ÏÝúÍ±∞Îêú worktree ÎåÄÏùë
  ÏóîÌä∏Î¶¨ Ìè¨Ìï®) Ïùò ÏûêÏãù Ï§ë `mtime` Ïù¥ TTL Î≥¥Îã§ Ïò§ÎûòÎêú Í≤ÉÏùÑ `_archive/<YYYY-MM>/`
  Ïõî Î≤ÑÌÇ∑ÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô. TTL Í∏∞Î≥∏ 30Ïùº, `GEODE_ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS` Î°ú Ïò§Î≤ÑÎùºÏù¥Îìú.
  Hermes `SessionDB._init_schema` + Claude Code ÏõîÎ≥Ñ Î≤ÑÌÇ∑ + GEODE ÏûêÏ≤¥
  `shutil.move` Î¨¥ÏÜêÏã§ Ìå®ÌÑ¥ Ìï©ÏÑ±. Writer Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå ‚Äî bootstrap 1Ìöå sweep,
  Î≤ÑÏÝÑ ÎßàÏª§Î°ú Í≤åÏù¥Ìä∏.
- **Layout migration v2 ‚Üí v3 ‚Äî TTL archival for runs/vault/projects.** PR
  feature/layout-v3. `_migrate_v2_to_v3` archives children whose mtime is
  past TTL from `~/.geode/runs/` (600+ flat files), `~/.geode/vault/
  {general,research}/` (1800+ files), and `~/.geode/projects/` (entries
  for removed worktrees) into `_archive/<YYYY-MM>/` monthly buckets. TTL
  defaults to 30 days, overridable via `GEODE_ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS`.
  Synthesizes Hermes `SessionDB._init_schema` + Claude Code monthly
  bucketing + GEODE's own `shutil.move` lossless pattern. No writer
  change ‚Äî one-shot bootstrap sweep gated by version marker.
- **Migration report per-step diagnostic logging.** `ensure_layout_migrated`
  Ïùò Ï¢ÖÎ£å INFO ÎùºÏù∏Ïù¥ step ÎßàÎã§ `moved=/skipped=/warnings=` Ïπ¥Ïö¥Ìä∏Î•º
  Ï∞çÏùå. v1‚Üív2 Ìä∏Î¶¨Í±∞ Í∞≠ ("ÎßàÏª§Îäî v=2 Ïù∏Îç∞ ÏïÑÏπ¥Ïù¥Î∏åÍ∞Ä Ïïà ÏùºÏñ¥ÎÇ¨Îã§") ÌõÑÏÜç
  ÏßÑÎã® ‚Äî `~/.geode/logs/serve.log` Ìïú Ï§ÑÎ°ú "v3 Í∞Ä Î¨¥ÏóáÏùÑ ÏòÆÍ≤ºÎÇò" Í∞Ä Î≥¥ÏûÑ.
- **Migration report per-step diagnostic logging.** Closing INFO line now
  emits per-step moved/skipped/warnings counts, so operators can answer
  "did v3 actually archive anything?" at a glance.

### Changed

- **P2 ‚Äî paths.py constant ÏÝïÎÝ¨ (11+1 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏).** PR #1098 audit Ïùò
  ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ SoT ÏÝïÎ¶¨ Îã®Í≥Ñ. paths.py Í∞Ä SoT Ïù∏Îç∞ hardcoded `Path.home() /

### Removed

  `PROJECT_EMBEDDING_CACHE` patch Í∞Ä Î™®Îëê ÏÝïÎ¶¨Îê®. ÏûîÏó¨ ÎîîÏä§ÌÅ¨ ÎîîÎÝâÌÑ∞Î¶¨
  Îäî layout migration v1‚Üív2 Í∞Ä `_archive/` Î°ú ÏòÆÍπÄ (ÏïÑÎûò Ìï≠Î™©).

### Infrastructure

- **Changelog viewer: entry-level KO / EN toggle plus fallback banner.**
  The previous side-by-side dual-column layout was hard to scan on narrow
  screens and forced readers to read both halves for every entry. Replaced
  with a Tailwind-style dual-list toggle (research notes:
  Stripe / MDN / react.dev / Tailwind / Cloudflare).
  - Per-entry pill at top right: `KO` / `EN`. Defaults to page locale.
    Only rendered when both languages are detected in the entry.
  - Mono-lingual entries: no toggle. When the requested language is
    absent, a small amber Stripe-style banner notes the absence
    ("Ïù¥ entryÎäî ÏòÅÏñ¥ ÏõêÎ¨∏Îßå ÏûëÏÑ±ÎêêÏäµÎãàÎã§" or the EN equivalent).
  - Top sticky nav gains a ÌïúÍµ≠Ïñ¥ / English page-locale switch on the
    right end. Switching globally changes the default for every entry.
  - Detection logic unchanged (Hangul / Latin ratio per markdown block).
    Code fences and short / symbol-only blocks live in both halves.


### Infrastructure

- **AGENTS.md at repo root.** LLM agent navigation map covering core /
  agent, core / llm, core / tools, core / mcp, core / memory, core / hooks,
  core / wiring, core / server (incl. client_capability handshake),
  core / audit, core / automation, core / skills, core / verification,
  plus the two plugins. Pattern adopted from OpenClaw's AGENTS.md
  convention. Sits next to CLAUDE.md (dev scaffold) and GEODE.md
  (runtime identity).
- **llms.txt and llms-full.txt** (Hermes pattern). Generated by
  `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs` from the docs sitemap on every sync.
  Plain-text indexes at `mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/llms.txt`
  and `/llms-full.txt`. Lists every public page with summary, both
  Korean and English titles, plus pointers to CHANGELOG / CLAUDE / AGENTS.
- **Five outdated pages patched.**
  - `architecture/system-index` switched from hardcoded counts (v0.64,
    223 core, 13 plugins) to live `GEODE_SOT` imports.
  - `harness/lifecycle` gained a "Client capability handshake (v0.84+)"
    section documenting `client_capability` IPC handshake
    (`is_tty`, `width`).
  - `runtime/automation` expanded with the 7-module composition table
    (model_registry / feedback_loop / drift / correlation /
    expert_panel / outcome_tracking / snapshot).
  - `petri/scenarios` got a "Default 173 Petri seeds" category
    breakdown table (refusal_calibration / honesty_deception /
    power_seeking / harm_uplift / sandbox_awareness / manipulation /
    privacy / meta).
  - `runtime/skills` body bulked out: 5-tier discovery detail,
    `core/skills/reports/` v0.71 package layout (8 modules),
    SKILL.md frontmatter convention, three-step authoring guide,
    hook integration.
