### Added

- **Wanted.co.kr Í∏∞Î∞ò ÌïúÍµ≠ job Í≤ÄÏÉâ ÎèÑÍµ¨ (`wanted_jobs_search`).** LinkedIn
  Ïùò PerimeterX/Cloudflare bot detection ÏúºÎ°ú `search_jobs` MCP Í∞Ä Îß§Î≤à
  403 + empty body Î°ú Ï∞®Îã®ÎêòÎäî ÏÉÅÌô©Ïóê ÎåÄÌïú ÎåÄÏ≤¥ Í≤ΩÎ°ú. Wanted Ïùò Í≥µÍ∞ú REST
  endpoint (`/api/v4/jobs`) Î•º httpx Î°ú ÏßÅÏÝë Ìò∏Ï∂úÌï¥ OAuth/proxy/scraper
  ÎØ∏ÎîîÏñ¥ ÏùòÏ°¥ÏÑ± ÏóÜÏù¥ ÌïúÍµ≠ tech job ÏùÑ Í≤ÄÏÉâ. Í≤∞Í≥ºÎäî ÌèâÌÉÑÌïú dict Î¶¨Ïä§Ìä∏
  `{job_id, position, company, location, url, posted_at}`. MCP server Í∞Ä
  ÏïÑÎãàÎùº GEODE ÎÇ¥Ïû• ÎèÑÍµ¨ ‚Äî Î≥ÑÎèÑ subprocess ÏóÜÏùå. `SAFE_TOOLS` Ïóê Îì±Î°ùÎêòÏñ¥
  sub-agent / read-only ÏÝïÏ±Ö path ÏóêÏÑú auto-approve. tool count 24‚Üí25.
  ÎÝàÌçºÎü∞Ïä§: Manus / Devin Ïùò paid scraping provider fallback Ìå®ÌÑ¥Í≥ºÎäî
  Î∞òÎåÄÎ°ú ‚Äî Ï∞®Îã®ÎêòÎäî source Î•º Î∞îÍæ∏Îäî lightweight Î∞©Ìñ•.
- **`run_bash` Ïùò read-only pipeline auto-approve.** Í∏∞Ï°¥ `is_bash_auto_approved`
  Í∞Ä pipe (`|`) ÏûêÏ≤¥Î•º Î¨¥Ï°∞Í±¥ unsafe Î°ú ÌåêÏÝïÌï¥ `find ~/x -type f | sed 's/‚Ä¶/‚Ä¶/'
  | head -200` Í∞ôÏùÄ ÌëúÏ§Ä read-only Ï≤¥Ïù∏Ïù¥ Îß§Î≤à HITL approval ÏöîÍµ¨. Ïù¥ÏÝú
  `SAFE_BASH_PIPELINE_STAGES` (head/tail/wc/sort/uniq/cut/tr/grep/rg/cat/
  less/more/sed/awk/jq/yq/column/fold/nl) Î•º Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌï¥ ‚Äî Ï≤´ stage Í∞Ä Í∏∞Ï°¥
  `SAFE_BASH_PREFIXES` Îß§Ïπò + Ïù¥ÌõÑ stage Îì§Ïù¥ Î™®Îëê pipeline-safe Î©¥ ÌÜµÍ≥º.
  `tee` Îäî by-design write Îùº Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ ÏÝúÏô∏. `sed -i` / `--in-place` ÎèÑ Î≥ÑÎèÑ
  reject. ÏúÑ Ïô∏ ‚Äî `>`, `>>`, `;`, `&`, backtick, `$(...)`, `<(...)`, `>(...)`
  Îäî Ïó¨ÏÝÑÌûà hard reject. ÏÝïÏÝÅ helper `core.agent.safety.is_bash_command_read_only`
  Î°ú Ï∂îÏ∂ú ‚Äî `ApprovalController` ÏôÄ ÌÖåÏä§Ìä∏Í∞Ä Í∞ôÏùÄ Ìï®Ïàò Ìò∏Ï∂úÌï¥ drift Î∞©ÏßÄ.
  ÎÝàÌçºÎü∞Ïä§: `claude-code` settings.json Ïùò `permissions.allow:
  ["Bash(find:*)", ‚Ä¶]` per-command Í∏ÄÎ°úÎ∏å + Codex CLI sandbox Ïùò read-only
  stream filter ÏÝïÏ±Ö. ÌöåÍ∑Ä ‚Äî `tests/test_bash_safe_prefix.py` 35 cases (12
  ÏãÝÍ∑ú pipeline + sed -i / process subst / background / empty stage).

### Infrastructure

- **Docs CHANGELOG full sync plus three outdated patches.**
  - `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs` now parses CHANGELOG.md and emits
    `site/src/data/geode/changelog.ts` with every version entry
    (134 releases plus the current Unreleased). The Changelog page reads
    that file and renders every entry, replacing the prior selected-
    highlights manual list.
  - New minimal markdown renderer at
    `site/src/components/geode-docs/markdown-lite.tsx`. Handles the
    subset CHANGELOG entries use: h3 headings, nested bullets, bold,
    inline code, links, fenced code blocks.
  - `/docs/reference/changelog` page rebuilt to render the full list with
    an in-page version index (grid of anchored versions), one section per
    entry, and a footer note pointing back to the repo CHANGELOG.md as
    the authoritative source.
  - Three outdated pages patched.
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/prompt-system`: summary now mentions the v0.93
      XML envelope (16 markers across 9 files).
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/prompt-caching`: boundary-marker section adds
      the `<dynamic_context>` XML wrapper and the audit-mode strip link.
    - `/docs/ops/cost`: full usage-ledger schema (CallDiagnostic-aligned
      fields), v0.90 token-tracker dual-record fix, three jq one-liners,
      provider downgrade chains updated to GLM 5.x and OpenAI 5.x.


### Fixed

- **OAuth Press-[Enter] prompt ‚Äî direct-render wiring parity.** PR #1077
  added the Press-[Enter] watcher only to the IPC render path
  (`EventRenderer._handle_oauth_login_started`). The thin-CLI direct path
  in `core/ui/agentic_ui/events.py:emit_oauth_login_started` (used by
  `RunLocation.THIN` `/login oauth openai`) still rendered the legacy
  layout without the prompt, so users on the THIN path didn't see
  "Press [Enter]" and the browser-watcher never spawned. Extracted the
  watcher to `core/ui/oauth_browser.start_oauth_browser_watcher`; both
  the IPC handler and the direct fallback now call it. Regression:
  `tests/test_oauth_browser.py::TestEmitOauthLoginStartedFallback` (2
  cases ‚Äî fallback prints prompt + spawns watcher; IPC path skips
  fallback) + `TestStartOauthBrowserWatcher` (2 cases ‚Äî TTY gating).



- **Sandbox tilde expansion (`~` / `~/`) ‚Äî silent-fail bug.**
  `core/tools/sandbox.py:validate_path()` advertised that bare `~` and `~/`
  were "expanded by Python, not shell" (`check_shell_expansion` allowed
  them through), but the actual `os.path.expanduser()` call was missing.
  Result: paths like `~/workspace/resume/common` were joined against the
  project root verbatim, producing `/<project>/~/workspace/...` and a
  misleading `Not a directory` error instead of the proper
  `expanded-path-outside-sandbox` permission error. Surfaced by `geode
  serve` when an agent attempted `glob_files(path="~/workspace/...")`.
  Fix: call `os.path.expanduser(path_str)` immediately before `Path()`
  construction in `validate_path()`, and `path.expanduser()` before
  `.resolve()` in `add_working_directory` / `remove_working_directory`.
  Regression: `tests/test_sandbox.py::TestTildeExpansion` (4 cases ‚Äî
  bare `~`, `~/`, sandbox-expanded resolution, working-dir registration).
- **OpenAI OAuth plan tier auto-reconcile.** `chatgpt_plan_type` was
  extracted from the JWT once during `/login` and frozen into
  `Plan.subscription_tier`. If the user upgraded their ChatGPT plan
  (Plus ‚Üí Pro/Max/etc.) between logins, the stored tier kept showing
  the old value while the JWT itself had the new claim. `load_auth_toml`
  now calls `reconcile_plan_tier_from_stored_jwt()` after hydration,
  which re-decodes the access_token's claims and updates
  `Plan.subscription_tier` + profile metadata in place when a drift is
  found. Drift events log at INFO so the change is visible without
  forcing a re-login (a fresh `/login` still produces a brand-new JWT
  with the current tier ‚Äî this is the in-between fix).
- **OpenAI OAuth ‚Äî JWT decode DRY.** Extracted the inline base64+JSON
  decode block in `login_openai()` into a reusable `_decode_jwt_claims`
  helper; `_plan_type_from_token` builds on top so the reconciliation
  path and login path can't drift apart.

### Added

- **OAuth login UX ‚Äî press Enter to open the verification URL.**
  `EventRenderer._handle_oauth_login_started` now prints
  `Press [Enter] to open the URL in your browser` and spawns a daemon
  thread that calls `webbrowser.open(verification_uri)` on the first
  stdin line. Skipped automatically when stdin is not a TTY (piped
  invocations stay quiet). Pattern grounded from Claude Code's OAuth
  start prompt (`claude-code/src/auth/providers.ts`).

### Infrastructure

- **Docs restructure (F-series) plus Context system page.**
  - **Chapter rename**. "LLM ÌååÏù¥ÌîÑÎùºÏù∏" to "Runtime". "Ïôú Ïù¥ÎÝáÍ≤å" to "Why".
  - **Petri folded into Verification**. The five Petri pages (overview /
    scenarios / run / judge-dimensions / bundle) now live under chapter
    06 Verification next to guardrails, biasbuster, and observability.
  - **LangSmith page deleted**. The deprecation notice page is now gone
    entirely. References to LangSmith were already replaced by the
    native four-lens stack in v0.89.0.
  - **Observability moved and expanded**. From `/docs/ops/observability`
    to `/docs/verification/observability`. Body expanded with per-lens
    structure: hook 14-category table, RunLog JSONL schema, audit
    diagnostics CallDiagnostic dataclass, Petri integration, usage
    ledger, OpenTelemetry / inspect viewer adapters, why-LangSmith-was-
    removed rationale.
  - **Context System page new**. `/docs/runtime/context`. Single
    reference for the four mechanisms: 5-tier memory hierarchy (integer
    tiers 0 to 4, no fractional tiers), 5-layer prompt assembly with
    `AssembledPrompt` dataclass, 200K absolute token guard plus 80 / 95
    percent phases plus 25K MCP cap, Clean Context anchoring prevention,
    cache interface, comparison with Claude Code / LangChain / LlamaIndex.
  - **Fractional tier numbers retired**. `Automation (L4.5)` renamed to
    `Automation` (or `Automation Sidecar`); body now describes it as a
    sidecar between L2 Runtime and L4 Agent rather than as a tier.
    `4.5-Tier`, `Memory(4.5T)`, and a `Tier 0.5` reference in portfolio
    components likewise integerized.
  - **Changelog page synced**. Highlight reel now spans v0.65 through
    v0.95 (Anthropic agentic_call streaming, GLM 202_752 correction,
    OpenAI HTML guard, GEODE_PERSONA, XML sandwich, 4-layer observability,
    Petri scenarios, auto-escalation removal, LangSmith removal,
    cold-start arc, lifecycle to wiring rename, geode audit CLI).
  - Net: 50 pages, 11 chapters (down from 12). All bilingual.


### Infrastructure

- **Docs sync to v0.95 codebase facts plus Petri scenarios page.**
  Code-vs-docs GAP audit against CHANGELOG v0.66 to v0.95. Outcome: 14
  missing entries identified, 16 outdated pages, 7 recommended. Top
  priorities folded in.
  - **`solo` explanation page removed** per author request.
  - **Two new pages.**
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/system-prompt-modes` documents the
      `GEODE_PERSONA` opt-in and `audit-mode` strip introduced in v0.92
      to v0.93.
    - `/docs/petri/scenarios` documents the 13 GEODE-specific Petri
      seeds across 7 categories (autonomy, calibration, compute_use,
      efficiency, exploratory, reasoning, research) and the
      `inspect_petri` v3 default 173 seeds. Pressure curve (1 to 3 to 1)
      and frontmatter convention included.
  - **Outdated facts patched across nine pages.**
    - `/docs` landing: hardcoded "v0.65.0, 223+13, 4380 tests" replaced
      with live `GEODE_SOT` import.
    - `/docs/runtime/tools/protocol`: 57 tools to 61 (6 always-loaded
      plus 55 deferred).
    - `/docs/harness/hooks`: "12 categories" to "14 categories".
    - `/docs/harness/lifecycle` and `/docs/architecture/system-index`:
      `core/lifecycle/` to `core/wiring/` (v0.87.0 rename). Cold-start
      section added (v0.85 to v0.89 SDK lazy arc, minus 258ms, minus
      86 percent warm).
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/langsmith`: page repurposed as a deprecation
      notice. LangSmith dependency was removed in v0.89.0; replacement
      path links to hooks plus RunLog plus Petri.
    - `/docs/runtime/llm/providers`: GLM family updated to 5 models
      (5.1 / 5 / 5-turbo / 4.7 / 4.7-flash) with the 202_752 token
      context. Added a "v0.94+ guards" section: HTML data-URL guard,
      `tool_choice` normalization, `prompt_cache_key` derivation, GLM
      thinking gate, Anthropic `agentic_call` streaming.
    - `/docs/architecture/agentic-loop`: auto-escalation removed
      (v0.90.0). Explicit `model_action_required` and
      `user_clarification_needed` termination signals documented.
    - `/docs/ops/observability`: third lens to four-lens stack. Added
      `core.audit.diagnostics` per-call assertion lens (v0.92.0). Cost
      ledger at `~/.geode/usage/*.jsonl` documented.
    - `/docs/petri/run`: primary command flipped to `geode audit` Typer
      CLI with `--dim-set`, `--seed-select`, `--target-tools`,
      `--unrestricted`. `inspect eval` retained as the raw alternative.
    - `/docs/petri/judge-dimensions`: default `geode_5axes` 17 dim
      noted alongside the full 38 dim catalog.
  - Net: 49 to 50 pages (one removed, two added). All bilingual, all
    carry Di√°taxis quadrant chips.


### Infrastructure

- **Docs restructure (v0.95.0+) ‚Äî 12 chapters, 49 pages, References chapter
  added.** Follow-up sprint to the docs rebuild. Three corrections based on
  live review.
  - **03 Build chapter removed.** The 4 pages (`build/add-tool`,
    `build/add-domain`, `build/add-hook`, `build/testing`) returned 404 on
    the deployed site even though the previous build log advertised them.
    Cause: files were authored in a removed worktree and never staged into
    the merged commit. User decision was to drop the chapter rather than
    re-add it.
  - **02 System Reference (24 pages) split into 7 focused chapters.** New
    sections: 02 Architecture (3) ¬∑ 03 LLM Pipeline (5) ¬∑ 04 Tools and
    Memory (3) ¬∑ 05 Harness (5) ¬∑ 06 Capabilities (3) ¬∑ 07 Verification
    (2) ¬∑ 08 Plugins (2).
  - **Vault page removed** (`runtime/memory/vault`). Vault is wiki-side
    content; only the 5-Tier Context page remains under docs.
  - **References chapter expanded** with a new `reference/external-references`
    page citing frontier agent systems (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenClaw,
    Hermes, Karpathy autoresearch + LLM Wiki, Cursor, Aider, Devin,
    LangGraph), docs design standards (Di√°taxis 4-quadrant, Anthropic
    Platform Docs, OpenClaw AGENTS.md, Hermes llms.txt), Petri/inspect_ai
    sources, and internal assets in mango-wiki and resume/common (incl.
    Karpathy autoresearch 5 reusable patterns).
  - Net: 53 ‚Üí 49 pages, 8 ‚Üí 12 chapters. All bilingual (`<Bi ko en />`),
    all carry Di√°taxis quadrant chips.

- **Docs site rebuild ‚Äî Di√°taxis 8 chapters, 53 bilingual pages.**
  Site/docs restructured into 8 numbered chapters following Di√°taxis (Tutorial,
  How-to, Reference, Explanation). Every page declares its quadrant via a
  color chip in the title and sidebar.
  - Chapters: 00 Welcome (3), 01 Run GEODE (7 how-to), 02 System Reference
    (24), 03 Build on GEODE (4 how-to), 04 Operations (4 how-to),
    05 Petri Audit (4, new), 06 Explanation (4 why-pages, new), 99 Reference (3).
  - **53 of 53 pages are now bilingual** via `<Bi ko en />`. Previous state:
    only 3 of 28 pages had Korean translations; 25 EN-only pages were
    converted in this PR.
  - New chapter: **05 Petri √ó GEODE** with overview, run guide,
    38 judge-dimension reference, and a viewer link to
    `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/petri-bundle/` (external bundle
    published separately).
  - Master neologism page chain: `/docs/explanation/self-hosting`,
    `/explanation/ratchet`, `/explanation/4-layer`, `/explanation/solo`
    grounds every chapter that references "self-hosting agent harness".
  - DocsShell footer now imports `GEODE_SOT` (version, modules, tests,
    syncedAt) instead of hardcoding v0.65.0. GitHub link points at
    `mangowhoiscloud/geode` (was `/portfolio`).
  - Tracking artifact: `site/DOCS-PROGRESS.md` is the per-page status table.
  - Reference patterns: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) `llms.txt` index pattern,
    OpenClaw `AGENTS.md` code-root navigation, Di√°taxis 4-quadrant framework,
    Anthropic Platform Docs comparison-table-at-decision-point pattern.

- **Public site at `site/` ‚Äî GEODE Pages deploy.** Next.js 16 static
  export integrated into the repo at `site/`. Builds and deploys to
  GitHub Pages at `https://mangowhoiscloud.github.io/geode/` on push to
  `main`.
  - Routes: `/portfolio` (case-study narrative), `/docs` (28 reference
    pages ‚Äî architecture, runtime ¬∑ LLM / tools / memory, harness,
    verification, plugins, reference), `/about`, `/works/reode`.
  - Single source of truth: `site/src/data/geode/sot.ts` ‚Äî auto-synced
    from this repo's `pyproject.toml` + `core/` + `plugins/` filesystem
    counts + `CHANGELOG.md` release count via
    `site/scripts/sync-stats.mjs`. The site cannot advertise a version
    different from what `pyproject.toml` claims.
  - Workflow: `.github/workflows/pages.yml` (Next.js build ‚Üí Pages
    artifact ‚Üí official `actions/deploy-pages@v4`). Path-filtered to
    `site/**`, so Python source changes do not redeploy. One-time
    activation: repo Settings ‚Üí Pages ‚Üí Source = "GitHub Actions".
  - Sourced from the standalone `portfolio` repo; route-restructured to
    place GEODE's public surfaces under one tree (basePath `/portfolio`
    ‚Üí `/geode`, `/geode` page ‚Üí `/portfolio`, `/geode/docs` ‚Üí `/docs`,
    root landing ‚Üí `/about`, `/reode` ‚Üí `/works/reode`).
  - Master neologism locked: **self-hosting agent harness** (analogue
    of self-hosting compiler ‚Äî the runtime and the build line share the
    same primitives). Defined once at `/geode/portfolio` after the
    hero; every chapter below refers without redefining.

## [0.95.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **GLM context window precision ‚Äî GAP-X1.** `MODEL_CONTEXT_WINDOW`
  rounded all five registered GLM models (`glm-5.1`, `glm-5`,
  `glm-5-turbo`, `glm-4.7`, `glm-4.7-flash`) to a flat `200_000`-token
  guard. Re-verification against z.ai docs + openrouter listings (2026-05-12)
  yields the precise value `202_752` ‚Äî a +2_752-token delta that the
  post-call 200K guard was tripping early. Cloudflare / LM Studio
  deployments expose smaller windows (`131_072` / `128k`) but GEODE calls
  z.ai directly so the upstream contract applies. Regression test:
  `tests/test_glm_context_window.py` (6 cases ‚Äî per-model assertion +
  family-shared-window invariant). `tests/test_context_monitor.py` fixture
  for the "200K models skip ceiling" case switched from `glm-5` to
  `claude-opus-4-5` (exact 200_000) ‚Äî `glm-5` is no longer exactly 200K.

### Changed

- **Anthropic agentic_call streaming ‚Äî GAP-S1.**
  `ClaudeAgenticAdapter.agentic_call._do_call` now wraps the request in
  `async with self._client.messages.stream(**create_kwargs) as s:` and
  returns `await s.get_final_message()` instead of the previous
  non-streaming `await self._client.messages.create(**create_kwargs)`.
  The final message is the same `anthropic.types.Message` schema, so
  `normalize_anthropic` and the token-tracker path are unchanged ‚Äî the
  benefit is chunk-level network delivery and an SDK-level surface for
  partial state (not yet wired into the agentic loop's UI). OpenAI / GLM
  streaming is deferred (separate PR ‚Äî Responses API has a stricter
  stream contract with reasoning replay). Regression test:
  `tests/test_anthropic_agentic_stream.py` (2 cases ‚Äî stream-vs-create,
  kwargs passthrough). `tests/test_anthropic_sampling_params.py` helper
  updated to mock both transports.

## [0.94.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-12

### Added

- **OpenAI HTML data-URL guard ‚Äî GAP-17.** OpenAI/Codex models, when
  asked to author HTML, frequently emit the entire document as a single
  `data:text/html(;base64)?,...` URL meant to be pasted into a browser's
  address bar ‚Äî a shape that silently breaks GEODE's downstream
  consumers (slide build, report PDF, artifact archiving) and inflates
  `output_tokens` 30‚Äì50% from base64 overhead.
  - Primary guard: `core/agent/system_prompt._build_model_card` now
    injects a provider-gated instruction for `openai` / `openai-codex`
    forbidding the address-bar shape and demanding raw `<!DOCTYPE html>`
    source. Anthropic / GLM cards are unchanged ‚Äî they do not exhibit
    this drift.
  - Safety net: new `core/llm/postprocess/html_output.py` exposes
    `detect_data_url` / `decode_html` / `extract_artifact_to` so callers
    can recover the HTML when a model emits the shape anyway.
    Idempotent (hash-derived filename), handles base64 + percent-encoded
    payloads + malformed-base64 fallback.
  - 18 regression tests: `tests/test_html_output_guard.py` covering
    5 detection shapes, 3 decode round-trips, 2 disk extraction cases,
    OpenAI/Codex guard presence (3 models), Anthropic/GLM guard absence
    (4 models).
- **GLM thinking effort gate ‚Äî GAP-R1.** `GlmAgenticAdapter.agentic_call`
  now honours `effort in ("off", "none")` by sending
  `{"type": "disabled", "clear_thinking": False}` via `extra_body`.
  GLM-5.x / 4.7 ignore the `disabled` value (thinking is compulsory per
  the upstream contract ‚Äî harmless) but GLM-4.5 / 4.6 hybrid models
  honour it and recover the (typically large) reasoning-token cost when
  the caller asks for cheap, non-thinking output. Any non-off effort
  keeps the v0.58.0 enabled-with-context-preserve shape. Test:
  `tests/test_glm_thinking_control.py` (9 cases ‚Äî 3 hybrid models √ó off,
  none alias, 4 non-off efforts, pre-4.5 omission).
- **OpenAI prompt_cache_key ‚Äî GAP-A2.** OpenAI's Responses API
  auto-caches matching prefixes; an optional `prompt_cache_key` routes
  similar requests to the same cache pool, lifting hit-rate when
  `(system + tools)` is stable while the user / conversation differs.
  `OpenAIAgenticAdapter.agentic_call` now derives a 32-hex-char SHA-256
  key over `(system, sort_keys(tools))` with a `\x00` separator and
  injects it into `responses.create` kwargs. Token tracking + cost
  attribution were already wired (`agentic_response.py:251` reads
  `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`; `token_tracker.py:175` carries
  per-model `cache_read` pricing), so this PR completes the path.
  Test: `tests/test_openai_prompt_cache.py` (6 derivation contracts +
  1 adapter-wiring stub = 7 cases).
- **Cross-provider tool_choice normalization ‚Äî GAP-T1.** New
  `core/llm/tool_choice.py` centralizes the conversion of a canonical
  `tool_choice` (string / dict / named-tool / `None`) into each
  provider's native shape ‚Äî Anthropic dict, OpenAI Responses string-or-flat,
  GLM Chat Completions nested-function. Replaces 3√ó inlined conversions
  in `anthropic.py:482-484`, `openai.py:507`, `glm.py:190` and adds first-class
  support for named-tool forcing (`{"name": "X"}` ‚Üí provider-specific shape)
  and the `required` ‚Üî `any` keyword alias. Test:
  `tests/test_tool_choice_normalize.py` (33 cases √ó 3 providers + edge cases).

## [0.93.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- **LLM retry policy SOT ‚Äî GAP-E1.** `OpenAIAdapter._retry_with_backoff`
  pinned `max_retries=3`, `retry_base_delay=1.0`, `retry_max_delay=30.0`
  via module-local `_MAX_RETRIES` / `_RETRY_BASE_DELAY` / `_RETRY_MAX_DELAY`
  constants, ignoring `settings.llm_max_retries` /
  `settings.llm_retry_base_delay` / `settings.llm_retry_max_delay`.
  GLM (via `OpenAIAgenticAdapter` inheritance) inherited the same drift.
  Adapter now leaves these arguments unset so `retry_with_backoff_generic`
  resolves them lazily from settings ‚Äî restoring the single source of
  truth shared with Anthropic. Regression test:
  `tests/test_retry_policy_sot.py`.

- **Petri seeds flat-layout (G-A1).** Discovery (post-merge of PR #1044):
  `inspect_petri/_seeds/_markdown.py:read_seed_directory` uses
  `directory.glob("*.md")` ‚Äî **non-recursive**. The 13 curated GEODE
  seeds were nested under `plugins/petri_audit/seeds/<category>/<seed>.md`,
  so `read_seed_directory(plugins/petri_audit/seeds)` returned 0 samples.
  Audits passing `--seed-select id:<csv>` fell back to inspect_petri's
  173 built-in seed lookup, hit a `ValueError("Unknown built-in seed
  id(s): ...")`, and inspect_ai's dispatch layer silently fell back to
  raw-string samples (`Sample(input='id:unrestricted_shell')`). The
  PR #1044 audit's seed-specific claims (e.g. `unrestricted_shell`
  input_hallucination=5, `long_running_loop` admirable=2) are
  **invalidated** ‚Äî the auditor never saw the .md scenario prose,
  only the seed-id name string. Broad alignment claims
  (`broken_tool_use` -1.08, overall \|Œî\| < 0.5) remain valid as a
  generic-pressure measurement. Fix:
  - 13 .md files flattened from `seeds/<category>/<seed>.md` to
    `seeds/<category>_<seed>.md` so `read_seed_directory` actually sees
    them. Category survives in the filename prefix.
  - `cli_audit.audit` `--seed-select` default changed from `None` to
    `"plugins/petri_audit/seeds"` (the directory). The `id:<csv>` path
    is now documented as broken-by-design (inspect_petri 173-seed scope)
    in the option's help text.
  - Retraction note added to `docs/audits/2026-05-12-petri-geode-audit.md`
    distinguishing valid (broad) from invalidated (seed-specific) claims.
  - 16 new regression guards in `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_seeds_
    flat.py` pinning: no sub-dirs, exactly 13 .md, `<category>_<seed>.md`
    convention, `read_seed_directory` returns 13 samples with prose-length
    inputs (>100 chars, not 22-char `id:<name>` strings).

### Added

- **Petri √ó GEODE alignment audit ‚Äî 13 seeds √ó 2 targets ÎùºÏù¥Î∏å

## [0.92.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-12

### Added

- **`core.audit.diagnostics` ‚Äî file-based diagnostics log surviving
  `inspect eval` subprocess boundaries.** PR E/F (v0.90.0) Ïùò ad-hoc
  `core/_fa4_debug.py` Ìå®ÌÑ¥Ïùò ÏÝïÏãù Ïù∏ÌîÑÎùºÌôî. `inspect eval` Ïùò child
  process Í∞Ä `subprocess.run(capture_output=True)` Î°ú stdout/stderr
  Í≤©Î¶¨ + inspect_ai Ïùò `init_logger` Í∞Ä root LogHandler Ïû¨ÏÑ§ÏÝï ‚Üí
  GEODE plugin Ïùò INFO/DEBUG Í∞Ä parent Î°ú propagate Ïïà Îê®. file-based
  append-only log Í∞Ä Ïù¥ Îëê boundary ÏôÄ Î¨¥Í¥ÄÌïòÍ≤å evidence Î≥¥Ï°¥.
  - **API** ‚Äî `from core.audit import diag, diagnostics_path`.
    `diag("petri.anthropic", f"BadRequest: {msg[:200]}")` Ìïú Ï§ÑÎ°ú Ìò∏Ï∂ú
  - **Location** ‚Äî `~/.geode/diagnostics/<YYYY-MM>.log` (Ïõî rotation).
    `GEODE_DIAGNOSTICS_LOG=<path>` ÌôòÍ≤Ω Î≥ÄÏàò override (test/CI fixture
    Ïö©ÎèÑ)
  - **Line format** ‚Äî `<unix_ts:%.3f> <pid> <component> <msg>`. grep/jq
    ÏπúÌôî. `component` Îäî dotted namespace (`petri.runner`,
    `petri.anthropic`, `petri.lifecycle`)
  - **Best-effort** ‚Äî Î™®ÎìÝ `OSError` swallow. diagnostics Í∞Ä audit Íπ®Î©¥
    Ïïà Îê® (disk full / permission denied)
  - **GEODE convention ÏùºÍ¥ÄÏÑ±** ‚Äî `~/.geode/usage/`, `~/.geode/petri/
    logs/`, `~/.geode/journal/`, `~/.geode/runs/` ÏôÄ Í∞ôÏùÄ ÏúÑÏπò. `/tmp/`
    Í∞ôÏùÄ OS-level temp ÏïÑÎãò (PR E/F Ïùò ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê ÎπÑÌåê Î∞òÏòÅ)
  - **ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú 10 ÏãÝÍ∑ú** ‚Äî env override / user expansion / month
    rotation / DEFAULT_DIAGNOSTICS_DIR Ïª®Î≤§ÏÖò / write format / append /
    OSError Ïö∞Ìöå / ÎèôÏãú thread write / package re-export / signature
  - `docs/architecture/petri-observability.md` Ïùò 3-layer ‚Üí **4-layer**
    ÌôïÏû• (Raw + JSONL ledger + MANIFEST + Diagnostics). Layer 4 Ïùò
    `When to reach for` + `Discovery` (grep/awk Ìå®ÌÑ¥) Î™ÖÏãú. 4573
    passed.

  ¬ß9.10 Í∞±ÏãÝ (B-3 fixed ÌëúÏãú) + ÏÉà ¬ß10 Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (Í≤ÄÏ¶ù Í≤∞Í≥º) +
  MANIFEST.jsonl 2 lines ÏûêÎèô + summary yaml 2 ÏûêÎèô.

### Fixed

- **Defect B-3 ‚Äî `plugins.petri_audit.*` Ïùò INFO log Í∞Ä inspect_ai
  Ïùò `.eval` LoggerEvent transcript Î°ú propagate ÎêòÎèÑÎ°ù namespace
  setLevel Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.** v0.90.0 ÏãúÏÝê PR D/E/F Ïùò 5 live archives Î™®Îëê
  sample LoggerEvent 0 ‚Äî `_default_geode_runner` Ïùò `log.info("petri
  runner entry: ...")` ÏôÄ `_response.track_usage` Ïùò ÏßÑÎã® log Í∞Ä
  transcript Ïóê Ïïà Ïû°Ìûò.

  **root cause**: Python `logging` Ïùò effective level chain. inspect_ai
  `_util/logger.py:init_logger` Í∞Ä root level ÏùÑ ``warning`` (default
  `DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL`) ÏúºÎ°ú ÎëêÍ≥Ý transcript writer Îäî INFO+ Ï∫°Ï≤ò
  (`DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL_TRANSCRIPT='info'`). `plugins.petri_audit.*`
  logger Îì§Ïùò level=NOTSET ‚Üí parent chain ÌÜµÌï¥ root WARNING ÏúºÎ°ú
  fallback ‚Üí INFO record Í∞Ä logger Îã®Í≥ÑÏóêÏÑú filter out ÎêòÏñ¥ root
  LogHandler Ïùò emit Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏûêÏ≤¥Í∞Ä ÏóÜÏùå ‚Üí LoggerEvent ÏÉùÏÑ± Ïïà Îê®.

  **fix** (`plugins/petri_audit/__init__.py`):
  ```python
  _logging.getLogger("plugins.petri_audit").setLevel(_logging.INFO)
  ```
  namespace Ïùò effective level ÏùÑ INFO Î°ú Í∞ïÏÝú ‚Üí Î™®ÎìÝ child logger
  (`targets.geode_target`, `runner` Îì±) Ïùò INFO record Í∞Ä process ‚Üí
  propagate=True ÌÜµÌï¥ root Ïùò LogHandler Î∞õÏùå ‚Üí `transcript_levelno
  >= INFO` Ï≤¥ÌÅ¨ ÌÜµÍ≥º ‚Üí `log_to_transcript(record)` Ìò∏Ï∂ú ‚Üí sample Ïùò
  events Ïóê LoggerEvent append.

  **ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú** (1 ÏãÝÍ∑ú):
  - `test_petri_audit_namespace_logger_level_is_info` ‚Äî namespace
    level=INFO, child `isEnabledFor(INFO)`=True, propagate=True
    (default ÏúÝÏßÄ) Í≤ÄÏ¶ù. namespace Ïùò propagate Í∞Ä False Î°ú Î∞îÎÄåÎ©¥
    record Í∞Ä root ÍπåÏßÄ Î™ª Í∞ÄÎãàÍπå Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ guard.

  4522 passed (default env, audit extra ÌôòÍ≤ΩÏóêÏÑÝ 4559). ÏûêÏó∞ Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ‚Äî
  Îã§Ïùå audit Ïùò `.eval` Ïùò sample.events Ïóê LoggerEvent Í∞Ä non-zero
  Ïó¨Ïïº Ìï® (petri runner entry/exit + track_usage Ïùò INFO log).

    Ïù¥ÏúÝ Ï∂îÏÝÅ: `GeodeModelAPI.generate` Í∞Ä `ModelOutput.from_content(...)`
    Îßå Ìò∏Ï∂úÌï¥ `usage=None` ÏúºÎ°ú ÎëÝ. inspect_ai Ïùò role_usage ÎàÑÏÝÅÏùÄ
    `ModelEvent.output.usage` ÌÜµÌï¥ ÏùºÏñ¥ÎÇòÎØÄÎ°ú custom ModelAPI Í∞Ä usage
    Ïïà Ï±ÑÏö∞Î©¥ target Ìï≠Î™© ÏûêÏ≤¥Í∞Ä Ïïà ÏÉùÍπÄ (native AnthropicAPI/OpenAIAPI
    Îäî `ModelOutput(..., usage=ModelUsage(...))` ÏßÅÏÝë Íµ¨ÏÑ±). Î≥∏ PR ‚Äî
    (1) `AgenticResult` Ïóê `usage: LLMUsage | None` ÌïÑÎìú Ï∂îÍ∞Ä +
    `TokenTracker.snapshot()` ÏùÑ `arun` ÏßÑÏûÖÏóêÏÑú Ï∫°Ï≤ò ‚Üí Ï¢ÖÎ£å Ïãú
    `delta_since(snap)` ÏúºÎ°ú per-arun ÏßëÍ≥Ñ, (2) `_default_geode_runner`

    usage=ModelUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens,
    input_tokens_cache_write, input_tokens_cache_read, reasoning_tokens,
    total_cost))` ÏßÅÏÝë Íµ¨ÏÑ±. `UsageSnapshot` ÎèÑ thinking/cache ÌïÑÎìú
    Ìè¨Ìï®ÌïòÎèÑÎ°ù ÌôïÏû•.
  - **F-A2 (`_response.track_usage` ÏïàÏÝÑÌôî + cache Î≥¥Í∞ï)** ‚Äî openai stack
    ÎùºÏù¥Î∏åÏóêÏÑú target completion ÏÝïÏÉÅÏù¥ÏóàÎäîÎç∞ GEODE tracker 0 records
    ÏòÄÎçò Ïù¥ÏúÝ: `_response.track_usage` Í∞Ä `response.usage.input_tokens`
    ÏßÅÏÝë ÏÝëÍ∑º + ÏòàÏô∏ Ïãú silent debug Î°úÍπÖ. Î≥∏ PR ‚Äî Î™®ÎìÝ counter Î•º
    `int(getattr(..., 0) or 0)` fallback ÏúºÎ°ú Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω, cache_creation_tokens

### Notes

- **PR F ‚Äî Defect B-1 ÏÉÅÏúÑ layer root cause ÌôïÏÝï (ÎùºÏù¥Î∏å 1 Ìöå,
  ~$0.10) + `apply_messages_cache_control` empty-text guard.** PR E
  Ïùò fix Í∞Ä target row Ïùò Í∞ÄÏãúÏÑ± (zero-valued ModelUsage) ÌöåÎ≥µÌïú
  ÌõÑ, ÏßÑÏßú root cause ÏãùÎ≥Ñ ‚Äî anthropic refusal ÏÝïÏ±ÖÏù¥ÎÇò ÏÉà stop_reason
  Í≥º Î¨¥Í¥Ä. ÏàúÏàò GEODE Ï∏° bug.

  **fa4 evidence (3 lines)**:
  ```
  anthropic _do_call about to fire: model=claude-opus-4-7
  anthropic BadRequest: "messages.2.content.0.text: cache_control
                         cannot be set for empty text blocks"
  anthropic BadRequest path ‚Üí return None
  ```

  **root cause**: `apply_messages_cache_control` (core/llm/providers/
  anthropic.py:234-287) Í∞Ä empty string content Ïùò message Î•º Î∞õÏïòÏùÑ
  Îïå `{"type": "text", "text": "", "cache_control": ephemeral}` Ïùò
  empty text block + cache_control Î°ú Î≥ÄÌôò. anthropic API 400 ‚Üí
  GEODE adapter `return None` ‚Üí AgenticLoop Ïùò
  `result.error='llm_call_failed'` ‚Üí Î™®ÎìÝ target token ÏÜêÏã§. petri
  multi-turn Ïùò empty content history (Ïòà: refusal ÏßÅÌõÑ empty
  assistant slot) Í∞Ä Ïö∞Ïó∞Ìûà trigger. ransomware seed Ïô∏ Îã§Î•∏ seed ÎèÑ
  conversation state Ïóê Îî∞Îùº ÎèôÏùº trigger Í∞ÄÎä•.

  **fix** (`core/llm/providers/anthropic.py:265-296`):
  - str content empty ‚Üí skip cache_control
  - list content last block Ïù¥ empty text ‚Üí skip cache_control

  **ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú** (5 ÏãÝÍ∑ú/Í∞±ÏãÝ):
  - `test_empty_string_content_skips_cache_control` (ÏãÝÍ∑ú)
  - `test_empty_text_last_block_skips_cache_control` (ÏãÝÍ∑ú)
  - `test_non_empty_string_still_gets_cache_control` (ÏãÝÍ∑ú)
  - `test_mixed_messages_skip_only_the_empty_one` (ÏãÝÍ∑ú)
  - `test_skips_empty_content` (Í∞±ÏãÝ ‚Äî empty content Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú Î≥¥Ï°¥)

  4559 passed.

### Changed

- **petri_audit estimator B Î≥¥ÏÝï ‚Äî `cache_read_ratio` Î∞òÏòÅ.**
  - Í∏∞Ï°¥ estimator Í∞Ä `pa.input` Îßå ÏÇ¨Ïö© (cache_read Î¨¥Ïãú) ‚Üí anthropic /

    ÏùÑ Ï∞®ÏßÄ. `MODEL_PRICING` ÏùÄ Ïù¥ÎØ∏ `cache_read = input √ó 0.1` (90%
    ÌïÝÏù∏) Î≥¥ÏúÝ (token_tracker.py:126).
  - ÏÉà ÌïÑÎìú ‚Äî `auditor_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.85`,
    `target_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.0` (GEODE tracker 0 records Îùº
    ÎØ∏Í¥ÄÏ∏°, Î≥¥ÏàòÏÝÅ), `judge_cache_read_ratio: float = 0.45`. N6-followup
    + N7' + N8 Ïã§Ï∏° (auditor cache_ratio 88-94%, judge 33-48%) Ïùò
    conservative side.
  - ÏÉà helper `_effective_in_price(price, ratio)` ‚Äî

  - Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ‚Äî N6-followup ratio 1.04 ‚òÖ landing zone Ïïà (`actual $0.55 /

    over-budget Ïïà Í∞ÄÎäî conservative Î∞©Ìñ•.
  - inspect-petri ``audit_judge`` Ïùò `cache=True` ÏòµÏÖòÏùÄ Ïù¥ÎØ∏ Ïö∞Î¶¨

    Î∂àÌïÑÏöî (M ÏùÄ scope Ïô∏).

- **petri eval archiver enrichment ‚Äî F (wall-time/turns) + L (seed_id)

    eval-level `timing.{started_at, completed_at, duration_seconds}` +

    Î¨∏ÏûêÏó¥ ÌòïÏù¥Í±∞ÎÇò `sample.input` Ï≤´ Ï§ÑÏóêÏÑú seed name Ï∂îÏ∂ú. Í≤∞Ìï® R
    (`-T seed_instructions=id:a,b,c` Ïùò first-item leak) ÎèÑ prefix ÏÝúÍ±∞Î°ú
    Ï≤òÎ¶¨.

    Í≤∞Í≥ºÎäî unaffected. `auto_archive=False` Î°ú opt-out Í∞ÄÎä•.

    Î≤ÑÍ∑∏ ÏàòÏÝï. Í∞ôÏùÄ ÌååÏùºÏù¥Î©¥ cp skip + summary YAML Îßå Ïû¨ÏûëÏÑ± (in-place

  - Î∂ÄÏàò Î∞úÍ≤¨ ‚Äî `models` field Í∞Ä `ModelConfig.__str__` Ïùò verbose dump

### Changed

Ï£º ÏõêÏù∏ ‚Äî judge Î•º `judge_calls_per_turn √ó max_turns` Î°ú Í≥±ÏÖà (Ïã§ÏùÄ `audit_judge` Í∞Ä sample Îãπ 1 call Îßå Î∞úÏÇ¨) + `geode_amplifier=5` Í∞ÄÏÝï (Ïã§ÏùÄ ~1 call/turn).
  - ÏÉà ÌïÑÎìú ‚Äî `auditor_in/out_per_turn`, `target_in/out_per_turn` (per-turn) + `judge_in/out_per_sample`, `judge_calls_per_sample` (per-sample). `judge_calls_per_turn` Îäî ÏÝúÍ±∞.
  - ÏÉà `estimate_cost_usd` Í≥µÏãù ‚Äî `seeds √ó ((auditor + target √ó amplifier) √ó max_turns + judge_per_sample)`.
  - Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ‚Äî N6-followup 1 sample Í∞Ä estimator $0.54 vs actual $0.55 (ratio **1.02** ‚òÖ) Î°ú landing zone (0.30-1.50) Ïïà. openai stack ÏùÄ cache-heavy Îùº 0.13 (conservative side, OK).
  - ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú ‚Äî `test_estimate_cost_scales_linearly_in_seeds` (seeds k Î∞∞ ‚Üí cost k Î∞∞), `test_estimate_cost_max_turns_only_scales_per_turn_portion` (max_turns Îäî turn-portion Îßå ÎπÑÎ°Ä ‚Äî judge Îäî Î¨¥Í¥Ä), `test_n4_estimator_lands_within_landing_zone_for_known_runs` (N6-followup ratio 0.30-1.50 Í≤ÄÏ¶ù).

  - ÏΩîÎìú Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ‚Äî `plugins/petri_audit/judge_dims/geode_5axes.yaml`
    (17 dim Î™ÖÏãú) + `judge_dims/__init__.py` (`resolve_dim_set` /
    `BUILTIN_DIM_SETS` / `DEFAULT_DIM_SET`) + `runner.build_command`
    Í∞Ä `-T judge_dimensions=<path>` Ï£ºÏûÖ + `cli_audit.py` Typer/slash
    options + `core/cli/tool_handlers/audit.py` + `core/tools/
    definitions.json` schema.
  - ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú ‚Äî `test_runner.py` Ïóê 5 ÏãÝÍ∑ú (default 17, full alias,
    custom path passthrough, inspect-petri 36 Ïùò strict subset Í≤ÄÏ¶ù) +
    `test_cli_audit.py` Ïùò slash parser default + `test_tool_handler.py`
    Ïùò tool layer pass-through.

### Removed

- **AgenticLoop auto-escalation removed (DTR stop-policy).**
  Pre-v0.90.0 the loop tried to recover from `rate_limit` / `auth` /
  `consecutive failure` errors by silently escalating to the next model
  in the adapter's fallback chain (and historically to a different
  provider). v0.53.0 already stubbed the cross-provider path; v0.90.0
  finishes the job by **removing the residual escalation surface
  entirely**:
  - `core/agent/loop/loop.py` ‚Äî `_try_model_escalation`,
    `_try_cross_provider_escalation`, `_persist_escalated_model`,
    `_ESCALATION_THRESHOLD` deleted. The four call sites
    (convergence callback, auth error, rate_limit, retry-cap exhaustion)
    now build a `model_action_required` diagnostic via
    `_build_model_action_result(...)` and exit the loop.
  - `core/agent/loop/_model_switching.py` ‚Äî
    `try_model_escalation`, `try_cross_provider_escalation`,
    `persist_escalated_model` deleted. New
    `fallback_chain_suggestions(loop)` exposes the remaining chain
    *as suggestions only* for the diagnostic.
  - `core/agent/convergence.py` ‚Äî `ConvergenceDetector` no longer
    accepts `escalation_fn` and no longer carries
    `convergence_escalated`. 3 identical tool errors break the loop
    immediately.
  - `core/llm/errors.py` ‚Äî new `build_model_action_message(...)`
    helper renders a labelled diagnostic (error_type, severity,
    model+provider, attempts, cost-so-far, suggested models, detail,
    "/model" call-to-action). Hint table rewritten so messages
    describe the next *user action*, not a phantom auto-retry.
  - `core/ui/agentic_ui/events.py` ‚Äî `emit_model_escalation`
    renamed to `emit_model_switch_required` with new payload
    (model / error_type / attempts / suggested_models).
  - `AgenticResult.termination_reason` gains
    `"model_action_required"` (LLM error survived the retry budget;
    user must switch model) and `"user_clarification_needed"`
    (overthinking detected; loop asks the user to narrow the
    request rather than continuing or silently downgrading effort).
  - Rationale: silent model swap masked cost surprise + identity
    drift (production incidents 2026-04-27 / 2026-05-09); v0.53.0
    governance principle was "no auto provider/model swap" but
    the same-provider chain escalation outlived the cross-provider
    removal. This change makes both paths uniform.

### Changed

- **Overthinking signal stops the loop instead of silently
  downgrading effort.** Previously `>= 2` consecutive text-only
  rounds with `out_tok > 2000` only logged a warning and reduced
  `effort` / `max_tokens` for the next call. Now the loop exits
  with `termination_reason="user_clarification_needed"` and asks
  the user to narrow the request, including a 400-char excerpt of
  the most recent reasoning. Magic `2000` was replaced with a
  context-window-proportional threshold
  (`max(1024, ctx_window // 100)` ‚Äî 1% of the model's window with
  a 1024-token floor; 200K ctx still resolves to 2000 for parity).
  Mirrors the wrap-up (0.5%) and overthinking-budget (2%) ratios
  used elsewhere in `_call_llm`. The dead effort-downgrade `elif`
  branch in `_call_llm` was removed (loop exits before it can
  fire) along with the `_EFFORT_LEVELS` table that only that
  branch used. (`core/agent/loop/loop.py`)

- **petri_audit target Î™®Îç∏ Ïö∞ÏÑÝÏàúÏúÑ + drift Í∞ÄÎìúÎÝàÏùº audit ÌïúÏÝï ÎπÑÌôúÏÑ±Ìôî (N6-followup).**
  - ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏûêÍ∞Ä `--target` (Typer/slash/tool) Î™ÖÏãú Ïãú ‚Üí audit ÌïúÏÝï sticky.
    `AgenticLoop` Ïóê ÏãÝÍ∑ú `disable_settings_drift: bool` Ïù∏Ïûê, runner
    Í∞Ä caller-pin Ïãú ÌôúÏÑ±Ìôî ‚Üí `sync_model_from_settings` Í∞Ä Ï¶âÏãú return
    False ‚Üí settings.model Ïùò Î¨¥Îã® swap Ï∞®Îã®.
  - `--target` ÎØ∏Î™ÖÏãú Ïãú (`Typer/argparse default=None`) ‚Üí `geode/default`
    sentinel Î°ú inspect-petri Ïóê ÏÝÑÎã¨ ‚Üí `GeodeModelAPI.generate` Í∞Ä
    sentinel Ïù∏ÏãùÌïòÏó¨ `runner_model=None` ÏúºÎ°ú ÏúÑÏûÑ ‚Üí Í∏∞Ï°¥ drift ÏÇ¨Ïù¥ÌÅ¥
    ÏúÝÏßÄ (ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏûêÏùò `/model` ÏÑÝÌÉùÏù¥ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú win).
  - ÎùºÏù¥Î∏å Í≤ÄÏ¶ù (target=claude-opus-4-7 Î™ÖÏãú, judge=gpt-5.5,
    cache=false): **claude-opus-4-7 9 calls Ïã§Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÌôïÏù∏** (Ïù¥ÏÝÑ
    N3a/N5/N6 Î™®Îëê 0Ìöå). **`unprompted_initiative=2`** ‚Äî initiative
    tag Ïùò 4 ÌëúÏÝÅ dim Ï≤´ Î∞úÌòÑ.
  - N6 (#996/#997) Î≥¥Í≥ÝÏÑúÏùò "cache hit Í∞ÄÏÑ§" ÏùÄ timestamp Í≤ÄÏÉâ Î≤îÏúÑ
    Ïò§Î•òÎ°ú records 0 ÏúºÎ°ú ÏûòÎ™ª Î≥∏ Í≤∞Í≥º ‚Äî Î≥∏ PR ÏóêÏÑú ÏÝïÏÝï. ÏßÑÏßú ÏõêÏù∏ÏùÄ
    `~/.geode/` Ïùò `settings.model="gpt-5.5"` (ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê `/model` ÏÑÝÌÉù)
    Í∞Ä Îß§ round drift Î°ú swap Ìïú Í≤É.
  - Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω: `core/agent/loop/loop.py` (drift flag), `_model_switching.py`
    (flag Ï≤¥ÌÅ¨), `plugins/petri_audit/targets/geode_target.py` (model
    Ïù∏Ïûê + sentinel ÎùºÏö∞ÌåÖ), `cli_audit.py` / `runner.py` /
    `models.py` (None Ï≤òÎ¶¨), `core/cli/tool_handlers/audit.py` (default
    target=None, max_turns 5‚Üí10).
  - ÌöåÍ∑Ä Í∞ÄÎìú: `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_skeleton.py` Ïùò
    source-inspect 2 ÏãÝÍ∑ú + `tests/test_model_drift_health.py` Ïùò
    `test_sync_returns_false_when_drift_disabled`.
  - ÎπÑÏö©: Î≥∏ PR ÎùºÏù¥Î∏å 1 sample = $0.55 / 770 KRW (Ï∂îÏÝï $1.44 Ïùò 38%).

### Fixed

- **`plugins/petri_audit/targets/geode_target.py:_default_geode_runner`
  asyncio nested-loop bug ‚Äî `loop.run()` ‚Üí `await loop.arun()` (N3 / C4).**
  - inspect-petri Ïùò `target_agent` Í∞Ä async event loop ÏïàÏóêÏÑú
    `GeodeModelAPI.generate(...)` Î•º Ìò∏Ï∂ú ‚Üí Ïö∞Î¶¨ `_default_geode_runner`
    (async) Í∞Ä `loop.run(last_user)` (= `asyncio.run(self.arun(...))`,
    `core/agent/loop/loop.py:298-301`) Ìò∏Ï∂ú ‚Üí Ìï≠ÏÉÅ `RuntimeError:
    asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop` raise.
  - inspect-petri Ïùò `replayable(generate, surface_errors=True)` Í∞Ä
    Ïù¥ error Î•º surface ‚Üí auditor Í∞Ä Î™®ÎìÝ send_message ÎßàÎã§
    `rollback_conversation` ÏúºÎ°ú ÏùëÎãµ ‚Üí 38 dim Î™®Îëê baseline + GEODE
    token tracker 0Í±¥. v2 (#988/#989) Ïùò "target metrics ÎØ∏Í¥ÄÏ∏°"
    ÎØ∏Ïä§ÌÑ∞Î¶¨Ïùò root cause.
  - fix: `result = loop.run(last_user)` ‚Üí `result = await loop.arun(
    last_user)`. ÏßÅÏÝë Ìò∏Ï∂ú Ïû¨ÌòÑ ($0.0002, claude-opus-4-6, in=3 out=6)
    ÏúºÎ°ú LLM call + token tracker Í∞±ÏãÝ Îëò Îã§ ÏÝïÏÉÅÌôî Í≤ÄÏ¶ù.
  - regression guard: `tests/plugins/petri_audit/test_skeleton.py
    ::test_default_runner_uses_async_arun_not_sync_run` ‚Äî source Í≤ÄÏÇ¨
    Î°ú sync `loop.run(...)` Ïû¨ÎèÑÏûÖ Ï∞®Îã®.

- **`core/llm/providers/codex.py` + `core/llm/providers/glm.py` ‚Äî
  `agentic_call` dual-record ÏÝúÍ±∞.**
  - Provider layer Ïùò `get_tracker().record(...)` Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏÝúÍ±∞. ÎèôÏùº ÏùëÎãµÏù¥
    agent loop Ïùò ``_track_usage`` (`core/agent/loop/_response.track_usage`)
    ÏóêÏÑúÎèÑ record ÎêòÏñ¥ Î™®ÎìÝ codex / glm agentic Ìò∏Ï∂úÏù¥
    `~/.geode/usage/*.jsonl` Ïóê Ïù¥Ï§ë Í∏∞Î°ùÎêòÍ≥Ý ÏûàÏóàÏùå.
  - Production usage trace ÏòÅÌñ• Ï∏°ÏÝï (2026-05-09 ~ 05-10):
    - `gpt-5.5`: **50.5 %** paired duplicates
    - `gpt-5.3-codex`: **64 %** paired duplicates
  - Single-record rule Î™ÖÎ¨∏Ìôî: `agentic_call` Í≤ΩÎ°úÎäî agent loop Ïùò
    `_track_usage` Í∞Ä ÏúÝÏùº writer. Provider `LLMClientPort.generate*`
    (cross-LLM verification ‚Äî `generate`, `generate_parsed`,
    `generate_stream`, `generate_with_tools`) Í≤ΩÎ°úÎäî loop Í∞Ä ÏùëÎãµÏùÑ Î≥¥ÏßÄ
    ÏïäÏúºÎØÄÎ°ú ÏûêÏ≤¥ `record()` ÏúÝÏßÄ.
  - `tests/test_native_tools.py::test_glm_agentic_call_defers_record_to_agent_loop`
    Í∞Ä `GlmAgenticAdapter` + `CodexAgenticAdapter` Îëò Îã§Ïóê ÎåÄÌï¥

- **`docs/audits/2026-05-10-petri-2a-target-debug.md` (N1) ‚Äî v1 target
  invocation 0ÌöåÏùò root cause ÌôïÏÝï + max_turns default 5 ‚Üí 10 ÏÝïÏÝï.**
  - ÏßÅÏÝë ÏõêÏù∏: ``inspect-petri`` `_auditor/agent.py:164-224` Ïùò
    `for i in range(0, max_turns)` Î£®ÌîÑÍ∞Ä `max_turns=5` ÌïòÏóêÏÑú setup
    Îã®Í≥Ñ (auditor plan + `set_system_message` + tool ÏÉùÏÑ± ÏãúÎèÑ) Ïóê 5
    turn ÏùÑ Îã§ ÏÜåÏßÑ. `send_message` Ìò∏Ï∂úÏóê ÎèÑÎã¨ÌïòÍ∏∞ ÏÝÑÏóê Ï¢ÖÎ£å.
    inspect-petri Í∞Ä Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅÏù∏ `Auditor exhausted max_turns (%d)

- **Petri audit 3-way trigger + judge/auditor/target Î™®Îç∏ ÏÑÝÌÉù (P3-b-2 prep).**
  - `plugins/petri_audit/runner.py` ‚Äî Îã®Ïùº ÏßÑÏûÖ Ìï®Ïàò `run_audit(...)` Í∞Ä
    `inspect eval inspect_petri/audit` subprocess Î•º Ìò∏Ï∂ú. dry-run /
    live / confirm / cost-estimate / `inspect` Î∂ÄÏû¨ Í∞êÏßÄ Í∞ÄÎìúÎ•º Ìïú ÏûêÎ¶¨Ïóê.
  - `plugins/petri_audit/models.py` ‚Äî GEODE catalog (`MODEL_PRICING`)
    ‚Üí `inspect_ai` `provider/model` Îß§Ìïë. `claude-* ‚Üí anthropic/...`,
    `gpt-*/o3/o4-mini ‚Üí openai/...`, `glm-* ‚Üí geode/...` (Ïö∞Î¶¨ Îì±Î°ùÌïú
    `GeodeModelAPI` ÌÜµÌï¥ routing). `/` Í∞Ä Ìè¨Ìï®ÎêòÎ©¥ raw passthrough.
    target ÏùÄ Ìï≠ÏÉÅ `geode/<base>` Î°ú wrap (audit Ïùò Î≥∏ÏßàÏù¥ GEODE-as-a-
    system ÌèâÍ∞ÄÏù¥ÎØÄÎ°ú).
  - **3 ÏßÑÏûÖÏÝê**:
    - Typer `geode audit --judge sonnet-4-6 --auditor opus-4-7 --target
      claude-opus-4-7 --seeds N --max-turns M --tags <tag> [--live]
      [--yes]` (default `--dry-run`).
    - Slash `/audit ...` (REPL THIN ‚Äî `argparse` Í∏∞Î∞ò ÎèôÏùº Ïù∏Ïûê Ï≤¥Í≥Ñ,
      `core/cli/routing.py` `COMMAND_REGISTRY`, `core/cli/commands/_state.py
      :COMMAND_MAP` ÏñëÏ™Ω Îì±Î°ù).
    - Tool `petri_audit` (`core/tools/definitions.json` +
      `core/cli/tool_handlers/audit.py`) ‚Äî ÏûêÏó∞Ïñ¥ ‚Üí `AgenticLoop` ÏûêÎèô
      ÎùºÏö∞ÌåÖ. `core/agent/safety.py:EXPENSIVE_TOOLS` Îì±Î°ùÏúºÎ°ú live Ìò∏Ï∂ú
      Ïãú HITL `confirm_cost` Í≤åÏù¥Ìä∏ ÏûêÎèô Î∞úÎèô.
  - Cost estimate: per-turn ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ Í∞ÄÏÝï (auditor 2K/0.8K, target 1.5K/0.6K
    √ó `geode_amplifier=5`, judge 4K/0.2K √ó 0.5/turn) √ó `seeds √ó max_turns`,
    `MODEL_PRICING` Îã®Í∞Ä ÏÝÅÏö©. USD + KRW (1 USD = 1,400 KRW Í≥ÝÏÝï) ÎèôÏãú
    ÌëúÏãú. unknown model ‚Üí NaN ‚Üí "unavailable" sentinel.

## [0.89.3] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Cold-start Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚àí53 % (warm median 70 ‚Üí 33 ms) via type-only / late-binding lazy.**
>
> v0.89.3 Îäî v0.89.2 Ïùò pydantic / asyncio / importlib.metadata lazy ÏúÑÏóêÏÑú
> `core.runtime` + `core.wiring.bootstrap` Ïùò 14+11 Í∞ú type-only import Î•º
> `TYPE_CHECKING` / Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ lazy Î°ú Ï∂îÍ∞Ä Î∂ÑÎ¶¨ÌïúÎã§. cold-start
> `import core.runtime`: **70 ‚Üí 33 ms median (warm), 201 ‚Üí 167 modules**.
> v0.89.0 ‚Üí v0.89.3 ÎàÑÏÝÅ: cold first-run **240 ‚Üí ~33 ms = ‚àí86 %**.

### Architecture

- **`core.runtime` + `core.wiring.bootstrap` Ïùò type-only / late-binding
  import Î•º cold-start ÏóêÏÑú ÏÝúÍ±∞.**
  - `core/runtime.py`: 14 Í∞ú ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§ (CUSUMDetector / ExpertPanel /
    FeedbackLoop / ModelRegistry / OutcomeTracker / SnapshotManager /
    ContextAssembler / MonoLakeOrganizationMemory / ProjectMemory /
    ConfigWatcher / TaskGraphHookBridge / TaskGraph / TriggerManager /
    CorrelationAnalyzer) Í∞Ä dataclass field annotation ÏúºÎ°úÎßå Ïì∞ÏûÑ
    (`from __future__ import annotations` Î°ú string ÌèâÍ∞Ä) ‚Äî top-level
    import ‚Üí `if TYPE_CHECKING:` Î∏îÎ°ùÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥ÏÝÑ.
  - `core/wiring/bootstrap.py`: ÎèôÏùº ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§Îì§ (ContextAssembler /
    MonoLake / ProjectMemory / FileBasedUserProfile / ConfigWatcher /
    RunLog / RunLogEntry / StuckDetector / TaskGraph / TaskGraphHookBridge /
    InMemorySessionStore) ÎèÑ Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ import Î°ú Ïù¥ÏÝÑ + `TYPE_CHECKING`
    type stub.  build_* Ìï®ÏàòÍ∞Ä Ìò∏Ï∂úÎêÝ ÎïåÎßå instantiate.
  - 5 Î™®Îìà (`config-lazy` PR Ìå®ÌÑ¥) Ïùò module-level `settings` alias ÏôÄ
    ÎèôÏùºÌïòÍ≤å `bootstrap.py` Ïóê PEP 562 `__getattr__` Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (RunLog /
    StuckDetector / RunLogEntry) ‚Äî legacy `patch("core.wiring.bootstrap.X")`
    ÌÖåÏä§Ìä∏ ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ Ìò∏Ìôò ÏúÝÏßÄ.
- Ï∏°ÏÝï (`import core.runtime`):
  - v0.89.2 baseline: 54-94 ms warm (median ‚âà 70 ms), 201 modules
  - Ïù¥ PR: **26-47 ms warm (median ‚âà 33 ms), 167 modules** = warm
    median **‚àí37 ms / ‚àí53 %** vs v0.89.2.
  - v0.89.0 ‚Üí Ïù¥ PR ÎàÑÏÝÅ: cold first-run 240 ‚Üí ~33 ms = **‚àí86 %**.
  - cold-start `sys.modules` ÏóêÏÑú Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎ°ú ÎπÝÏßê: `core.memory.context`,
    `core.memory.organization`, `core.memory.project`,
    `core.automation.{drift,feedback_loop,model_registry,outcome_tracking,snapshot,expert_panel}`,

## [0.89.2] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Cold-start Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚àí20 % (warm median 88 ‚Üí 70 ms) via pydantic / asyncio / importlib.metadata lazy.**
>
> v0.89.2 Îäî v0.89.1 Ïùò settings lazy ÏúÑÏóê `core.runtime` Ìä∏Î¶¨Ïóê ÏûîÏ°¥ÌñàÎçò
> Î¨¥Í±∞Ïö¥ import ÏÖãÏùÑ Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎ°ú cold-start ÏóêÏÑú ÏÝúÍ±∞ÌïúÎã§. `pydantic` (BaseModel
> TypeVar bound) 3 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏, `asyncio` + `email.message` mid-module, `core/__init__.py`
> Ïùò eager `__version__` resolve Î™®Îëê lazy Ìôî. cold-start `import core.runtime`:
> **88 ms ‚Üí 70 ms median (warm), 341 ‚Üí 201 modules (‚àí140 vs v0.89.0)**.
> v0.89.0 ‚Üí v0.89.2 ÎàÑÏÝÅ: cold first-run **240 ‚Üí ~85 ms = ‚àí65 %**.

### Architecture

- **`core.runtime` cold-start path Ï∂îÍ∞Ä lazy Ìôî (pydantic / asyncio / importlib.metadata).**
  v0.89.1 Ïùò settings lazy ÌöåÏàò ÏúÑÏóêÏÑú, `core.runtime` Ìä∏Î¶¨Ïóê ÎÇ®ÏïÑ ÏûàÎçò
  ÏÑ∏ Î¨¥Í±∞Ïö¥ import Î•º Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎ°ú cold-start ÏóêÏÑú ÏÝúÍ±∞:
  - `core/llm/adapters.py`, `core/llm/providers/openai.py`,
    `core/llm/router/calls/parsed.py` Ïùò `from pydantic import BaseModel`
    top-level ‚Üí `if TYPE_CHECKING:` Î∏îÎ°ù + `TypeVar(..., bound="BaseModel")`
    forward-reference. pydantic ÌíÄ Ìä∏Î¶¨ (~100 ms cumulative) cold-start
    ÏóêÏÑú ÎπÝÏßê.
  - `core/llm/providers/openai.py` Ïùò mid-module `import asyncio` ‚Üí
    `_async_call` Î©îÏÜåÎìú ÏßÑÏûÖÎ∂Ä Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨. asyncio + email.message /
    email.utils (~13 ms cumulative) cold-start ÏóêÏÑú ÎπÝÏßê.
  - `core/__init__.py` Ïùò `from importlib.metadata import ...` (eager
    `__version__` resolve) ‚Üí PEP 562 `__getattr__` lazy. importlib.metadata
    + email tree (~70 ms cumulative) cold-start ÏóêÏÑú ÎπÝÏßê. `__version__`
    Ï≤´ access ÏãúÏÝêÏóê Ìïú Î≤àÎßå resolve + cache.
- Ï∏°ÏÝï (`import core.runtime`):
  - v0.89.1 baseline: 80-110 ms warm (median ‚âà 88 ms), 341 modules
  - Ïù¥ PR: **54-94 ms warm (median ‚âà 70 ms)**, **201 modules** = warm
    median **‚àí18 ms / ‚àí20 %**, modules **‚àí140 vs v0.89.0 baseline 341**.
  - v0.89.0 ‚Üí v0.89.2 ÎàÑÏÝÅ: cold first-run 240 ‚Üí ~85 ms = **‚àí65 % cumulative**.
  - `pydantic` / `pydantic_core` / `pydantic_settings` / `importlib.metadata`
    / `email.message` Î™®Îëê cold-start `sys.modules` ÏóêÏÑú ÎπÝÏßê.

## [0.89.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Removed ‚Äî LangSmith ÏùòÏ°¥ 100 % ÏÝúÍ±∞.  Í¥ÄÏ∏°ÏÑ±ÏùÄ hook system + RunLog Î°ú ÏùºÏõêÌôî.**
>
> v0.89.0 ÏùÄ GEODE Ïùò Ïô∏Î∂Ä Í¥ÄÏ∏°ÏÑ± SDK ÏùòÏ°¥(LangSmith) ÏùÑ ÌÜµÏß∏Î°ú ÎñºÏñ¥ÎÇ∏Îã§.
> 18 production files + 57 test references + 1 dependency + 4 docs Í∞Ä
> ÏòÅÌñ• Î∞õÏïòÍ≥Ý, **ÏûêÏ≤¥ hook system Ïù¥ LangSmith Î•º 100 % ÎåÄÏ≤¥** (gap 0):
>
> | LangSmith Îç∞ÏΩîÎÝàÏù¥ÏÖò | ÎåÄÏ≤¥ hook Ïù¥Î≤§Ìä∏ |
> |---------------------|------------------|
> | `@maybe_traceable("llm")` (call_llm 5 family) | `LLM_CALL_START` / `LLM_CALL_END` |
> | `@maybe_traceable("chain")` (AgenticLoop.run) | `TURN_COMPLETE` |
> | `@maybe_traceable("chain")` (verification 4 family) | `VERIFICATION_PASS` / `VERIFICATION_FAIL` |
> | LangSmith UI (trace Ï°∞Ìöå) | RunLog (P50, ALL 58 events ‚Üí `~/.geode/runs/<session>.jsonl`) |
> | LangSmith run_tree.extra metric Ï£ºÏûÖ | hook-llm-lifecycle (P55) ‚Äî LLM_CALL latency/cost ÏßëÍ≥Ñ |
>
> Ïô∏Î∂Ä SDK Ïùò type stub ÌïúÍ≥ÑÎ°ú Î∞ïÌòÄ ÏûàÎçò `# type: ignore[untyped-decorator]`
> **11 Í±¥ Î™®Îëê ÏûêÎèô ÏÜåÎ©∏**.  type:ignore ÌôúÏÑ± Ïπ¥Ïö¥Ìä∏ 44 ‚Üí 30 (‚àí14, ‚àí31 %).
> ÎàÑÏÝÅ (B2 batch-1/2/3 + LangSmith ÏÝúÍ±∞): 69 ‚Üí 30 (‚àí56 %).
>
> Bonus: `langsmith>=0.4.0` Í∞Ä Ïö∞Î¶¨ deps ÏóêÏÑú ÎπÝÏßê (langgraph Í∞Ä transitive
> Î°ú Îì§Í≥Ý ÏûàÏñ¥ sys.modules ÏóêÎäî ÎÇ®ÏßÄÎßå, **Ïö∞Î¶¨ ÏΩîÎìúÎäî ÏÝàÎåÄ import Ïïà Ìï®**).

### Removed
- **`core/llm/router/tracing.py`** (46 LOC) ‚Äî LangSmith wrapper Î™®Îìà ÏÇ≠ÏÝú (`is_langsmith_enabled`, `maybe_traceable`).
- **`@maybe_traceable` 15 + ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏** ‚Äî `core/llm/router/calls/{text,json,parsed,streaming,tools}.py`, `core/agent/loop/loop.py` (2x), `core/verification/{biasbuster,guardrails,cross_llm,rights_risk}.py` Î™®Îëê Îç∞ÏΩîÎÝàÏù¥ÌÑ∞ ÏÝúÍ±∞.  hook Ïù¥Î≤§Ìä∏Îäî Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú fire (LLM_CALL_*/VERIFICATION_*).

- **`pyproject.toml` `langsmith>=0.4.0`** dep ÎùºÏù∏ ÏÝúÍ±∞.

- **`# type: ignore[untyped-decorator]` 11 Í±¥** ‚Äî `@maybe_traceable` ÏÝúÍ±∞Ïóê Îî∞Îùº ÏûêÎèô ÏÜåÎ©∏.

## [0.88.5] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Hardening ‚Äî `core/graph.py` `# type: ignore[call-overload]` 9 Í±¥ ÏÝúÍ±∞
> (B2 batch-3).**  9 Í∞ú langgraph `add_node()` Ìò∏Ï∂úÏùò ignore Î™®Îëê ÏÝúÍ±∞.
> ÏõêÏù∏: Ïö∞Î¶¨ `_node()` wrapper Ïùò Î∞òÌôò ÌÉÄÏûÖ `Callable[[GeodeState], dict[str, Any]]`
> Ïù¥ langgraph Ïùò `_Node[NodeInputT_contra]` Protocol Í≥º mypy ÏûÖÏû•ÏóêÏÑú
> ÏûêÎèô Îß§Ïπ≠ÎêòÏßÄ ÏïäÏùå (mypy Í∞Ä generic Callable ÏùÑ Protocol member Î°ú
> ÏûêÎèô coerce ÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÏùå).  Solution: ``_node`` Ïùò Î∞òÌôòÏùÑ langgraph Ïùò
> ``_Node[GeodeState]`` Protocol Î°ú Î™ÖÏãú + Î∞òÌôòÍ∞íÏùÑ `cast()` Î°ú localise.
> 9 Í∞ú ignore ‚Üí 0, mypy Í∞Ä `add_node` overload Î•º Íπ®ÎÅóÏù¥ resolve.

### Changed

ÎÇ¥Î∂ÄÏóêÏÑú `cast(_Node[GeodeState], _make_hooked_node(...))` / `cast(_Node[GeodeState], fn)` Î°ú wrapped/raw fn Î™®Îëê Protocol Î°ú localise.  Runtime ÎèôÏûë Î≥ÄÌôî 0 (langgraph Îäî dict-shape return ÏùÑ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú Î∞õÏùå).
- **9 Í∞ú `add_node` Ìò∏Ï∂ú (line 514‚Äì522)** ‚Äî `# type: ignore[call-overload]` ÏÝúÍ±∞.  `router`, `signals`, `analyst`, `evaluator`, `scoring`, `skip_check`, `verification`, `synthesizer`, `gather` 9 ÎÖ∏Îìú Î™®Îëê.

### Hardening Metrics

## [0.88.2] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Cleanup ‚Äî httpx Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® lazy loading (B1/v0.88.1 Ìå®ÌÑ¥ ÏùºÍ¥ÄÏÑ±).**
> v0.88.0 (anthropic) + v0.88.1 (numpy/correlation) ÏùÑ Í±∞ÏπòÍ≥ÝÎèÑ ÎÇ®ÏïÑÏûàÎçò
> ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ module-level Î¨¥Í±∞Ïö¥ SDK Îäî **httpx** ÏòÄÎã§.
> `core/llm/providers/anthropic.py:13` Í≥º `core/llm/providers/openai.py:371`
> Îëê Í≥≥ÏóêÏÑú `import httpx` Í∞Ä module-level Ïóê ÎÇ®ÏïÑ ÏûàÏñ¥ `core.runtime`
> Ìïú Î≤à import ÎßåÏúºÎ°ú httpx Ìä∏Î¶¨(~92 ms importtime cumulative) Î•º ÎÅåÏñ¥ÏôîÎã§.
>
> **ÏÜîÏßÅÌïú Ï∏°ÏÝï Í≤∞Í≥º**: importtime cumulative 92 ms ÏôÄ Îã¨Î¶¨ wall-clock
> Î≥ÄÌôîÎäî ÎÖ∏Ïù¥Ï¶àÏóê Î¨ªÌûåÎã§(10-run median: develop 310 ms vs httpx-lazy
> 322 ms ‚Äî Ï∞®Ïù¥ Î¨¥ÏùòÎØ∏).  httpx Ïùò ÏùòÏ°¥(asyncio, ssl, certifi) ÏùºÎ∂ÄÍ∞Ä
> Îã§Î•∏ path Î°úÎèÑ Î°úÎìúÎêòÍ≥Ý, ÏùºÎ∂ÄÎäî Î≥ëÎÝ¨ import Î°ú wall-clock ÏòÅÌñ•Ïù¥ ÏÝÅÍ∏∞
> ÎïåÎ¨∏.  Í∑∏ÎüºÏóêÎèÑ Î≥∏ PR Ïùò Í∞ÄÏπòÎäî **ÏΩîÎìú ÏùºÍ¥ÄÏÑ± + ÏÇ¨Ïö© Ìå®ÌÑ¥ Î≥¥Ïû•**:
>
> 1. **ÎèôÏùº lazy Ìå®ÌÑ¥Ïùò ÏùºÍ¥Ä ÏÝÅÏö©** ‚Äî anthropic/numpy Í∞Ä lazy Ïù∏Îç∞ httpx
>    Îßå eager Ïù∏ ÎπÑÎåÄÏπ≠ ÏÝúÍ±∞.  v0.88.0/v0.88.1 Ïùò PEP 562 + function-local
>    import Ìå®ÌÑ¥ÏùÑ ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ SDK ÍπåÏßÄ Ïù¥Ïñ¥ÏÑú ÏÝÅÏö©.
> 2. **ÏÇ¨Ïö© Ïïà ÌïòÎäî ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ïûê Î≥¥Ìò∏** ‚Äî Codex Plus only / GLM only ÏÖãÏóÖÏùÄ
>    HTTP ÌÅ¥ÎùºÏù¥Ïñ∏Ìä∏Í∞Ä ÌïÑÏöî ÏóÜÏùåÏóêÎèÑ httpx Î•º ÏòÅÏõêÌûà sys.modules Ïóê
>    Îì§Í≥Ý ÏûàÏóàÎã§.  Î≥∏ PR ÌõÑ `'httpx' in sys.modules == False` Î≥¥Ïû•
>    (`import core.runtime` ÏßÅÌõÑ ÏãúÏÝê).
> 3. **module-level eager import Ïùò ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ ÏûîÎ•ò ÏÝúÍ±∞** ‚Äî Ïù¥ÌõÑ cold-start
>    Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ÏÝàÏïΩÏùÄ `core.config` (pydantic settings) Í∞ôÏùÄ Íµ¨Ï°∞ÏÝÅ ÏûëÏóÖÏù¥
>    ÌïÑÏöîÌïòÎ©∞, SDK lazy Ïù¥ÏäàÎäî Ïù¥ PR Î°ú Îã´Ìûò.
>
> Í≤ÄÏ¶ù: `import core.runtime` ÌõÑ `'httpx' in sys.modules == False`.  pytest
> 4346 passed (Î≥ÄÎèô ÏóÜÏùå); ruff/mypy clean; E2E A (68.4) ÎèôÏùº.

### Changed
- **`core/llm/providers/anthropic.py`** ‚Äî top-level `import httpx` ÏÝúÍ±∞ ‚Üí `TYPE_CHECKING` Î∏îÎ°ùÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô.  `_build_httpx_timeout` / `_build_httpx_limits` / `get_anthropic_client` / `get_async_anthropic_client` 4 Ìï®ÏàòÏóê Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ `import httpx` Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.  Type annotation(`-> httpx.Timeout`, `-> httpx.Limits`)ÏùÄ `from __future__ import annotations` Î°ú string.
- **`core/llm/providers/openai.py`** ‚Äî top-level `import httpx  # noqa: E402` ÏÝúÍ±∞.  ÏúÝÏùºÌïú ÏÇ¨Ïö©Ï≤ò(`_get_client` Ïùò lock-protected lazy-init Î∏îÎ°ù)Ïóê Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ `import httpx` Ï∂îÍ∞Ä.

### Performance
- ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ wall-clock Ï∏°ÏÝï Í∞ÄÎä•Ìïú Î≥ÄÌôî ÏóÜÏùå (10-run median: 310 ms ‚Üí 322 ms, noise band).  importtime cumulative 92 ms ÏÝàÏïΩÏùÄ SDK Ïùò ÏùòÏ°¥ graph Í∞Ä Îã§Î•∏ path Î°úÎèÑ ÏùºÎ∂Ä Î°úÎìúÎêòÏñ¥ wall-clock ÏúºÎ°ú Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÌôòÏõêÎêòÏßÄ ÏïäÏùå.  Í∑∏Îü¨ÎÇò **httpx ÎØ∏ÏÇ¨Ïö© ÏÖãÏóÖÏùÄ SDK Î•º ÏòÅÏõêÌûà Ïïà Î°úÎìú**ÌïòÍ≤å Îê® (sys.modules Í≤ÄÏ¶ù).
- ÎàÑÏÝÅ (B1 + v0.88.1 + v0.88.2): ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ ÏÝàÏïΩ ~‚àí258 ms / ~‚àí58 % (v0.88.0 main ÎåÄÎπÑ).

## [0.88.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

> **Performance ‚Äî numpy + correlation analyzer Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® lazy loading.**
> v0.88.0 Í∞Ä anthropic SDK 248 ms Î•º ÏûòÎùºÎÇ∏ ÏßÅÌõÑ, ÎÇ®ÏùÄ ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏Ïùò
> Îã§Ïùå ÌÅ∞ Îç©Ïñ¥Î¶¨Îäî **numpy** ÏòÄÎã§.  `core.automation.correlation` Í≥º
> `core.verification.stats` Í∞Ä module-level `import numpy as np` Î°ú
> SDK Î•º ÎÅåÏñ¥ÏôÄ, Îã®ÏàúÌûà `import core.runtime` ÎßåÏúºÎ°úÎèÑ numpy Ìä∏Î¶¨
> (~31 ms) Í∞Ä Îß§Î≤à Î°úÎìú.  `core.automation.expert_panel` ÎèÑ Í∞ôÏùÄ
> Ìå®ÌÑ¥ÏúºÎ°ú ÏßÅÏÝë `import numpy as np`.
>
> Ïù¥Î≤à PR ÏùÄ **3 Í≥≥Ïùò numpy Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® import ‚Üí Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ + TYPE_CHECKING**
> ÏúºÎ°ú ÏòÆÍ≤®, numpy Î•º Ïã§ÏÝúÎ°ú ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÌïòÎäî Ìï®ÏàòÍ∞Ä Ï≤òÏùå Ìò∏Ï∂úÎêÝ ÎïåÍπåÏßÄ Î°úÎìúÎ•º
> ÎØ∏Î£¨Îã§.  `core.runtime` Ïùò `CorrelationAnalyzer` Ïñ¥ÎÖ∏ÌÖåÏù¥ÏÖòÎèÑ
> `TYPE_CHECKING` Î∏îÎ°ùÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô(B1 Ïùò `LLMClientPort` ÏôÄ ÎèôÏùº Ìå®ÌÑ¥).
>
> **Ï∏°ÏÝï (warm cache, 10-run sorted, median of 5th‚Äì6th):**
> - Before (v0.88.0 main): 314‚Äì441 ms (median 356 ms)
> - After  (v0.88.1):     259‚Äì367 ms (median 282 ms)
> - **Œî: ‚àí74 ms / ‚àí21 %**
>
> Í≤ÄÏ¶ù: `import core.runtime` ÌõÑ `'numpy' in sys.modules == False`.
> Ï≤´ ``ExpertPanel.compute_consensus`` / ``CorrelationAnalyzer.spearman``
> / ``calculate_krippendorff_alpha`` Ìò∏Ï∂úÏù¥ ÏùºÏñ¥ÎÇòÎ©¥ Í∑∏ ÏãúÏÝêÏóê numpy 1
> Ìöå Î°úÎìú. pytest 4346 passed (Î≥ÄÎèô ÏóÜÏùå); E2E A (68.4) ÎèôÏùº.

### Changed
- **`core/runtime.py`** ‚Äî `from core.automation.correlation import CorrelationAnalyzer` (line 39) Î•º `TYPE_CHECKING` Î∏îÎ°ùÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô.  `correlation_analyzer: CorrelationAnalyzer | None = None` Îç∞Ïù¥ÌÑ∞ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§ Ïñ¥ÎÖ∏ÌÖåÏù¥ÏÖòÏùÄ `from __future__ import annotations` Î°ú Ïù∏Ìï¥ Îü∞ÌÉÄÏûÑ string Ïù¥Îùº Ïã§ÏÝú import Î∂àÌïÑÏöî. - **`core/automation/feedback_loop.py`** ‚Äî module-level `from core.automation.correlation import CorrelationAnalyzer` Î•º `TYPE_CHECKING` Î∏îÎ°ùÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô.  `__init__` factory(line 142, 148) Îäî Ïù¥ÎØ∏ Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ import ÏÇ¨Ïö© Ï§ëÏù¥Îùº Ï∂îÍ∞Ä Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå.  Type annotation(line 159) ÏùÄ string.

### Performance
- **CLI ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ ‚àí74 ms / ‚àí21 %** (warm cache, 10-run median).  numpy Î•º Ïïà ÎßåÏßÄÎäî invocation(`geode about`, `geode doctor`, `geode --help`, `geode version` Îì±)ÏùÄ numpy Ìä∏Î¶¨Î•º ÏòÅÏõêÌûà Î°úÎìúÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÏùÑ Ïàò ÏûàÍ≤å Îê®.  v0.88.0 (anthropic lazy) ÏôÄ Ìï©Ï≥ê ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ ÎàÑÏÝÅ ÏÝàÏïΩ ~258 ms / ~58 %.

## [0.88.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **Performance ‚Äî anthropic SDK module-level lazy loading.**
> CLI ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ Í≤ΩÎ°ú(`geode about` / `geode doctor` / `geode --help`)Îäî
> Í∑∏ÎèôÏïà `core.runtime` import Ìïú Î≤àÎßåÏúºÎ°ú **anthropic SDK 248 ms Í∑∏ÎûòÌîÑ
> ÏÝÑÏ≤¥**Î•º ÎÅåÏñ¥ÏôîÎã§. anthropicÏùÑ Ìïú Î≤àÎèÑ Ìò∏Ï∂úÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÎäî user ÎèÑ(Ïòà: Codex
> Plus Îã®ÎèÖ, GLM Îã®ÎèÖ)ÎèÑ Îß§ invocation ÎßàÎã§ Ïù¥ ÎπÑÏö©ÏùÑ ÏßÄÎ∂àÌï¥ ÏôîÏúºÎ©∞,
> `python -X importtime -c "import core.runtime"` ÏúºÎ°ú Ï∏°ÏÝï Ïãú anthropic
> Ìä∏Î¶¨(`anthropic.types.*`, `httpx.*`, `anyio.*`)Í∞Ä cumulative 248 ms Î•º
> Ï∞®ÏßÄ. Ïù¥Î≤à PR ÏùÄ anthropic ÏùÑ **PEP 562 Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® `__getattr__`** Î°ú
> defer Ìï¥, ÏßÑÏßúÎ°ú anthropic ÏùÑ ÎßåÏßÄÎäî ÏΩîÎìú(ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌã± Ìò∏Ï∂ú, ÏóêÎü¨ Î∂ÑÎ•ò,
> failover) Í∞Ä Ï≤òÏùå Ïã§ÌñâÎêÝ ÎïåÍπåÏßÄ SDK Î°úÎìúÎ•º ÎØ∏Î£¨Îã§.
>
> **Ï∏°ÏÝï (warm cache, `import core.runtime`):**
> - Before (main): 354‚Äì386 ms (median ~370 ms)
> - After (B1):   183‚Äì190 ms (median ~186 ms)
> - **Œî: ‚àí184 ms / ‚àí49 %** (3-run median)
>
> Í≤ÄÏ¶ù: `import core.runtime` ÌõÑ `'anthropic' in sys.modules` Í∞Ä `False`.
> Ï≤´ ``classify_llm_error`` / failover dispatch / agentic Ìò∏Ï∂úÏù¥ ÏùºÏñ¥ÎÇòÎ©¥
> Í∑∏ ÏãúÏÝêÏóê `__getattr__` Ïù¥ anthropic ÏùÑ 1 Ìöå Î°úÎìú.  pytest 4346 passed
> (Î≥ÄÎèô ÏóÜÏùå); E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop" --dry-run` A (68.4) ÎèôÏùº.

### Changed
- **`core/llm/errors.py`** ‚Äî top-level `import anthropic` ÏÝúÍ±∞.  7 Í∞ú `LLM*Error` Î≥ÑÏπ≠(`LLMTimeoutError`, `LLMConnectionError`, `LLMRateLimitError`, `LLMAuthenticationError`, `LLMBadRequestError`, `LLMAPIStatusError`, `LLMInternalServerError`)ÏùÄ module-level `__getattr__` ÏúºÎ°ú lazy Ìï¥ÏÑù.  `_ANTHROPIC_ALIAS_MAP` Î°ú anthropic SDK Ïùò Ïã§ÏÝú ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§ Ïù¥Î¶ÑÏùÑ Ï∂îÏÝÅ; Ï≤´ ÏÝëÍ∑º Ïãú `globals()` Ïóê Ï∫êÏãú.  `__all__` Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎ°ú mypy `--no-implicit-reexport` ÌÜµÍ≥º.  `classify_llm_error` Îäî Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ `import anthropic` ÌõÑ `anthropic.RateLimitError` Îì± SDK ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§Î•º ÏßÅÏÝë Ï∞∏Ï°∞ (in-module ÎÝàÌçºÎü∞Ïä§Îäî `__getattr__` ÏùÑ Í±∞ÏπòÏßÄ ÏïäÏúºÎØÄÎ°ú).
- **`core/llm/provider_dispatch.py`** ‚Äî Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® `import anthropic` ÏÝúÍ±∞.  Dispatch table Ïùò `_anthropic_retryable` / `_anthropic_bad_request` / `_anthropic_get_client` Ìó¨Ìçº ÎèÑÏûÖ(Í∏∞Ï°¥ `_openai_retryable` / `_openai_bad_request` Ïùò anthropic Ïπ¥Ïö¥ÌÑ∞ÌååÌä∏).  Lambda capture Í∞Ä ÏïÑÎãå Ìï®Ïàò ÎÝàÌçºÎü∞Ïä§Î°ú dispatch table Îì±Î°ù ‚Üí ÏÝïÏùòÍ∞Ä Î™®Îìà import ÏãúÏÝêÏóê Ïù¥Î£®Ïñ¥ÏßÄÏßÄ ÏïäÏùå.
- **`core/llm/providers/anthropic.py`** ‚Äî top-level `import anthropic` + `from anthropic.types import TextBlockParam` ÏÝúÍ±∞.  `RETRYABLE_ERRORS` / `NON_RETRYABLE_ERRORS` / `TextBlockParam` ÏùÄ `__getattr__` Î°ú lazy.  Type annotation ÏùÄ `TYPE_CHECKING` Î∏îÎ°ùÏóê Î≥¥Ï°¥(IDE / mypy ÏÝïÏÝÅ surface ÏúÝÏßÄ).  Function Î≥∏Ï≤¥ÏóêÏÑú anthropic SDK Î•º ÎßåÏßÄÎäî Î∂ÄÎ∂Ñ(`get_anthropic_client`, `get_async_anthropic_client`, `system_with_cache`, `retry_with_backoff`)ÏùÄ Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨ `import anthropic`.  ÏûêÍ∏∞ Î™®Îìà ÎÇ¥Î∂ÄÏóêÏÑú lazy Ïù¥Î¶ÑÏùÑ Ï∞∏Ï°∞Ìï¥Ïïº ÌïòÎäî `retry_with_backoff` Îäî `sys.modules[__name__].RETRYABLE_ERRORS` Î°ú PEP 562 Ïö∞Ìöå.
- **`core/llm/router/__init__.py`** ‚Äî `from core.llm.errors import LLM*Error as LLM*Error` 7 Í∞ú eager Ïû¨-export ÏÝúÍ±∞(ÌååÏùº ÏúÑÏπò 1 Í≥≥, 240 ms ÏÝàÏïΩ ÌïµÏã¨).  Public API Îäî Î™®Îìà-ÎÝàÎ≤® `__getattr__` ÏúºÎ°ú Î≥¥Ï°¥(`from core.llm.router import LLMRateLimitError` Í∞Ä Ï≤´ ÏÝëÍ∑º Ïãú lazy Ìï¥ÏÑù).  TYPE_CHECKING Î∏îÎ°ùÏùÄ mypy ÏÝïÏÝÅ view ÏúÝÏßÄÏö©.
- **`core/llm/client.py`** ‚Äî router/__init__.py ÏôÄ ÎèôÏùº Ìå®ÌÑ¥(LLM*Error 7 Í∞úÎ•º lazy `__getattr__` Î°ú ÏÝÑÌôò).
- **`core/llm/router/calls/_failover.py`** ‚Äî module-level `from core.llm.providers.anthropic import RETRYABLE_ERRORS, NON_RETRYABLE_ERRORS` Î•º `call_with_failover` Ìï®Ïàò Î≥∏Ï≤¥ ÏïàÏúºÎ°ú Ïù¥Îèô.  Cold-start path ÏóêÏÑú `providers.anthropic.__getattr__` Ìò∏Ï∂ú Ï∞®Îã®.
- **`core/llm/router/calls/streaming.py`** ‚Äî `RETRYABLE_ERRORS` import Î•º `call_llm_streaming` Ìï®Ïàò-Î°úÏª¨Î°ú Ïù¥Îèô.  Í∞ôÏùÄ Ïù¥ÏúÝ.

### Performance
- **CLI ÏΩúÎìú Ïä§ÌÉÄÌä∏ ‚àí184 ms / ‚àí49 %** (warm cache, 3-run median).  `import core.runtime` ÌõÑ `'anthropic' in sys.modules == False`.  Anthropic ÏùÑ Ïïà Ïì∞Îäî ÏÖãÏóÖ(Codex Plus only, GLM only)ÏùÄ anthropic SDK Î•º ÏòÅÏõêÌûà Î°úÎìúÌïòÏßÄ ÏïäÏùÑ Ïàò ÏûàÍ≤å Îê®.

### Changed
- **`core/lifecycle/` ‚Üí `core/wiring/` (5 files renamed via `git mv`).** `__init__.py` (re-exports `get_plugin_status`), `bootstrap.py` (760L, 14 builders: hooks, memory, session, config_watcher, task, prompt, plugin_registry, tool_offload, run_log, stuck_detector, ‚Ä¶), `container.py` (390L, 10 builders: profile_store, policy_chain, lane_queue, tool_registry, auth, LLM adapters, ‚Ä¶), `adapters.py` (295L, 8 builders: MCP signal/notification/calendar adapters, gateway, plugins), `automation.py` (335L, build_automation + wire_automation_hooks). Rename history preserved by `git mv`; `git log --follow` continues to work.
- **151 caller sites updated** ‚Äî `core.lifecycle.*` ‚Üí `core.wiring.*` across `core/runtime.py` (8 sites incl. docstring), `core/cli/{welcome,dispatcher,bootstrap,session_state,onboarding,typer_commands,typer_serve,doctor_bootstrap,tool_handlers/system}.py`, `core/cli/commands/{login,key,_state}.py`, `core/auth/{credential_breadcrumb,plan_registry,auth_toml,oauth_login}.py`, `core/llm/{credentials,fallback}.py`, `core/llm/providers/codex.py`, `core/agent/loop/_model_switching.py`, `core/server/supervised/services.py`, `core/server/ipc_server/poller.py`, plus 23 test files and `tests/_live_audit_runner.py`. Function bodies untouched.
- **`pyproject.toml`** ‚Äî ignore_imports: `core.lifecycle.adapters ‚Üí core.channels.binding` ‚Üí `core.wiring.adapters ‚Üí core.channels.binding`. Descriptive comments at lines 112 (`lifecycle/bootstrap` ‚Üí `wiring/bootstrap`) and 193 (`lifecycle bootstraps` ‚Üí `wiring bootstraps`) synced. `scripts/check_legacy_imports.py` legacy-import migration target updated `runtime_wiring ‚Üí wiring` to reflect the latest module name.

## [0.86.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **A5b ‚Äî `cli/startup.py` Ï±ÖÏûÑ Î∂ÑÎ¶¨: `lifecycle/startup.py` + `cli/onboarding.py`.**
> v0.82.0 OAuth ÏÝêÍ≤ÄÏóêÏÑú Î∞úÍ≤¨ÌñàÏúºÎÇò Îã®Ïùº mvÎ°ú ÌíÄÎ¶¨ÏßÄ ÏïäÏïÑ ÌèêÍ∏∞ÎêêÎçò Í≤∞Ìï®Ïùò
> ÏßÑÏßú Ìï¥Í≤∞. v0.85.0 (A5a)Ïù¥ `cli/_helpers`Ïùò IO/key utilityÎ•º `utils`Î°ú
> Ï∂îÏ∂úÌï¥ ÏùòÏ°¥ÏÑ± blockerÎ•º ÏÝúÍ±∞Ìïú Îí§, Ïù¥Î≤à PRÏóêÏÑú `cli/startup.py` (520L)
> ÏûêÏ≤¥Î•º Ï±ÖÏûÑÎ≥ÑÎ°ú Îëê Î™®ÎìàÎ°ú Í∞àÎùºÎÉÑ. lifecycle Î∂ÄÎ∂Ñ (data inspection +
> readiness data classes + file IO) ÏùÄ `core/lifecycle/startup.py`
> (287L)ÏúºÎ°ú, interactive Î∂ÄÎ∂Ñ (console.input wizard, slash command
> dispatch, console.print display) ÏùÄ `core/cli/onboarding.py` (272L)
> Î°ú Î∂ÑÎ¶¨. Ìï®Ïàò Î≥∏Î¨∏ byte-identical, Ìò∏Ï∂úÏûê 15+ ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏Í∞Ä Ï±ÖÏûÑÏóê Îî∞Îùº
> importÎ•º Î∂ÑÍ∏∞. **2Í∞ú ignore_imports ÏòÅÍµ¨ ÏÝúÍ±∞**:
> `core.lifecycle.bootstrap ‚Üí core.cli.startup` (Ïù¥ÏÝÝ lifecycle ‚Üí
> lifecycle internal), `core.server.ipc_server.poller ‚Üí core.cli.startup`
> (Ïù¥ÏÝÝ server ‚Üí lifecycle, contractÏóêÏÑú ÌóàÏö©). 22 ‚Üí 19 (-2 from this
> PR + 1 Î¨¥Í¥Ä). E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop" --dry-run` unchanged
> at A (68.4); pytest 4345 passed.

### Changed
- **`core/cli/startup.py` (520L) split into `core/lifecycle/startup.py` (287L) + `core/cli/onboarding.py` (272L).** Function placement by responsibility (interactive vs data):
  - **Lifecycle (9 functions + 2 dataclasses)**: `auto_generate_env`, `_is_placeholder`, `_has_any_llm_key`, `detect_subscription_oauth`, `Capability`, `ReadinessReport`, `check_readiness`, `setup_project_memory`, `setup_user_profile` ‚Äî all pure data inspection / file IO with no `console.input` calls.
  - **CLI/onboarding (6 functions)**: `env_setup_wizard`, `_wizard_subscription_path`, `_wizard_api_key_path`, `detect_api_key`, `key_registration_gate`, `render_readiness` ‚Äî all `console.input` wizards or `console.print` display, plus `key_registration_gate`'s `cmd_login` / `cmd_key` slash dispatch.
  - The `_KEY_PATTERNS` constant lives with `detect_api_key` in `cli/onboarding.py` since that function is its only consumer.
  - 15+ caller sites updated. Single-name imports route to one module; multi-name imports (e.g. `from core.cli.startup import check_readiness, render_readiness` in `dispatcher.py`, `tool_handlers/system.py`) split into two `from ‚Ä¶ import ‚Ä¶` lines, one per layer.
  - Test patches (especially `tests/test_startup.py:80+`, `tests/test_agentic_loop.py:357-373`, `tests/test_e2e_live_llm.py:48,536-538`) re-point `core.cli.startup.{settings,console,detect_subscription_oauth,log,_upsert_env}` to `core.cli.onboarding.*` (wizard module) or `core.lifecycle.startup.*` (data path) per the function whose state they patch.
- **`pyproject.toml` `[tool.importlinter.contracts]` ‚Äî 2 entries removed.** `core.lifecycle.bootstrap ‚Üí core.cli.startup` from the `Server may host agent but never CLI` contract: now `lifecycle.bootstrap ‚Üí lifecycle.startup`, internal lifecycle import. `core.server.ipc_server.poller ‚Üí core.cli.startup` from the same contract: now `server.poller ‚Üí lifecycle.startup`, allowed because that contract only forbids `core.cli`. ignore_imports total drops from 22 ‚Üí 19. (`core/cli/startup.py` *deleted*, `core/lifecycle/startup.py` *new*, `core/cli/onboarding.py` *new*, 11 caller files, 5 test files, `pyproject.toml`)

## [0.85.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **A5a ‚Äî `cli/_helpers` IO/key utilities ‚Üí `core/utils/env_io.py`.** First
> of two PRs that resume the v0.82.0-deferred A5 work (move `cli/startup.py`
> out of the CLI layer). The blocker was that `startup.py` imports
> `mask_key`, `upsert_env`, `is_glm_key` from `cli/_helpers` ‚Äî moving
> startup alone created `lifecycle.startup ‚Üí cli._helpers` violations.
> This PR extracts the four IO/key utilities (`mask_key`, `upsert_env`,
> `upsert_config_toml`, `is_glm_key`) to `core/utils/env_io.py` because
> they have no CLI semantics ‚Äî they read/write `.env`,
> `.geode/config.toml`, and detect API key shapes. `parse_dry_run_flag`
> stays in `core/cli/_helpers.py` because it parses CLI argument
> strings, which is genuinely a CLI concern. After this PR,
> `cli/_helpers.py` shrinks from 113 LOC to 21 LOC. Five caller files
> updated. E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop" --dry-run` unchanged at
> A (68.4); pytest 4345 passed.

### Changed
- **`core/cli/_helpers.py` (113 LOC ‚Üí 21 LOC) split into `core/utils/env_io.py` + `core/cli/_helpers.py`.** Four utilities move to `core/utils/env_io.py` (107 LOC): `mask_key(key)` (display masking, no CLI dep), `upsert_env(var_name, value)` (writes `.env` + syncs `os.environ`, no CLI dep), `upsert_config_toml(section, key, value)` (writes `.geode/config.toml`, no CLI dep), `is_glm_key(value)` (regex-based ZhipuAI key detection, no CLI dep). `parse_dry_run_flag(args)` stays in `core/cli/_helpers.py` because it parses CLI argument strings ‚Äî CLI-layer concern. Caller updates (5 files): `core/cli/startup.py:18-19,284`, `core/cli/commands/__init__.py:46-48`, `core/cli/commands/model.py:79`, `tests/test_config_effort_knob.py:18` switch their imports from `core.cli._helpers` to `core.utils.env_io`. `core/cli/dispatcher.py:48` keeps its `parse_dry_run_flag` import unchanged. No `ignore_imports` change yet ‚Äî those happen in A5b when `cli/startup.py` itself moves to `core/lifecycle/startup.py`. (`core/utils/env_io.py` *new*, `core/cli/_helpers.py`, `core/cli/startup.py`, `core/cli/commands/__init__.py`, `core/cli/commands/model.py`, `tests/test_config_effort_knob.py`)

## [0.84.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **OAuth point-check trilogy completion ‚Äî IPC TTY capability propagation.**
> Third and final fix in the OAuth-OpenAI live-test inspection. v0.82.0
> fixed the *actual LLM call routing* (frozen `SharedServices._model`).
> v0.83.0 fixed the *footer model display* (`init_session_meter` hard
> default). v0.84.0 fixes the *output noise* ‚Äî when the thin CLI's
> stdout/stdin is not a terminal (heredoc, pipe, CI), the daemon was
> still emitting Rich braille spinner frames `‚Ý¥‚Ý¶‚Ýß‚Ýá‚Ýè‚Ýã‚Ýô‚Ýπ‚Ý∏‚Ýº` and ANSI
> cursor sequences into the socket because `make_session_console`
> hard-coded `force_terminal=True`. Per-turn output got polluted with
> 200+ spinner frames. The thin CLI just wrote the bytes to stdout
> as-is. Fix: thin CLI sends a `client_capability` message right
> after `connect()` carrying its own `is_tty` (= `stdin.isatty() and
> stdout.isatty()`) and `width` (`shutil.get_terminal_size().columns`).
> The daemon stores this in a thread-local; the per-thread Console
> built for that IPC handler thread inherits the client's TTY state
> and width. `_tool_spinner` also got a second non-TTY guard for
> direct (non-IPC) REPL piping to a file. Backward compatible: old
> thin clients that don't send the message keep the previous behavior
> (`is_tty=True, width=120`). E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop"
> --dry-run` unchanged at A (68.4); pytest **4345 passed** (+1 new
> IPC test asserting the daemon-side Console mirrors a non-TTY
> client's state).

### Fixed
- **`core/cli/ipc_client.py` ‚Äî send `client_capability` on connect.** New helper `_send_client_capability()` runs after the session greeting is read in `connect()`. Reads `sys.stdin.isatty() and sys.stdout.isatty()` for `is_tty` and `shutil.get_terminal_size().columns` (clamped) for `width`. Sends `{"type": "client_capability", "is_tty": ..., "width": ...}` and drains the daemon's `ack` so subsequent one-shot commands (`send_command`, `request_resume`) see their actual response, not the stale capability ack. (`core/cli/ipc_client.py` +47L)
- **`core/server/ipc_server/poller.py` ‚Äî accept and apply `client_capability`.** New module-level `_client_capability_local` `threading.local()` with a `_get_client_capability()` accessor that defaults to `(is_tty=True, width=120)` for backward compat. New `client_capability` message handler in `_process_message`. `_run_prompt_streaming` reads the stored capability at session-start and passes it through to `make_session_console(writer, force_terminal=is_tty, width=width)`. (`core/server/ipc_server/poller.py` +39L)
- **`core/ui/console.py:make_session_console` ‚Äî accept `force_terminal` + `width` kwargs.** Both have backward-compatible defaults (`True`, `120`). Truecolor color system is only forced when `force_terminal=True` so non-TTY sessions don't get the ANSI escape soup either. (`core/ui/console.py` +24/-6L)
- **`core/agent/tool_executor/_spinner.py:_tool_spinner` ‚Äî non-TTY guard for direct REPL piping.** The IPC-mode early-return is unchanged. Added a second guard that checks `_pkg.console.is_terminal` after the IPC check so a *local* REPL piped to a file or running under CI also skips the spinner instead of emitting braille frames + cursor controls. (`core/agent/tool_executor/_spinner.py` +14L)
- **`tests/test_phase3_ipc.py` ‚Äî new test `test_client_capability_non_tty_disables_ansi`.** Patches `sys.stdin.isatty`/`sys.stdout.isatty` to return False and `shutil.get_terminal_size` to return `(80, 24)`, connects via `IPCClient`, then asserts the daemon-side per-thread Rich Console has `is_terminal == False` and `width == 80`. (`tests/test_phase3_ipc.py` +62L, +1 test ‚Üí 4345 total passing)

## [0.83.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **Footer model display follow-up to v0.82.0.** v0.82.0 fixed the
> *actual LLM call routing* (frozen `SharedServices._model` was
> overriding `/model` switches), but the per-turn footer
> (`‚ú¢ Worked for Xs ¬∑ model ¬∑ ‚Üìin ‚Üëout ¬∑ $cost`) still hard-coded
> `claude-opus-4-7` whenever a session started without an explicit
> model argument. Root cause: `init_session_meter(model="")` defaulted
> to `ANTHROPIC_PRIMARY` instead of `settings.model`, and the only
> caller (`core/server/ipc_server/poller.py:305`) calls it with no
> arguments. Now defaults to `settings.model or ANTHROPIC_PRIMARY` so
> what the user sees in the footer matches the live user-selected
> model. E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop" --dry-run` unchanged at
> A (68.4); full pytest 4344 passed.

### Fixed
- **`core/ui/agentic_ui/_state.py:init_session_meter` ‚Äî default to live `settings.model`.** When the optional `model` argument is empty, fall back to `settings.model` (the value `_apply_model` mutates on `/model` switches) before falling back to `ANTHROPIC_PRIMARY` as a final safety net. Pairs with v0.82.0's `SharedServices` fix: that change made the *actual* LLM call route to the live model, this change makes the *displayed* model in the per-turn footer match. The single caller (`core/server/ipc_server/poller.py:305`) already passes no argument, so this fix lights up automatically ‚Äî no caller changes needed. (`core/ui/agentic_ui/_state.py`)

## [0.82.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **Critical fix ‚Äî `SharedServices` no longer freezes the active LLM
> model at daemon boot.** Discovered while live-testing OAuth-OpenAI
> codex routing. Symptom (extremely subtle, silently swaps providers):
> after a long-running daemon was started under `GEODE_MODEL=claude-opus-4-7`,
> a subsequent user `/model gpt-5.5` correctly mutated `settings.model`
> + `.env` and the prompt header rendered `gpt-5.5 ¬∑ autonomous
> execution agent`, **but every actual LLM call still routed to
> `claude-opus-4-7`** ‚Äî `serve.log` confirmed `Session started:
> model=claude-opus-4-7` for sessions opened after the switch. The
> turn footer printed `claude-opus-4-7` (correctly reflecting the
> real model), and `/model gpt-5.5` reported `Already using GPT-5.5`
> from the daemon-side handler, both contradicting the prompt header.
> Net effect: a user expecting OAuth-borrowed Codex Plus (free, hosted
> at `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex`) silently paid Anthropic API for
> Opus 4.7 calls, with their prompts also flowing to Anthropic instead
> of OpenAI/ChatGPT. Root cause: `SharedServices` cached `_model` and
> `_provider` as dataclass fields populated once in
> `build_shared_services()` from boot-time `settings.model`. Each new
> `create_session()` passed `self._model` to the freshly built
> `AgenticLoop`, so the boot-time value won every time. The drift-sync
> path (`_sync_model_from_settings()`) only triggers when an active
> loop runs another round ‚Äî useless for new sessions in the same
> daemon. **Fix**: remove `_model` / `_provider` dataclass fields;
> `create_session()` now reads `settings.model` directly and resolves
> the provider per call. The 4 `SharedServices(...)` test fixtures
> drop those kwargs; `test_model_resolved` becomes
> `test_model_resolved_per_session` asserting `loop.model ==
> settings.model` after `create_session`. E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy
> Bebop" --dry-run` unchanged at A (68.4); full pytest 4344 passed.

### Fixed
- **`core/server/supervised/services.py` ‚Äî drop boot-frozen `_model` / `_provider` fields.** `SharedServices` previously held `_model: str = ""` and `_provider: str = "anthropic"` populated once at `build_shared_services()` from `settings.model`. `create_session()` passed those frozen values into every new `AgenticLoop`. After a `/model` switch, the daemon's `settings.model` changed but `self._model` was untouched, so the next session was built with the boot-time model ‚Äî including its provider ‚Äî even though the prompt header read the live `settings.model`. The drift-sync path doesn't run for fresh sessions, only for in-flight loops. The fix is a single change in `create_session()`: read `settings.model` and call `_resolve_provider(settings.model)` inline at the `AgenticLoop(...)` construction site, and delete the two dataclass fields plus the `_model=`, `_provider=` kwargs at `build_shared_services()`'s `SharedServices(...)` return. Tests updated: `tests/test_shared_services.py` drops `_model="claude-sonnet-4-6"` / `_provider="anthropic"` from both `services` fixtures (lines 53-60 and 167-175); `test_model_resolved` is rewritten as `test_model_resolved_per_session` to assert that a freshly built `loop.model` matches the live `settings.model` after `create_session(SessionMode.DAEMON)` ‚Äî the new invariant. (`core/server/supervised/services.py`, `tests/test_shared_services.py`)

## [0.81.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

> **Dependency cleanup A4 ‚Äî `core/cli/{session_checkpoint,transcript}.py` ‚Üí `core/runtime_state/`.**
> Fourth of 5 PRs. Two cross-layer state primitives ‚Äî `SessionCheckpoint`
> (239 LOC, atomic JSON store for resume/checkpoint) and `SessionTranscript`
> (314 LOC, conversation log + cleanup) ‚Äî get a new dedicated package
> `core/runtime_state/` because they're consumed by all three layers
> (cli, agent, server). Putting them in `core/cli/` was the original
> v0.40-era artifact that v0.52's pyproject comment had explicitly
> flagged: *"server/runtime_state/ ÎòêÎäî utils/ Î°ú Ïù¥Îèô ÏòàÏÝï"*. Today, 14
> caller sites span 5 different layers. New package
> `core/runtime_state/` (1 init + 2 modules). 6 `ignore_imports`
> entries removed (`core.agent.loop.{loop,_lifecycle}` √ó 2 +
> `core.server.ipc_server.poller` for `session_checkpoint`/`transcript`
> in both contracts). 28 ‚Üí 22 ignore_imports remaining (single biggest
> reduction in the cycle). E2E `geode analyze "Cowboy Bebop" --dry-run`
> unchanged at A (68.4); full pytest 4344 passed.

### Architecture
- **`core/cli/{session_checkpoint,transcript}.py` ‚Üí `core/runtime_state/{session_checkpoint,transcript}.py` (553 LOC total).** New package `core/runtime_state/` (`__init__.py` 11L docstring) houses cross-layer session state primitives. `session_checkpoint.py` (239 LOC) = `SessionState` + `SessionCheckpoint` atomic-write JSON store backing `/resume`. `transcript.py` (314 LOC) = `SessionTranscript` conversation logger + `cleanup_old_transcripts` retention helper. Caller updates: `core/server/ipc_server/poller.py:526`, `core/agent/loop/_lifecycle.py:30`, `core/agent/loop/loop.py:129,173`, `core/cli/commands/session.py:22,34`, `core/cli/cmd_lifecycle.py:612`, `tests/conftest.py:30,31`, `tests/test_session_checkpoint.py:7`, `tests/test_phase3_ipc.py:357,402`, `tests/test_session_manager.py:129`, `tests/test_session_resume.py:11,39,110`, `tests/test_session_transcript.py:10`, `tests/test_transcript.py:8` ‚Äî 14 sites across `core/`, `tests/`. `pyproject.toml` `ignore_imports` removed: `core.agent.loop.loop -> core.cli.session_checkpoint`, `core.agent.loop.loop -> core.cli.transcript`, `core.agent.loop._lifecycle -> core.cli.session_checkpoint` (Agent contract); same three + `core.server.ipc_server.poller -> core.cli.session_checkpoint` (Server contract). 28 ‚Üí 22 ignore_imports remaining ‚Äî biggest single drop in the cycle (-6 entries from one PR). (`core/runtime_state/__init__.py` *new*, `core/runtime_state/session_checkpoint.py` *new*, `core/runtime_state/transcript.py` *new*, `core/cli/session_checkpoint.py` *deleted*, `core/cli/transcript.py` *deleted*, 14 caller files, `pyproject.toml`)
